### Kris Kolo — 2018-11-10T22:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kriss-optimal-journal.22135/post-255415>

Going to add a bit more "color" here about my symptoms over time.

My first 47 years were -- in my mind -- super healthy feeling. 4 grandparents all lived into their 90's. I rarely if ever missed school or work due to "not feeling well." Felt very hale and hearty. Did a lot of power walking. Had chicken pox at age 3 and a light case of mono at 14 - but other than that was rarely sick.

Until 2009 when my pain started to set in, gently at first but worsening with every passing month.

Of course looking back there were clues before 2009. In 2002 I was rear ended super gently - no pain at first but went to a chiropractor a couple weeks later who with one "adjustment" put my neck INTO excruciating pain...which has never really gone away. I live with it...managing ergonomically. But a drive on a dirt road (man do they have some of those here in MX!!) can create days of extra neck distress. Now I sense my physical structure was already weakened at that point..so the neck never had strength to heal properly.

Also in 2004 I had a period with low energy around 6-7 pm...odd for me. Also HORRIBLE constipation. Went to MD and he suggested thyroid. Went on armour...that solved evening energy issue but constipation lingered (and was really bad) for another 8 years until menopause and fibro type pans set in (about the same time), when for some strange reason the constipation went away...can't think of anything I did to cause that improvement. Maybe hormone shifting?

Then in 2006 I got some strange red spots in 3 areas around my cheeks/ nose. I had always had clear, great skin - so this was odd. Not pimples. They would cycle and be painful/ scratchy for 2-3 months, go away for a month, then come back. This lasted a couple years - til 2008.

In early 2009 I decided to get a Mercola sun lamp for Vit D in January. I knew my D was low. I was feeling wiped out from an ugly 9 year divorce process from husband #1 which had involved multiple depositions and being dragged thru court several times and spending way too much $. That just ended in 2009. I used the sunlamp for about a minute or two for 3-4 days...and found to my dismay that it seemed to bring back those strange skin spots - but in a more painful way. So I stopped the sunlamp after less than 10-20 min of use.

I then noticed that whenever I went into the outdoor light that my face spots started aching and burning more. SOOOO....I started trying to stay OUT of the sun to keep them from going crazy. For about 2-3 years I lived like a vampire. Shifted my daytime power walks in the neighborhood til after dark. Darted from car to house and from office to car quickly as I could.

In 2012 a couple doctors told me maybe my issue was environmental - mold - although I had never "reacted" to any building. But I dutyfully tossed all worldly possessions and moved homes...twice...just in case. Lived in an airstream trailer for 8 months looking at my family from outside kitchen window. Nothing moved needle on advancing pain. We even built a "healthy house" out of inert materials/ metal roof...my husbands allergies disappeared but I just kept getting worse.

I was treated for a couple years for lyme that I don't believe I had in retrospect (MDs very mixed but we were grasping at straws to figure out my isssues!).

Anyway...I've done a million other therapies (neural retraining, hyperthermia treatment in Germany, camping in middle of no where for a month, diet treatments of all shapes / sizes...endless list.

BUt I meant for this post to talk about symptoms, so let me turn to that.

Although I can pull myself together and LOOK fairly normal - I'm not. Here's a partial list of what I feel/ experience:

\- SLEEP: at night I fall asleep fairly easily (now at around 9 am given I'm living in candlelight/ avoiding electronics at night since Aug '18). I sleep maybe 4-5 hours with 1-3 wakings...the wakings feel like they're mainly about pain mitigation. Whatever shoulder and hip I'm lying on are SCREAMING in pain within about an hour if I'm sleeping..if I'm awake I can only lie on one side 10 min before the pain forces me to roll to other side. At first in 2009 I only got up 1-2 x/ night and could sleep longer...but those windows of sleep have gotten shorter and shorter. Now I find I sleep from 9 or 9:30 to 2:30 or 3:30...then I wake and can't get back to sleep. It is excruciating because from 2:30 til I rise out of bed at 630 am or so...I feel not only the pain that has me tossing around dozens and dozens of times...but I also feel ...."poisonous" during those early morning dark hours. Not sure how else to describe it. Vaguely nauseous but mainly feel like the wrong thing is coursing thru my arteries and veins. This improves when I rise - daytime is full of pain but with movement and being upright (with cortisol?) I feel like that poisonous feeling can be kept at bay. The only time that poisonous feeling ever goes TOTALLY away is when I am sitting in direct strong UV light.

\- Sore throat since 2010
\- Immune markers low (CD-57, others)
\- No cold, flu or fever since 2004 (before that got 1 cold or flu a year, usually mild)
\-- osteoporosis
\-- poor gum/ tooth health (started when pain started in 2009 - before that had no cavities and perfect teeith)
\-- Painful neck - can't lie on back for more than a few minutes before neck and headache set in
\-- Painful soles of feet - feels like I am walking always on hard bed of marbles - very uncomfortable
\-- Shoulder/ hip pain - profound when lying on side in bed at night. Awake can only lie on 1 side for 10 min before pain from shoulder/ hip pressed against bed gets so bad I must get off that side and roll to other (asleep I can sometimes do up to 1 or 1.5 hours before waking and having to roll over
\-- Frequent urination (sometimes urgent)
\-- Painful joints - feel like knees and shoulders and elbows and toes are on fire most of the time
\-- Extremely painful toes - ache as if they are cut off from blood flow (increasingly with each year passing)
\-- Slow to heal - smallest cuts or bumps take a great while to heal
\-- Hair loss (body) pretty extreme - underarms, legs
\-- Some hair loss (head hair)
\-- Hands and fingers ache - hard to hold a pot or pan or even type without finger pain from pressure
\-- carpal tunnel - both wrists
\-- dry eyes,dry mouth (better after summer of sunning 2018)
\-- dry/ painful vaginal tissues (better after sunning the summer of 2018)
\-- constipation (managed via magnesium ascorbate each day)
\-- Buttocks pain - fierce burning that gets worse and worse - can't sit very long before having to get up, can't sit at all on hard or even plastic seat surfaces - rely on multiple gel cushions
\-- low thyroid
\-- extremely low testosterone, estrdiol, progesterone
\-- Cortisol reasonably normal by day but a little high in evening - stage 2 adrenal fatigue according to one doctor
\-- sleep - choppy. get woken every 60-90 min by extreme pain. Can fall asleep easily by 9 or 930 but often wake at 230 or 330 after 6 fitful hours and then can't get back to sleep so just lie in pain for remainder of hours til sunrise

There are other symptoms but these are some of the main ones.

### Novah — 2019-02-03T20:06:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kriss-optimal-journal.22135/post-262205>

> [Kris Kolo said:](/goto/post?id=251635)
>
> UPDATE Sept 24 2018:
>
> Have pulled together any labs I could find on attached spreadsheet - most of them on Kruse's list of labs he likes to see. Unfortunately my doctor's office just said I have to pay out of pocket for ones highlighted in red. Of which there are MANY. Which would you prioritize? Also - since I'll be in Playa del Carmen in Oct - has anyone gotten tests like these done in Mexico for much better out of pocket price? I've signed up for consult with Dr K in cancun at his Silver member consults there in mid Nov so would love to get my ducks in a row on testing front.
>
> Update:
>
> -   I've switched my winter's plans for Florida to Playa Del Carmen (why mess around????) Home is Boston MA (would love to meet/ find Reuben!!!). First trip for a week in mid Oct.
> -   I am suffering greatly now (pain, inflammation) that days are mostly cloudy or cool for past 2 weeks...no more pain relief from sun and i can feel my dry eyes/ dry mouth returning
> -   I started earthing PROPERLY about 1 week ago and it has been AMAZING in its impact at night. Nothing has ever helped my sleep, which is awful mainly because of pain (pain in hip/shoulder against bed woke me every hour or two..often wake in great pain about 6x/ night. Since starting grounding sheet...that is down to 2-3 x / night!!!!!!) First real improvement in sleep since all this set in about 9 years ago.
> -   MY 2018 LABS here: [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1AbLXahylxmbAJMWY99Wnmnb3pGXbKJxnZvn\_nqbU\_gE/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1AbLXahylxmbAJMWY99Wnmnb3pGXbKJxnZvn_nqbU_gE/edit?usp=sharing)

Hi

Here's what I see when I look at your test results:

1\. Ferritin looks low, especially the last test result at 11 - is that a typo?

My doc likes her ladies ferritin to be 80-90.

Low ferritin (iron deficiency) has a ton of symptoms - muscle pain, joint pain, hair loss, low thyroid, sleep problems, headaches, dizziness, and the list goes on.

Low ferritin/iron can lead to low thyroid function (and vice versa).

2\. Have you had your copper and zinc levels measured? Low copper can inhibit ferritin from rising.

3\. Your TIBC looks slightly on the high side.

[http://www.ndhealthfacts.org/wiki/Total\_Iron\_Binding\_Capacity\_(TIBC)](http://www.ndhealthfacts.org/wiki/Total_Iron_Binding_Capacity_\(TIBC\))

4\. If your venous oxygen test is low, could that imply that your red blood cells aren't carrying enough oxygen, and hence point again to an iron/ferritin insufficiency issue?

"Iron is an essential element for blood production. About 70 percent of your body's iron is found in the red blood cells of your blood called hemoglobin and in muscle cells called myoglobin. Hemoglobin is essential for transferring oxygen in your blood from the lungs to the tissues. Myoglobin, in muscle cells, accepts, stores, transports and releases oxygen."

5\. Creatinine looks low.

6\. Omega 6/3 ratio looks significantly below the normal range - I'm curious why; maybe look into a re-test for a more current result.

7\. I'd be curious to see what more Omega 3 specific testing shows - something that gives results like this test:

[http://rmalab.com/sites/default/fil...s/20131128\_FattyAcidProfile\_Sample\_Report.pdf](http://rmalab.com/sites/default/files/tests/sample_reports/20131128_FattyAcidProfile_Sample_Report.pdf)

[http://rmalab.com/sites/default/files/tests/instructions/20170830\_ci\_fap.pdf](http://rmalab.com/sites/default/files/tests/instructions/20170830_ci_fap.pdf)

8\. Homocysteine looks high - High homocysteine can indicate low B12, folate, B6... What are your B12 and folate levels like?

9\. Re: sleep & pain -- What's your RBC (not serum) magnesium level?

10\. What's your blood pressure tend to be like?

So overall:

\- low ferritin -- potential iron deficiency
\- high TIBC -- potential iron deficiency
\- Venous oxygen test low - potential iron deficiency
\- low creatinine
\- high homocysteine - potential B12,folate, b6 deficiency

Curious about:

\- Another Omega 6/3 test
\- Further Omega 3 testing
\- Copper/zinc test
\- RBC (not serum) magnesium test
\- Blood pressure

Just my 2 cents

P. S. Oh and a Vitamin D re-test to see what that's up to, now that you're getting it from natural sun and not supplements.

### Kris Kolo — 2019-02-21T20:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kriss-optimal-journal.22135/post-263725>

Update on my time in Mexico on the Yukatan, living in Puerto Aventuras. It is month 5 and I expect to be here til late April - total of 7 months all together. Although I had to go home for 6 weeks from Thxgiving til New Years to the suburbs of Boston - so not a perfect experiment.

I found I felt much worse when home over the christmas holidays...not at first but within a week. I had huge cravings for carbs - not anything I had ever really noticed before...I don't eat many carbs and haven't since 2004 - just small amounts of fruit when warranted, and sweet potato once in a while for most part.

WHen i got back to mexico in early January I felt awful for a week - that seems to happen each time I return here...Jack suggested maybe my cells are "adjusting" to different magnetic fields down here?

And JUST when that "adjustment" ended...I got whacked with my first ever case of food poisoning...e-coli to be specific (a doctor down here in PDC ran tests). I had eaten a batch of ceviche from PDC with a friend. I got sick about 24 hr later and she also got sick a little after me. In my case I had horrible runs for 3-4 weeks...i LIVED in the bathroom. Usually my stomach is very tough - rarely have reactions (bad ones) to anything I eat and I've never had food poisoning before in my 57 years, to the best of my memory.

So now I am 3 weeks past that food poisoning. Doing LOTS of sun, happily. SOmetimes on beach, sometime in yard behind my small building, sometimes just by walking my dog around the area or to the marina. I also go on my roof deck as it is private and i can do "native" style sunbathing LOL.

I will say that I'm not feeling any better overall. WHILE i'm in direct strong sun i still get good pain relief (maybe even 70% pain relief) but the minute there is no sun (sunset or I go inside)...I'm back to worse than ever in terms of throbbing joints, fragile painful ligaments/ tendons, inability to withstand any pressure agains my body/ skin. Even sunglasses hurt on my nose - and pressing the button to release/ tighten my dog's collar hurts as does anything else I do with my hands now. That wasn't the case 6-9 months ago...hands and other areas have gotten worse.

But I'm determined to keep at this. I've ONLY been doing sunrises and sunsets and avoiding blue light since early August last year...I'm only 6 months into it and I'm patient...Jack said at my early Nov consult in cancun that because of my age (57) and the time I've had this pain (almost 10 years)...that it could take 2-3 years to turnaround.

And I am VERY determined to make this work.

Overall I'm loving the sun of the Yukatan...it is phenomenal and helps me a lot with pain mitigation in the daytime!! (I've also loved the ocean and being in the water here in Puerto aventuras...although not in the last week because the high winds of recent weeks have caused mountains of sargasso seaweed to come in....hard to even climb the mountain to get into the ocean LOL.

But I do fear that this area and many others along this coast are too high tech. That EMF is too high. You can ALWAYS see cell towers and the big power lines are never far away. Readings for RF and mag/ electric are good inside the buildings here because their walls are so wonderfully solid...but I fear the dirty electricity along much of this coast is high in any kind of populated place I would feel safe. I just wouldn't feel "safe" in the jungle away from these populated areas like this town of 5000 (Puerto Aventuras). PDC didn't feel safe for me living here as a single woman either.

I know the great mag fields from crater down here should help mitigate the 3G and 4G stuff that is all around any place I feel safe....but every "body" is different. Maybe the sun isn't doing me enough good because I'm still dehydrated.

In fact I know I am. My BUN/ Creatinine ratio bounced between 12 and 19 last year and the previous year while I was living in my boston suburb...but was a shocking 35 in early January here in Puerto aventuras...admittedly I had been back in Boston suburb for 6 weeks over christmas - it is possible things there changed since I left living there full time in Oct? I know my food poisoning and related dehydration started AFTER the blood was drawn for the tests I'll attach to my journal here in a moment.

I hope now to stay here just til early May, then assuming I'm still not feeling better - will go explore the lower Pop density areas around Apalachicola and Eastpoint and Carrabelle FL on panhandle in May...with eye to trying THAT area in Sept - Dec of 2019

The good nows from my 6 weeks back in boston in Dec was that my husband and I decided to sell our boston area home and we are buying a place for us to spend summers in Vermont...in a town with population under 1000. Queechee. Hoping that can be a safer place for the few warm months of the year for me than boston...sure it will be in fact, if I set that house up right. Will get an EMF expert in and planning on sleeping in a small shed I'll put up next to house...and will live outdoors 80% of each day - like I'm now doing here in Mexico. Just inside to cook a bit, and on rainy or stormy days.

Onwards and upwards!! I AM going to conquor this thing with the help of Jack and my own learning/ persistence.

Kris

### Kris Kolo — 2019-05-21T01:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kriss-optimal-journal.22135/post-270956>

After spending much of the time from late Oct 2018 til early April 2019 in PDC and Puerto Aventuras in Yukatan, my BUN/ Creatinine got worse....is up around 31 (recently and in late Jan). Late last summer and in Oct it had been in the 12-19 range. I didn't feel at all better in Mexico - I felt much worse, especially when I arrived for a couple weeks. My meters for RF and magnetic and electric read very well - but I got a dirty electricity meter in late Dec and realized that DE levels were very high - in multiple places I tested in PDC and also Puerto Aventuras. I'm sure there are more "rural" and natural places in Mexico but as a single woman, I'm not sure I'd feel comfortable living in a rural or jungley place there after getting feel of place over 6 months and also meeting several people who had problems with safety.

So while I felt pain go down WHILE I was in direct sun in Mexico - I generally felt worse when NOT in direct sun (evenings and nights were extremely pain-filled and new parts of me started to hurt a lot that hadn't been issues before over the 10 years of gradual, worsening pain. I tried hard to find a place I could sleep grounded outside but there were so many "blue lights" ...and I just wasn't going to feel safe alone on the beach sleeping.

So I came back to suburban Boston in mid April and stayed just long enough to pack up my house and start moving process to a rural Vermont town of 1000 peeps 2 hours north of Massachusetts . Now I am hanging out in Apalachicola on Gulf coast for solar benefits...eating tons of their wonderful, inexpensive oysters and drinking in the pain relief from hot sun here. Pain levels at night not impacted though...continue to be awful if not in direct sun. And I've been eaten alive by the yellow flies who don't seem to respond to any bug sprays and who are supposed to be extra numerous this year (they are only around here in May).

In early June I return to rural Vermont where I'll get some time with family who can come up from Boston area to visit - it's been a long year away from them. I plan to stay in Vermont - sleeping in an outdoor shed with a concrete floor which I'll put copper filings in if I can find them...and I'll sleep on mattress low to ground and try to "ground" - at least I'll be out of the house and the house's power grid. We ve already done some mitigation work (got rid of dimmers and any LED/ fluorescents etc.) and we have tossed router than comcast delivered in favor of a weak one that we can do "wired only" connections with....when I am there in residence there will be NO WIFI. And in the unusual case where I have to sleep inside house all breakers will be off. I play to live outdoors about 23 hours a day...even cooking outside and doing my 2-4 hours of work a day outdoors with a landline and cabled shilded connection to internet.

THen in late Sept I am planning on going to St. Croix to check that out as a potential place to spend winter. I know I will have to buy and drink imported bottled water (expensive) but it is at the 17th latitude, it is more rural and low pop density vs. places like St Thomas or much of FL, and they speak english - a big advantage for me over mexico where I struggled, unsuccessfully, to pick up some spanish.

My sleep is maybe a tiny bit better in the past couple months - the pain in hip and shoulder wake me maybe every two hours and it is excruiciating to roll over and rearrange the 6 pillows that prop me up...but I always go right back to sleep and maybe even get one 3 hour segment of sleep in a night. I go to bed around 9 pm once dark (never use anything but a black light flashlight or candles in evening now)....and sleep til about 6, waking about 3-5 times. There have been times in past I've NOT been able to get back to sleep starting at 2 pm...thankfully that has gotten better, even as the pain keeps getting worse.

CT for me is face dunking and 84 degree baths - because anything more than that creates extra pain so bad that my sleep goes to hell. I'm looking forward to being in a place with a warm ocean again so I can spend 1-2 hours a day in the ocean water - like I did in Mexico until the sargasso got so bad that it was hard to go in without skin reactions.

A **high BUN** value can mean kidney injury or disease is present. Kidney damage can be caused by diabetes or **high** blood pressure that directly affects the kidneys. **High BUN** levels can also be caused by low blood flow to the kidneys caused by dehydration or heart failure.

Phosphatidylcholine (from BodyBio )
and or zinc may be helpfull.

# Thread: Fukushima
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima.22139/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Laurie — 2018-09-25T05:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima.22139/post-251648>

Hello Kruse Tribe,

I am new to the community but my friend said that Jack says that Fukushima is not an issue when eating sea food. I was wondering Jack or anybody could tell me what the science is behind this and why eating fish that is so radiated and toxic is not an issue.

Thanks so much!

Laurie

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-26T17:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima.22139/post-251718>

PLENTY:

The topic of radiation in seafood is overrated and misunderstood (few know about hormesis and transcription factors like PARP1 and Ku70). Sharing this with people who say, "I'm not sure about eating seafood due to Fukushima. Radiation has been condemned by the entire scientific community as being a “dangerous bar”. This is surprising, however, since there is strong scientific evidence for the molecular mechanisms of how transient “radiation drinks” could promote health. I tell my patient's seafood radiation has many surprising benefits. Today we know that low doses of radiation stimulate DNA repair through the activation of four transcription factors, PARP-1, PARP2, ATM, and Ku70. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds). PARP-1 and PARP-2 activate two DNA repair pathways called “Single strand break repair” (SSBR) and one called “Base Excision Repair” (BER). Both of these repair single-stranded DNA breaks. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds), Ku-70 activates the DNA repair pathway called “Non-homologous end joining” (NHEJ). [https://scholar.google.com/scholar?...+parp1+parp2&btnG=&hl=en&as\_sdt=0,22&as\_vis=1](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=radiation+DNA+repair+parp1+parp2&btnG=&hl=en&as_sdt=0%2C22&as_vis=1)

Science evolves even if your thinking remains stagnant. We must expand our grip of science…………or risk falling from the cliff. I tell this to my patients and all the alternative functional medicine doctors who keep telling their patients seafood is bad. It is really bad advice based on outdated thinking.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-26T17:20:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima.22139/post-251719>

Just do a google search and you'll be busy for MONTHS.

### Laurie — 2018-09-26T20:03:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima.22139/post-251738>

Thanks Jack. That was super helpful. This is what I wanted to understand.

Based on what you explained, it seems that nature and our biology uses what is in our environment to continue to evolve such as the case with low dose radiation due to Fukushima which you explained.

I wish the same could be true for 5g. But that does not seem to be the case. There does not seem to be biological evolution with 5g....only sickness and biological disaster.

### Annie Dru — 2018-09-26T01:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-ancestral-cuisine-educator-turned-pupil-of-light.22142/post-251684>

Hi all,

I'm delighted to be here, and beyond grateful to have happened upon this community. I've spent the last decade immersed in the work of Dr. Weston A. Price; as a field educator and board member for both the Price Pottenger Nutrition Foundation, the Hunt Gather Grow Foundation, and WAPF Wise Traditions Conference presenter. Understanding the concept of solar intelligence as it pertains to the "sacred foods" of our ancestors was a game-changer for me... until I unwittingly immersed myself in the toxic light of an "alien blue sun". A perfect storm of events (TBI, menopause, family crisis & 4G) conspired to bring me to my knees health-wise, culminating in a constellation of bizarre symptoms no amount of "ancestral cuisine" could remedy. Just when I was at the brink of throwing away God's greatest gift, I discovered Dr. Kruse's work and immediately "saw the light". It is my hope to absorb this information adequate to share it with my students & greater "real food" community. Thank you for allowing me an opportunity to learn.

> [stiffyoungman said:](/goto/post?id=254566)

> How does one account for being sunburnt when the goal is to get as much sunlight as possible while there?

Its optimal to build a solar callus in the spring by going out every morning getting as much skin exposed. continue all summer and you eill be amazed at your tolerance. IR light in the am preps skin for stronger UV later on. If your tolerance is low than pace yourself make sure you get as much light when the sun is not as strong in the am and closer to sunset. utilise shade at mid day when it gets to much for you.

# Thread: Hi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rebecca Pascal — 2018-09-26T07:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/post-251694>

Hi - that's me on the right in my profile pic. We played outside all day, every day when we were kids - no sunscreen.  Yes, I received the same haircut as my 3 brothers.  Anyway, I'm now almost 50 and smack dab in perimenopause/menopause. My adrenals are shot, and my progesterone, estrogen, testosterone, and DHEA are all low. I'm also hypothyroid. I have also had allergies since I was a child. I was always skinny, and in the past 3-4 years, I have begun to gain weight despite the fact that I do kickboxing 3x a week, and tough, hilly bike rides. I know wifi is killing me. I have been telling ppl this for years and most look at me like I'm mental. I always hold the cell phone away from head and use the speaker bc holding it up to my head makes me sick. I've learned in the past couple of years that it's imperative to ground myself to Mother Earth, but I'm not spending enough time outside. I LOVE to learn. I already own several of the books Dr. Kruse suggested to read (though, admittedly, haven't read them all.) I watched about 10 hours of Dr. Kruse's Youtube vids, and decided to look up his website. I really like his spirit. I really admire his tenacity and his wisdom. I've been threatening for years to start a "Super Group" of smart ppl from all branches of science and math to discuss various topics, mostly to explore Metaphysical concepts, which fascinate me. I wish I had the capacity to learn (IQ) these complex concepts without rereading the materials 5 times, but that won't dissuade me. I will just have to shift the 40+ hours a week I devote to metaphysical reading/watch vids to reading Dr. Kruse's website, and possibly cracking open that Biology textbook I kept from college. I haven't trusted my health to the traditional healthcare system for years bc I know that all the research is being funded by Big Pharma. I do my own homework, which is how I stumbled upon a video with Dr. Kruse while researching the harmful effects of fluoride. Bottom line is that I became a member so that I could learn how to restore my health. I hope that I possess the intellectual capacity to grasp the material. I wish you all love and light on your healing journey. Thanks so much for the work you are doing, Dr. Kruse.

### caroline — 2018-09-26T07:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.22145/post-251697>

Hi Rebecca and welcome....

First of all - Dr. K. will need a current pic of you ....He needs to know who he is talking too.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. your health history as well as your Mom's and Gran's.

Where do you live? work? your postcode matters.

How is your sleep? libido? What do you eat? drink?

Do you have Jack's book? it is the first couple of years of his blog.

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to all Jack's webinars plus the monthly live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything

Thanks!!

HOT TAKE: The purpose of life is a life of purpose. My purpose is a passion for showing the soft white underbelly of technology risk for your biology. “It’s not ironic when you see someone who has a cancer of the brain…have…the marks of right where they used their cell phone,” DeBaun said.
If you're not ready to hang up the devices. He says keeping a safe distance can eliminate any harm. Even if that means taking the extra steps to charge your phone away from where you sleep.

I tested different kind of "nnemf" shields for a smartphone using i RF meter. They are all crap.
Somebody should make a app instead: When lockscreen is on, airplane mode also goes on. When you un-lock the sceen, call/sms antenna goes back on.
if somebody needs to reach you they can send you a sms and you can call back when you disable airplane mode with unlock screen.

I wonder if pepole know that having mobiledata/4g antennas active (wifi, bluetooth and NFC active even more?), is almost like having a continuous call in your pocket/in front of your face.

### Solkysset — 2018-11-26T00:42:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-is-the-new-second-hand-smoke-for-the-public.22149/post-256580>

There's an app in F-Droids app store called SleepyWiFi that'll just automatically disable WiFi when the phone is locked. I haven't seen anything to do airplane mode yet.

# Thread: New Platinum (meant to sign up for gold) member from Atlanta
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-platinum-meant-to-sign-up-for-gold-member-from-atlanta.22152/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scott Hardin — 2018-09-27T01:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-platinum-meant-to-sign-up-for-gold-member-from-atlanta.22152/post-251774>

Hey y'all,

This is Scott,

I'm 42 years old and live in Atlanta. I've been on the site for 2 weeks but I've been following some of Jack's recommendations for a little longer. I have a 40 day streak of watching the sunrise on the parking deck of my building.

For most of my adult life I've struggled with 1) frequent respiratory infections 2) Inconsistent energy levels 3) ADHD and 4) episodic depression.

Since getting up for the sunrise every morning my focus, energy and mood have been significantly better, but I did have to fight off a sinus infection recently (first one I have done without antibiotics) but it took 2 weeks!

Mitochondrial Haplotype: K1a

Potentially relevant SNPs
[rs1800497](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497\(T;T\)) - Dopamine receptor related - associated with ADHD

Wet and Dry Age related macular degeneration
[rs11200638](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638\(A;A\))
[rs10490924](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924\(T;T\))

homozygous for C677T
[rs1801133](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133\(T;T\))

Warrior
[rs4680](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680)[(G;G)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680\(G;G\))

BSML Vitamin D recepter polymorphism
[rs1544410](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410\(A;A\))

[rs3135391](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391)[(C;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391\(C;T\))
HLA-DRB1\*1501 carrier; higher multiple sclerosis risk

I'm not quite sure where to start the Journal - I would appreciate any feedback from the group and also from Jack!

### caroline — 2018-09-27T04:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-platinum-meant-to-sign-up-for-gold-member-from-atlanta.22152/post-251787>

Hi Scott .....if you click on forum and scroll down 5 - you will see the optimal journal section.

Give Dr. K. your health history and also your Mom's and Gran's.

welcome!

# Thread: CT vs Atrial Fibrillation. A nature's hint?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vs-atrial-fibrillation-a-natures-hint.22153/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JakZ — 2018-09-27T17:59:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vs-atrial-fibrillation-a-natures-hint.22153/post-251804>

At the end of the last year I had my first tries with cold exposure - it was one or two times in a small pool near the house. Unfortunately, I've been quite busy, didn't take this excercise seriously and stopped doing it as fast as I started.
Few weeks later I got my first episode of atrial fibrillation - I woke up with a racing heartbeat and a pain in my chest. Before I went to the hospital, the one thing I can remember is that I had a very strong desire to jump into that cold pool.
The question is: would cold water immersion help stabilizing the heartbeat or was it a bad clue from my body? Does anyone else have similar experience?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-02T19:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vs-atrial-fibrillation-a-natures-hint.22153/post-252177>

Fully answered here: [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...otif\_id=1538491234905386&notif\_t=feed\_comment](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2338793329518341?comment_id=2338941432836864&reply_comment_id=2340389366025404&notif_id=1538491234905386&notif_t=feed_comment)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-02T19:51:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vs-atrial-fibrillation-a-natures-hint.22153/post-252183>

My October 2018 webinar being released today is all tied to this link here.....Why are arteries weak in some people versus others? Note she lived in Ireland. That is a big clue to the webinar......[https://www.livescience.com/63720-beach-wave-ruptures-womans-artery.html](https://www.livescience.com/63720-beach-wave-ruptures-womans-artery.html)

# Thread: Bloodwork
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloodwork.22156/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2018-09-27T20:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloodwork.22156/post-251830>

So I'm not a huge fan of bloodwork because I used to obsess way too much over the numbers, but after several years of not knowing my "status" I got my doc to order some tests. Would appreciate some feedback!

Some background in case I need to do more:
\-no mitochondrial diseases or symptoms of anything currently (since moving out of city)
\-reversed hypogonadism (just by becoming cold adapted in Canada)
\-reversed leaky gut via morning sun, seasonal eating/epi-paleo, fasting, etc.
\-reversed adrenal fatigue
\-reversed seasonal affective disorder -> CT is my miracle
\-living out of the city, low population density, low EMF (that I have measured) but working in Ottawa 3-5 days per week
\-drinking natural, well-water that has been tested, free of fluoride
\-exercise 3x per week with weights in my basement with windows open (sun coming in) and blacklight + 660 nm RED LED
\-diet: cutting carbs like a bad habit as I'm at 45th latitude and summer is over

WBC: 3.7 x10^9/L (3.5-10.5)
RBC: 5.07 x10^12/L (4.00-5.50)
HGB: 151 g/L (125-170)
HCT: 0.424 L/L (0.380-0.500)
MCV: 83.6 fL (80.0-100.0)
MCH: 29.8 pg (27.0-34.0)
MCHC: 356 g/L (315-355)
RDW: 12.2 % (11.5-15.5)
Plat: 169 x10^9/L (130-380)
MPV: 9.2 fL (9.0-14.0)
Neutrophil: 2.2 x10^9/L (2.0-7.5)
Immature Granulocyte: 0.0 x10^9/L (<=0.1)
Lymphocyte: 1.1 x10^9/L (0.8-3.5)
Monocyte: 0.3 x10^9/L (0.1-1.0)
Eosinophil: 0.1 x10^9/L (0.0-0.5)
Basophil: 0.0 x10^9/L (0.0-0.1)
Na: 136 mmol/L (136-145)
K: 3.6 mmol/L (3.5-5.1)
Cl: 100 mmol/L (98-107)
CO2: 27 mmol/L (21-32)
Glucose: 4.5 mmol/L (4.0-11.0)
HbA1C: 4.9 % (4.8-6.0)
Urea: 5.0 mmol/L (2.1-8.0)
Creatinine: 86 umol/L (49-93)
eGFR (CKD-EPI): (>90 mL/min/1.73 m^2 >=90
Ca: 2.45 mmol/L (2.12-2.52)
Mg: 0.86 mmol/L (0.74-1.03)
AST: 19 U/L (15-37)
ALT: 17 U/L (17-63)
ALP: 59 U/L (50-136)
GGT: 11 U/L (15-85) LOW
Iron: 14 umol/L (12-31)
Ferritin: 184 ug/L (24-336)
Vitamin B12: 372 pmol/L (133-675)
CK: 87 U/L (30-250)

Cholesterol: 6.6 mmol/L (3.5-5.2) HIGH
HDL Cholesterol: 2.02 mmol/L (>=1.00)
Non-HDL Cholesterol: 4.6 mmol/L (<=4.5) HIGH
LDL Cholesterol: 4.2 mmol/L
Triglycerides: 0.77 mmol/L (<=2.00)

CRP: 2.0 mg/L (<=10.0)

LH: 0.8 IU/L (1.0-9.0) LOW
FSH: 3.2 IU/L (1.0-19.0)
Estradiol: < 37 pmol/L (40-160) LOW
Testosterone Total: 7.4 nmol/L (6.1-27.1)
Free Testosterone: 118 pmol/L (110-660)
Bioavailable Testosterone: 2.8 nmol/L (2.8-15.5)
SHBG: 44 nmol/L (13-89)
Cortisol: 397 nmol/L (185-624)
DHEA-S: 9.8 umol/L (2.3-18.7)
Progesterone: 2.1 nmol/L (0.5-6.6)
TSH: 1.92 mIU/L (0.34-5.60)
FT4: 11.5 pmol/L (7.0-17.0)
FT3: 4.6 pmol/L (3.3-6.0)

My thoughts? Why is my CRP this high? Makes no sense, but then I thought I didn't ask for HS-CRP. Is this test not very accurate? My last one (when I was NOT-SO-HEALTHY came back at <0.2mg/dl) so I'm chalking it up to lab error.

Can someone help with my BUN:Creatinine ratio? Is it Urea multiplied by Creatinine (i.e. 5.0x0.86 = 4.3:1) or did I royally screw that up? Also thought my sodium and potassium seemed a little low, but I guess not bad considering Cl and CO2 levels. I eat a ton of celtic sea salt and recently added to my drinking water, but normally my Na is 140 and K 4.5.

FWIW this test was taken at 8:50 AM, and I was awake since about 6:00 AM this morning doing CT in my pool under the stars and moonlight LOL

### Sun Disciple — 2018-09-27T23:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloodwork.22156/post-251839>

"Can someone help with my BUN:Creatinine ratio? Is it Urea multiplied by Creatinine (i.e. 5.0x0.86 = 4.3:1) or did I royally screw that up? Also thought my sodium and potassium seemed a little low, but I guess not bad considering Cl and CO2 levels."

### axel brandt — 2018-09-28T05:32:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloodwork.22156/post-251863>

you still have hypogonadism (with normal FSH), that means there is a primary testicular failure. If you are lifting heavy weights 3 times a week, have normal cholesterol levels, and not drinking alcohol in excess,it means that you have an ADAM (androgen deficiency in the aging male). That´s very common, it´s not an illness, is part of the aging process. Sooner or later, every woman will enter menopause,with an abrupt decline on estradiol levels. We too have a decline on our sexual hormones levels, the difference is that the decline is not so abrupt, and that some men have a mild decline maintaining normal levels their whole life.
Try to get rid of excess visceral fat,(belly fat) because there, we have an enzime called aromatase, that converts testosterone to estradiol.

### Antonis — 2018-09-28T13:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloodwork.22156/post-251873>

BUN/creatine ratio looks really good

For sure, it's been interesting how different I appear to be than average.

But I think CT 4 and CT 6 blog really clears it up, especially now that we know Trytophan is a seasonal time crystal. It can act be ketogenic or glucogenic depending upon the light that excites it. With the amount of CT I do, and lack strong UV light at my latitude, it is likely more ketogenic and hence why I get the results I get.

It appears in winter melatonin and prolactin are higher while cortisol and dopamine are lower. I believe prolactin inhibits dopamine, and dopamine is the controller of the pituitary & compound pharmacy, so this could explain why my hormones are lower. I'm likely living more of my life in the winter pathway.

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) not sure you recall, but the best hack (or I should say "reverse-hack") I've ever done was to follow Ray Peat's approach. Here is what I did:

\-Stopped keto & fasting late winter
\-Ate tons of carbs (orange juice, milk, potatoes, rice, honey, etc)
\-Stopped wearing blue blockers
\-Stopped all CT, and started wearing warmer clothing
\-Drank less water

The results? My T levels tripled. That gave me the answer I needed that what I was doing and my levels didn't matter because of the way I lived in the cold adapted pathway. It just proved that the blue light and being "warm-adapted" was firing my PVN as the Vitamin A/Melanopsin bond was broken easier and my pituitary started firing up to offset this.

That whole experiment made me change my beliefs about lab results.

# Thread: My Optimal Journal - Scott Platinum Member
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-scott-platinum-member.22162/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18

### Scott Hardin — 2018-09-29T18:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-scott-platinum-member.22162/post-251957>

Hey I'm Scott

I'm 42 years old and live in Atlanta. I've been on the site for 2 weeks but I've been following some of Jack's recommendations for a little longer. I have a 40 day streak of watching the sunrise on the parking deck of my building.

For most of my adult life I've struggled with

**Health challenges**
1) frequent respiratory infections 2) Inconsistent energy levels 3) ADHD and 4) episodic depression.

**Positive results so far**
Since getting up for the sunrise every morning my focus, energy and mood have been significantly better, but I did have to fight off a sinus infection recently (first one I have done without antibiotics) but it took 2 weeks!

**Relevant DNA SNPs**

Mitochondrial Haplotype: K1a

Potentially relevant SNPs
[rs1800497](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1800497\(T;T\)) - Dopamine receptor related - associated with ADHD

Wet and Dry Age related macular degeneration
[rs11200638](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs11200638\(A;A\))
[rs10490924](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs10490924\(T;T\))

homozygous for C677T
[rs1801133](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133)[(T;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1801133\(T;T\))

Warrior
[rs4680](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680)[(G;G)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs4680\(G;G\))

BSML Vitamin D recepter polymorphism
[rs1544410](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410)[(A;A)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs1544410\(A;A\))

[rs3135391](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391)[(C;T)](https://www.snpedia.com/index.php/rs3135391\(C;T\))
HLA-DRB1\*1501 carrier; higher multiple sclerosis risk

**Mom's health history:**
Young - health problems include sinus infections and depression
Post 60 years old - Degenerative disc issues in neck and lower back.
Appears 10 years younger than her age but has VERY low energy

**Grandmother's health history
Lived to 92 years old** - was active until the last 6 months. Died of **lung cancer**. Lived with a smoker until he died when they were both about 60 years old.

Had some kind of uterine cancer around 30 years old - no recurrences or major health problems until her 90s.

Always appeared about 15 years younger than her age - had very high energy up until the last 6 months of her life.

### Scott Hardin — 2018-09-29T18:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-scott-platinum-member.22162/post-251960>

Other health events -
2 years old - severe trauma with lawnmower - have a partial but functional right foot
10 years old - tonsillectomy
15 years old - suddenly myopic - (coincided with me quitting outdoor sports)
21 first diagnosed with depression - has recurred multiple times in my adult life (watching the sunrise seems to work better than mediation for me)
26 years old - multiple angiolipoma removal - still have many but they are very slow growing

### Scott Hardin — 2018-10-01T15:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-scott-platinum-member.22162/post-252056>

30339 population dense. In my condo I get relatively low emf when wifi is off and i’m away from the windows (.001 to .01 micro watts/cm2)

My office is near (200 meters )a huge cell tower and the radiation is consistently higher there (.1 watts/cm2). When my lease is up I will relocate.

I am considering moving to Colombia. I have visited many times and usually feel great there. The best I’ve ever felt is when I was staying in the Amazon but I cannot relocate there because i cannot rum my business from there. Medellin is more likely.

Diet is not dialed in but i don’t eat after 8 pm

I am drinking San Peligrino and Great Value un fuoridated drinking water.

Ive listened to the 2 vermont talks which are on youtube and also many others including some by doug wallace.

I have read jacks epipaleo book

I spend 30 minutes barefoot on the parking deck of my building every day at sunrise with my shirt off and wearing tan thru shorts.

I spend 2 hours in the morning on the patio of my unit.

About 4 days a week I spend 30 or more minutes tanning at the pool tracking my time with D-Minder. I have fitzpatrick type 2 skin BUT i do not burn.

I had lasic years ago on my left eye but not my right. I still “need” lenses in both because the surgery didn’t go well but I’ve started only wearing a lens in one eye (until i can get glasses) and I don’t put them on until I have to drive somewhere which is rarely before 11am.

I wear a sleep mask but I have ordered blackout curtains as there are very bright white street lights here.

I wear blue blocking glasses after sundown.

My time outdoors has eliminated my need for Ritalin in order to focus and I recently beat a sinus infection without antibiotics for the first time in 20 years. I still have some post nasal drip ( as I have my whole life) but it seems to be getting better each day.

My libido is probably as high as it was in my mid 20s (it had been very low for a wile)

My mood has been largely great since i started the new habits BUT it tends to suck on days where the sunrise is covered in thick clouds.

Current concerns - completely healing my sinuses for good. dropping body fat from 22 to 15%. I want to stop having to get up to pee in the middle of the night.

### Scott Hardin — 2018-10-02T01:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-scott-platinum-member.22162/post-252104>

The plan is to move to Colombia. I'm curious Jack's thoughts on my skin type near the equator.

I've changed all the overhead lights in my office to full spectrum halogen. If I change the lamps to incandescent do I still need to wear clothes that cover my whole body?

Also, I've started wearing my tan-through swim trunks as boxer shorts - that way when I have a moment (I work near a national forest and river) I can drop trou, take of my shirt and go for a hike.

**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AstralJam8 — 2018-09-30T08:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightshades-and-teeth.22165/post-251999>

Something I've noticed about Nightshades (especially the skins) is that they do something rather unusual to my teeth. It's hard to describe, but it feels like anytime I eat cooked/softened Nightshades (bell peppers, tomatoes, sometimes potato skins, etc.) my teeth feel like they've lost their substance and smooth texture, like they're made of rigid micro-sponge that could easily scratch or break. It's rather unsettling when it happens, feels like if I were to bite into anything hard in this condition it would demolish a few teeth. The effect seems most pronounced if the food was sauteed or fried with oil (I don't do this when cooking for myself but I often encounter this when eating out,) and it goes away after about an hour and has always puzzled me. Anyone have thoughts on this?

### JanSz — 2018-09-30T14:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightshades-and-teeth.22165/post-252000>

Get Spectracell Micronutrient analysis, possibly something shows up.

### drezy — 2018-09-30T16:54:33+0300

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=252000)
>
> Get Spectracell Micronutrient analysis, possibly something shows up.

I may be the nightshade astringents/tannins.

If you ever want to experience this to an extreme find a grocery store that sells persimmons. Find the least ripe one and bite in. All the saliva in your mouth will seem to disappear and all smoothness you are used to will be rough.

[https://theindianspot.com/natural-astringent-tomato/](https://theindianspot.com/natural-astringent-tomato/)

Docs live with unexamined beliefs that that little multiple-use vial of vaccine fluid that comes from Big Pharma drug manufacturers is pure and safe. They can't tell by looking at it, or have any certainty when using it on patients. That's way over their heads. So, unexamined beliefs and the actions that follow let them sleep at night, for now. And, different batches for different regions could have the same label name but different experimental additives tracked in the lot numbers; well-meaning missionaries carrying vaccines to African villages comes to mind.

A basic adult detox article is this one: [https://www.livingwhole.org/a-vaccine-detox-for-adults/](https://www.livingwhole.org/a-vaccine-detox-for-adults/) It's incredibly sad when mothers of hospital newborns have to do 'underground' detox measures to try to 'mitigate' the assault with a deadly needle on babies minutes after being born. Hospital staff, likewise, have no clue what's in those vials. Let's cheer for and stand behind the families who opt out of public schooling if attendance demands a horrific number of assaults on the next generation's developing immune system. My 'belief' that vaccinations will shorten lifespan is more thoughtfully considered than top-down orders to mindless managers who push such mandates on workers to get pay-checks and promotions.

I would rather wear a face mask than choose to be poisoned, if those are the options. It could also be an opportunity to look more deeply at your priorities. Confront the feeling of “shunning” and the buttons that pushes, and could open up some blockages there. And when someone asks you why the mask, it could be an opportunity to plant a seed of consciousness, in whatever way the situation calls for.
And fully accept whatever decision you make, and move forward from there.

### RebeccaCameron — 2021-08-25T17:35:49+0300

The flu season is here, and you need to make sure you are vaccinated. Fluzone is a relatively new antivirus that can spot germs before they have a chance to hurt you. It will help you save money on insurance premiums and potentially save your life. To get the best results from this virus, make sure you get vaccinated before and during covered visits to your doctor. Also, I observed that doctors are already using the soft from [iinsight.biz](https://www.iinsight.biz/). This allows them to see all previous records from their mobile phones. This app is making their work faster and more efficient.

# Thread: Jaime T. Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaime-t-optimal-journal.22171/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jaime T. — 2018-10-01T18:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaime-t-optimal-journal.22171/post-252079>

I live in a suburb of Charleston, SC- 32\*. Fair skinned and freckly, after many severe sunburns as a kid I had a sun phobia. I was a vegetarian for 25 years until 2 years ago. Due to these health issues I completely switched it up and added grass-fed meats and tons of seafood. Several health issue starting around age 36, 5 years ago. We are still trying to figure out exactly what the issues are but so far am diagnosed with **Hypothyroid** (4 years ago) and **Diabetes Insipidius** (6 months ago). I'm thinking it is pituitary in origin though my non-contrast MRI was unremarkable. I have mostly self tested as the 3 doctors I have seen only help so much. I have TONS of labs dating back several years....It's been a slow process. In following this all upstream, I learned a little about **Alpha MSH** and thought I bet I am low (the low ADH and light skin were my tipoffs) so got myself tested--- <8! Though I cant seem to find what optimal is. My major symptom is global muscle weakness and fatigue, weight gain and general fatigue and brain fog at times. I generally don't sleep well due to having to pee multiple times per night.
1 month ago I started getting early morning sun on my face (make it to the beach for the sunrise 2xweek).
I stopped being scared of the sun and started adding 25 minutes mid morning sun and 25 around noon.
Long walks on the beach on the weekends.
Grounding as much as possible.
More fats and even more seafood.
flux on computer.

I am slow to add things as I learn more but this is what I have done so far. GUESS WHAT? The last week I have only woke up a max of 2x per night to pee and two nights I didn't wake at all! For someone who was urinating 7 liters per day and up to ~5 times per night this is a big deal. While it's still too early to tell I am thinking the sun and perhaps increase in MSH is helping my DI!

A little more insight:
Currently taking:
100mcg Levothyroxine
40mcg Liiothyronine (will be raising again due to labs)
Prescribed .1 Desmopressin that I only took 5 times.

Haplogroup:
**HV0**

I am excited and nervous to learn more - though I am not sure where to go next and what my next steps should be. Thanks to all who share their stories and thoughts, It's so helpful. Any suggestions on what to integrate next?

### JanSz — 2018-10-10T15:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaime-t-optimal-journal.22171/post-252794>

Jaime T.
Charleston, SC- 32\*. Fair skinned and freckly, after many severe sunburns as a kid I had a sun phobia.
Hypothyroid
Diabetes Insipidius
the low ADH and light skin were my tipoffs) so got myself tested--- <8!

urinating 7 liters per day

40mcg Liiothyronine (will be raising again due to labs)
Prescribed .1 Desmopressin that I only took 5 times.---------------------------

CORTISOL, SERUM

6/4/18 1.1 (6.2-19.4) (AM DEXA Pill Dexamethasone
7/8/18 7.8 (6.2-19.4) (AM)
9/21/18 11.3 (4.0-22.0) (AM)
MSH
7/2/18 <8 (0-40)
\======================================================================
**I am not a doctor.**
Circadian Rhythm is a king.
We have been made by sunlight. No sunlight=no life

You need lots of sunlight. You have a lots of sunlight in your outdoors but not using it.
You have a fair skin.
I would use MT-2 injections. (Personally I am using them right now).
You can buy MT-2 and 31Ga needles and Bacteriostatic water over internet.
That will allow you to stay naked exposed to sunlight.
You will get dark skin.
That will get you vit D and many other
\-----------

pee multiple times per night
urinating 7 liters per day
and
you are **over-weighted**

**That is a left handed good news.** (It would be worst if you were skinny).
You have a Desmopressin script (good news)

That desmopressin may be too strong.
Lets try not using it but keep buying it so you have it.
I can tell you how I diluted mine so it is more gentle.

It would be a good idea if you could listen to Dr Boros and dr O video.
Discussed here and some other places.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...szlo-boros-last-week.22255/page-2#post-252762](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nearly-4-hour-podcast-with-laszlo-boros-last-week.22255/page-2#post-252762)

specially from time 49:20 till about 55:50
The take home is that you can
loose your body fat weight by about 1.5 pounds a day or more
if you (would not drink water)
and
in the process you will raise your (currently low) ADH

I am assuming that what doctor Boros is doing is an extreme case.
He drinks only 25cc=8.5 oz water a day and eats fat (bacon and olive oil) but coconut oil is also good.
**He drinks only when he feels thirst, but first he waits 20 minutes to confirm that he is actually thirsty.**
He mentions that he have high ADH. I think that is his natural state. He did not give more details about that.

You have low ADH.
**Thirst raises ADH,** so if you are thirsty at this moment, and do not drink, 20 minutes latter **that thirst may disappear if ADH raises sufficiently.**
If it would work naturally, you good.
If not, you may need to use (minute amounts) of desmopressin.
Right now, the minimum dose that you can take is too strong (hence I talk about diluting desmopressin).

I would say that at the first phase you should try to reduce your drinking from current 7liters a day to about 1-1.5Liters a day.

If you have a problem doing it, I will explain to you how I am diluting my desmopressin.

.............
\------------------
There is a guy in Canada who also drinks 7 liters a day, but he is skinny. Skinny like no fat at all.
I would welcome suggestions for that situation.

//////

### Vervaina — 2018-10-02T04:33:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferroportin-hepcidin-and-cancer-prognosis.22173/post-252112>

So many smart people on this website I bet someone has a great explanation here...

My cancer doctor gave me some studies today to review about the relationship to ferroportin and hepcidin and iron regulation. She is interested in this and thinks that cancer/iron connection is more about ferroportin and hepcidin and that actual ferratin levels are just a secondary to these two things.

One study of over 800 women revealed that decreased ferroportin expression is associated with a significant reduction in breast cancer survival (due to higher metastasis) - high ferroportin and low hepcidin are extremely favorable in these patients. Too much hepcidin degrades ferroportin through phosphorylation.

My doctor is interestedin this connection and in how one would bring ferroportin up and lower hepcidin.

I believe that **Allitridin** can increase ferroportin and because diallyl sulfide bonds need UV - low ferroportins is probably linked to a low quantum environment . Since it is degraded through phosphorylation it might low levels also be due to poor protein folding due to inadequate DHA or ATP? Or high deuterium levels?

What about hepcidin? Would it be the too much hepcidin = ECT going too fast? Blue light? High D levels? Uncoupling?

My brain is still working on lower than desired dopamine levels (probably from too much fun in my teens  ) so don't know if I am on the right track here.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferroportin-hepcidin-and-cancer-prognosis.22173/post-252184>

on re reading the above a fast ect = high hepdicin wouldn't make sense. A fast ECT might down regulate phosphorylation?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-11T04:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferroportin-hepcidin-and-cancer-prognosis.22173/post-252863>

HERE IS A SAMPLE of the info quality posted for physicians in the healthcare practitioner forum: The iron metabolism story is linked to poor RBC circadian function and will be critical for every physician to get down pat. The loss of iron movements in cells is linked to higher heteroplasmy levels. It will always link back to Vitamin A becoming weaponized to destroy the red light chromophores in the mitochondria. How does it happen? That is quite complex. A loss of the control of iron metabolism in humans will always smell like of a melanopsin dysfunction is because of how iron is handled in humans.

[Heme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hematologist)/onc MD's have been especially interested in systemic iron [metabolism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metabolism) in the diseases they treat because iron is essential for [red blood cells](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_blood_cells), where **MOST** of the human body's iron is contained. As most of you clinicians know already I believe all cancers are circadian disease linked to bad mitochondrial biology. So how is this all interconnected?

So when you know where most of the iron should be and where it should not be........you are lead to getting a deeper understanding of what controls heme synthesis and IRON. And that answer shocks small thinkers. The answer is the circadian biology of mitochondria.

Sunlight stimulates RBC creation and drives the flow of iron in your body so it too is a melanopsin issue. WHY?

Heme synthesis begins its first step in mitochondria. If you have a circadian mismatch disease tied to melanopsin dysfunction by definition you are blue light toxic or nnEMF toxic and this cause a defect in mitophagy and ruins the iron cycle in humans.

People in healthcare often forget heme synthesis occurs partly in the mitochondria and partly in the cytoplasm. This is exactly how the TCA and urea cycle are built in us too. The process begins in the mitochondria because one of the precursors is found only there. Since this reaction is regulated in part by the concentration of heme, the final step (which produces the heme) is also mitochondrial.

People with defects in iron always have a blood dyscrasia at some level and it usually bleeds into fertility and thyroid hormone creation too because of its link to AM sunlight deficiency.

Remember purple light = UV light.

Purple light in sunlight stimulates RBC synthesis in humans. Blue light cause RBC's to break down sooner and live a shorter life than they can. What is the color of the main breakdown color of heme? It is the complementary yellow color, of bilirubin. Yellow is the complement of purple and orange is the complement of blue in the color wheel.

This is also why people with iron issues often have B12 and folate issues and most small thinker blame this on methylation problems but they forget that methylation is also a circadian controlled process. So.......mst people with RBC issues also have methylation defects.

UV light is purple in the visible spectrum so anyone who is anemic is deficient in SUN redox. This is also why B12 and folate absorption spectra are yellow too and are both linked to poor solar redox. Also, recall from Fritz Popp work every cell releases ELF-UV in a quantized fashion. People with bad redox always emit more UV than they absorb and they usually have iron metabolism issues that manifest in their blood. This is why I wrote the recent patreon blog about looking at your peripheral blood smear to see if you are nnEMF toxic. Once you know where the piece fits the story begins to make a lot of sense.......both quantum and common sense. [#melanopsinwisdom](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/melanopsinwisdom?hc_location=ufi) 101.

In the meantime, if cost is not an issue, first blush thoughts as I consider my yearly 3 to 6-month escapes in the sun...

a) Instead of a building, I will live on a 58' Leopard Catamaran anchored off a different coastal town each year - Immersing myself in the daily sungazing and free electrons of the ocean away from all the EMF and 5G smog. If I were to escape to a sunny lot of land - with unobstructed views of the sunrise or sunset, in a rural environment, and off the grid if possible, near a beach, but close enough to visit a city for essential food - there's just too many precautions for me think about. Being on the water takes care of so much. Anchor off the coast of a marina doesn't cost anything, well except for the boat! If you have a couple of friends with a similar vision and could help with the initial purchase? Boom! Sorry, I understand this is probably not helpful at all to you.

b) Easy access to 100% grass-fed beef, fresh seafood, (or whatever you eat) clean water to drink (and swim in!)

c) As the architect for my future life, I think great architecture is like a really great poem. You have to work it work it work it, you reduce it down to the things that only say what they have to say and you get rid of everything else and that's where the sweet spot is for me. Jack has helped me with this vision as I simplify life and embrace ancestral habits. Check out Geoffrey Warner, Alchemy Architects

# Thread: Deuterium depleted water

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PeterL — 2018-10-02T08:09:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22192/post-252134>

Hello I was wondering of there are members that are familiar with the properties of distilled water in relationship to the levels of deuterium. I have been drinking partially distilled water now for a number of years.

By partially distilled I mean that I will turn off the distiller when the water about 85 % boiled off, (on a timer). My rational is that since deuterium boils at a slightly higher temperature than 100 C. that the remaining water left would be higher in deuterium and is therefore discarded. The 85% water that has been distilled should be lower in deuterium I believe.

Any thoughts on this would be greatly appreciated. Thanks Peter

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-02T19:38:31+0300

# Thread: Pre-Kruse like Pre-Med, what to study to understand?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-kruse-like-pre-med-what-to-study-to-understand.22202/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### greentrees — 2018-10-02T08:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-kruse-like-pre-med-what-to-study-to-understand.22202/post-252144>

Hi everyone, just wondering if anyone has an idea of what to study to understand Kruse's work? If you had to take a pre-med course to help with understanding medicine, what would a pre-Kruse program look like? What subjects have helped you along the road?

I'm currently studying entry physics, chemistry and life sciences at uni as I have zero science background and after 8 months of reading the blogs, listening to webinars, I needed a science background to understand. I started with wanting to learn what deuterium was but if you don't know about atoms, their isotopes, what a neutron is and atomic weight (none of which I knew) then the deuterium story won't be understood. So chemistry is a must imo. I wonder if we could come up with a basic list of concepts or courses to learn to make other concepts more understood. I'm finding physics is mostly about the motion of objects and not finding it particularly relevant as of yet, way too many tennis balls

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-02T19:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-kruse-like-pre-med-what-to-study-to-understand.22202/post-252179>

That is what the Farm will provide for future clinicians who want to learn how to use their education. Here is a sample of what I will bring to the table.[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...otif\_id=1538491234905386&notif\_t=feed\_comment](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2338793329518341?comment_id=2338941432836864&reply_comment_id=2340389366025404&notif_id=1538491234905386&notif_t=feed_comment)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-02T19:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-kruse-like-pre-med-what-to-study-to-understand.22202/post-252182>

My October 2018 webinar being released today is all tied to this link here.....Why are arteries weak in some people versus others? Note she lived in Ireland. That is a big clue to the webinar......[https://www.livescience.com/63720-beach-wave-ruptures-womans-artery.html](https://www.livescience.com/63720-beach-wave-ruptures-womans-artery.html)

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-10-03T22:36:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-kruse-like-pre-med-what-to-study-to-understand.22202/post-252279>

> [greentrees said:](/goto/post?id=252144)
>
> Hi everyone, just wondering if anyone has an idea of what to study to understand Kruse's work? If you had to take a pre-med course to help with understanding medicine, what would a pre-Kruse program look like? What subjects have helped you along the road?
>
> I'm currently studying entry physics, chemistry and life sciences at uni as I have zero science background and after 8 months of reading the blogs, listening to webinars, I needed a science background to understand. I started with wanting to learn what deuterium was but if you don't know about atoms, their isotopes, what a neutron is and atomic weight (none of which I knew) then the deuterium story won't be understood. So chemistry is a must imo. I wonder if we could come up with a basic list of concepts or courses to learn to make other concepts more understood. I'm finding physics is mostly about the motion of objects and not finding it particularly relevant as of yet, way too many tennis balls

All the books Jack recommends are intended for lay audiences. They are a good starting point. I have found the works of Amir Aczel helpful. He has written a lot of popular texts on physics and quantum mechanics. Otherwise I have been using an app called Scribd to download and listen to every book on electricity, magnetism, and light that I can find. That's been helpful. Otherwise just broadly studying the sciences at uni is the way to go.

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Forest — 2018-10-02T08:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-fix-housing.22206/post-252148>

as a transitions place
For one living on a student budget

Make it so easy that a 5 y.o. Can implement

Plz

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-02T17:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-fix-housing.22206/post-252163>

Forest,

The only 'quick fix' for securing an EMF-safe domicile is the _absolute cardinal rule_\-- **Prudent Avoidance**. That means finding a place to live that is reasonably distant from sources of microwave RF radiation (cell towers, antennas, airport navigation radar, 5G DAS mimo antennas, wi-fry sources, etc.).

You can only determine how safe you are by reliable metrics-- by measuring ambient exposure with meter equipment (if you are on a student budget be creative and rent or borrow such equipment, or try to barter with a building biologist for services).

You may also have to contend with dirty electricity and stray voltage in the house, not to mention ELF radiation from wiring and appliances.

This may all sound overwhelming, so start with baby steps and work to ensure a clean, healthy environment. We assume of course that you have already ditched a personal cell phone/smartphone and wi-fi-- those are steps most in your immediate control.

Good luck
