# Thread: James' optimal journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-optimal-journal.22211/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Gerard — 2018-10-02T20:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-optimal-journal.22211/post-252187>

I happened upon a Jack Kruse video a few weeks ago about fluoride and now I'm here. Here is a basic summary of some sh\*t.
My Mother (where it sort of begins)
Couldn't get pregnant, she went to a variety of different doctors to try to find what was wrong.
Dr William L Gentry found endometriosis with an addition of fibroid tumors on my mothers ovaries.
He did a surgery. (or series of surgeries I am not sure)
She was able to have children. This started with my older brother who was born through C-section in 1986.
I followed 2 years later as a C-section too. I was sexed with a procedure that was deemed necessary for my safety. Amneocentesis.In 1994 my mother was having unusual bleeding. She had a uterus full of fibroid tumors. My mother had to wait for a year becuase of the family business before she was 'allowed' to take time off to have a hysterectomy.

On my Birthday July 22, 1994 My mother had her hysterectomy.

After Surgery she was given a button to self administer morphine.
It was 9 pm and she had just called my dad complaining about how her legs were restless.

The pain management nurse came in.

PMN: go ahead and press the button

Mom: I don't feel like I need it just yet

PMN: I'm not leaving until you press the button

Mom: eventually pressed the button

PMN: didn't say anything, just got up and left

Within 1 minute My mom felt her heart start beating as fast as she could imagine it beating. She couldn't breathe. The button for the nurse did not work. She tried calling out, but no one could hear her. Finally a black nurse was walking by and brought over a machine that my mom believes the nurse used to check her blood presure. This nurse immediately called the head nurse and the head nurse came in and immediately started preparing for defribrilation. At that moment my mom felt sick and just before she vomited a short Indian doctor came over to her and asked her ' how are you feeling right now Mrs. Gerard? ' the head nurse snapped at him saying ' she can't breathe !' Her blood pressure came down. My mothers doctor was called, but he was at a party ( his birthday is the 23rd the day after mine). The next morning her doctor is there and says to her ' Well all I can tell you is don't take morphine anymore'. He left abruptly.

After the Hysterectomy, my mom's neck was injured during surgery. She had a tough time getting better. A chiropractor and a naturopath were the only people she trusted, because she was getting results from them. Her view or sentiment is that, she had had surgery before, but these people almost killed her and didn't seem to care.

About 2 years later when I was about 10 my doctor wanted me to have a vaccine for STDs and at this point my parents decided to not go back. Dr Akram Salihi had brought in a separate Dr., Dr Hadeel Naqib, and things were getting strange where they had previously been great. I think there were a few times I did need to go back for school or baseball related physicals, but generally speaking I have not seen a doctor since then.

Next I'll write about my brother. And then myself to sort of Kick off my optimal Journal.​

### James Gerard — 2018-10-04T17:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-optimal-journal.22211/post-252343>

**My brother:**
In 2008, my brother discovered he had an anaplastic astrocytoma that was causing bouts of brownout in his vision.

He subsequently had 2 resections. The first in late january of 2009, and the second in 2014.

One night he was out with a cop that my brother and I grew up with. My brother had a few beers. My brother and the cop both got in separate cars to leave the bar. My brother was with a friend in his car. My brother gets pulled over by a cop (different cop). The cop makes him do a breathalizer and makes him do a walking test (on a hill). He blew one point over the legal 0.09 . So he gets a DWI. From what I can remember this happened after both resections( my brother was asked if there was anything that would effect his balance). My Brother was looking into Professor Ben Williams (Surviving terminal cancer) and had also flown to Texas to be treated by Dr Burzynski. At the Burzynski clinic. I believe the only treatment he was able to recieve was targeted gene therapy (not the anteneoplaston).

My brother was interested in getting a treatment of proton therapy or proton radiation. The insurance we have is shared in a family business. When my brother was in Texas the coverage suddenly stopped for some reason, no one was able to use their coverage. Then it came back on the next day as if the power had gone out. This is only one of many instances of timed events against my brother receiving treatment. Under Obamacare proton radiation treatment was marked as experimental and thus was no longer covered by our insurance, even though they said it was covered. I am uncertain as to whether he had an opportunity at an alternative radiation treatment or laser therapy where he would need to sign off on possibly being mentally retarded afterward. This might have been the proton therapy.

In 2007 Martin O'malley signed into law some things dealing with a 'task force' for drunk driving in Maryland. What happened between my brother, the insurance company, the MVA, and the Police department is extremely disturbing. That is all I will write here so as to not go into detail I am not comfortable with posting.

My brother moved some years ago and he had a neighbor that would mow his lawn a lot on a tractor mower. My brother got into conversation with him and as it turns out, the MVA, the insurance company, and the police department had done the exact same thing to this man as what they did to my brother. He liked to drive his tractor because he was not allowed to drive. His neighbor died from his tumor.

I believe if it wasn't for Burzynski and mainly Prof. Ben Williams, my brother might be dead by now.

The takeaway is that if you get a tumor, ( or sick in any way that disrupts the story being told ) your insurance will fight you for the treatment you want (in spite of the experimental treatment laws preceding Trumps right to try)

( I like Trumps right to try btw. It's like a little crack of light into the can of worms, now all someone needs if a crowbar ).

You will be pulled over and you will need to maneuver through the outrageous activity of the MVA and the police department and your insurance company at the same time in hopes you will just give up and die.

Another takeaway is Dr Burzynski. If you want evidence of the lack of virtue in people and why you need to watch your own ass, look no further than what has been done to him. There is a documentary on youtube.

My brother is approaching another 5 year mark, which will hopefully put him in a survival bracket he is more comfortable with, to ease tension at least. I've linked him some things about Jack Kruse, but I'm not sure what he thinks yet.

My hope is that this will serve as a utility that I can come back and edit or make notes about based on what I can find on my own about siblings and the nature of my health and his.

​

### James Gerard — 2018-11-09T18:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-optimal-journal.22211/post-255327>

I have had a UHS2 AC Gauss meter from alpha labs for 2 or 3 weeks. I have been testing different areas in my house and work, noticing changes at different times of day. The reading stays at .15 Milligauss for most of the house except the kitchen where it can jump to .8 . In the morning, the reading goes as high as .4 Milligauss outside and .2 Milligauss inside. I have yet to measure my basement in the morning: I will get a reading and update this post. I plan to make a map of all the sources I find and add to it so I can go over this over and over and over again and each time making sure to do something to change for the better. I would sleep in my basement, but there are refrigerators that cause the basement space to be higher than in my bedroom at night, so I am keeping my bed in my bedroom for now

My magnetico sleep mat will arrive today.

I have Curbed exposing my eyes to any artificial light when it gets dark. Sometimes it is dark before I leave work and I stay on my computer after dark, because it is constantly getting darker earlier and I fail to compensate. In october I was much more successful at totally staying in the dark when the sun went down. Being in the dark, I'd go to bed. Going to bed at 7pm, I would wake up at 3 or 4 AM. I sleep near a large window and it seems that it was staying very light at night during the first part of the changing season, because the light would wake me up at 3:00AM and 4:00 AM. Now it is staying dark again and I seem to wake up at around 5:00 AM even though it is getting darker earlier.

I have been taking cold showers. My shower seems to be right at 60 degrees F when on cold (note this is wrong, I believe it is more around 50 or 48 degrees fahrenheit updated4/4/2019), so I just use that. I have a high sierra shower head with a steel hose that recently replaced a dingy old plastic and metal shower head with a broken plastic hose. I sometimes go without showering. I have not washed my hair or body with soap since I got a new showerhead, and so far there has been no problem with that and perhaps a slight benefit I can feel. At first I was using coconut oil on my skin because I was experiencing after shower itchiness, especially on my legs and back. Now I don't even need the coconut oil and there is no itch after I shower, perhaps a slight tingling.

I eat Bacon, pork chops, steak, or eggs in the morning. for the last 3 days as my one meal a day. Before that I would have lunches and this being Maryland where I live, eating out I would get crab dip or shrimp or steak. I had been following a ketogenic diet prescribed by Dr Eric Berg in the summer that included as many vegetables as one could eat, so I have been eating seasonally in that respect because I have stopped most vegetables at this point, I will eat broccoli once a week. I have been noticing the help from the ketogenic dietary change since the beginning of march and I have never stressed over whether I thought I was in ketosis. I was eating 7 cups of spring mix in a salad every night with hard boiled eggs.

I have some books that have helped me a lot so far in my attempt to be slow, but thorough in what I learn. I'd like to mention 2.

Mathematics in 10 lessons by Jerry P King. This book has been an inspiration to learn about things like Sinichi Mochizuki solving the abc conjecture and what is the story of math?

Old Books and New Histories: An Orientation to Studies in Book and Print Culture by Leslie Howsam. This book is very valuable in it's outline. It is short, but very valuable in the perception of skills regarding different book professions and where they seem to overlap and what the conflicts are embedded in this situation.

I want to get my labs done so I can discern what my problems might be with more accuracy than looking in a mirror or with a comparison to the Bristol stool chart. Hopefully my next post I can share test results.

### WarrenT — 2018-10-03T03:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-interpretation.22215/post-252220>

Hello!
I would love any help in interpreting my numbers.

### PeterL — 2018-10-03T05:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22217/post-252223>

Hello I was wondering of there are members that are familiar with the properties of distilled water in relationship to the levels of deuterium. I have been drinking partially distilled water now for a number of years.

By partially distilled I mean that I will turn off the distiller when the water about 85 % boiled off, (on a timer). My rational is that since deuterium boils at a slightly higher temperature than 100 C. that the remaining water left would be higher in deuterium and is therefore discarded. The 85% water that has been distilled should be lower in deuterium I believe.

Any thoughts on this would be greatly appreciated. Thanks Peter

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-03T06:29:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22217/post-252225>

### JanSz — 2018-10-13T22:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22217/post-253081>

> [PeterL said:](/goto/post?id=252223)
>
> Hello I was wondering of there are members that are familiar with the properties of distilled water in relationship to the levels of deuterium. I have been drinking partially distilled water now for a number of years.
>
> By partially distilled I mean that I will turn off the distiller when the water about 85 % boiled off, (on a timer). My rational is that since deuterium boils at a slightly higher temperature than 100 C. that the remaining water left would be higher in deuterium and is therefore discarded. The 85% water that has been distilled should be lower in deuterium I believe.
>
> Any thoughts on this would be greatly appreciated. Thanks Peter

Any water that you use, tap water, RO or other popular (and cheap) waters
will have 150ppm D/H (any differences are insignificant).
Taking that kind o 150ppm water
you can
cool it until it have 32F=0C but it is still liquid
shake it vigorously until you see some ice in the bottle
drain the liquid, discard (the small amount ) of ice
What you got is
water frozen once with deuterium content
real close to
145.084ppm (D/H)
145.084 =(-68.54/1000+1)\*155.76 frozen 1x

My friend (in Poland) did that and followed that with testing at Eurofins

I did that (in USA) and found that it is easy to accomplish the (freezing/discard ice) routine
even more than once if you wish.

For my water freezing I used 1.5L Preventa bottles previously filled with DDW.
They have somewhat significant volume and are easy to store in vertical position in the door of my freezer.

..............
....................

### JanSz — 2018-10-13T22:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22217/post-253085>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=253083)
>
> I want to make sure I understand this.
>
> He started with 155.76 and froze once and then had 145? Those two numbers were measured?

No, he started with 150ppm (assumed), he did not tested the initial water.
The 155.76 you see comes from V-SMOW
In results from Eurofins my friend got
δX=(-68.54)
so he had to recalculate that to get ppm
145.084 =(-68.54/1000+1)\*155.76

So,(assuming that the starting water had 150ppm, and knowing that after one freezing it had 145ppm
145/150=

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4791982/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4791982/)

Anyhow, that temperature is rather useless for anything else, so dedicated freezer is better option.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-14T00:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water.22217/post-253094>

^^^the point of the sign is clear.....most sunlight is between 600-3100nm. That is why all the key chromophores are red. Same is true in water. Red light moves things with mass and since deuterium is double the atom mass of H+ red light is more effective moving H+ over deuterium which is the job of the cytochromes and the ATPase.

# Thread: Getting Started
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-started.22219/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shane Saunders — 2018-10-03T08:43:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-started.22219/post-252229>

Hi, my name is Shane Saunders, I'm a co-owner of Breathe Me, and I'm also co-host of The Breathing Edge Podcast.

I served in the Australian Defence force for 13 years, two tours in the Middle East and I had the privilege of training our nation’s Navy divers, sailors, soldiers and Elite Special Forces. It was here I developed a desire to understand how people can reach their peak and maintain that level consistently.

From an early age, I've always been a sungazer especially in the morning, and I love Sunset.

I’ve invested thousands of hours (and many thousands of dollars) studying and trialling multiple disciplines, and I thought I was on the right track with the breathing and cold immersion, and now I came across Dr Jack's work now the pieces are falling into place.

I've joined the group on here to share ideas and learn what it is that I don't know.

Much Appreciation.

# Thread: Social Stereotypes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-stereotypes.22220/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2018-10-03T10:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-stereotypes.22220/post-252231>

The more i dig into a connected natural lifestyle the more i reflect on the impact of social stereotypes and how they are as bad/deleterious for us as flouride in our water or aluminium in our bodies.

Sunglasses, Drinking (particularly socially accepted levels of alcoholism (i am from australia...its prevalent there)), Recreational drugs (the art of 'being cool' in younger years), Fashion/clothing (socially perceived ideals of dressing 'correctly'), Religion, Eating at certain times, Eating certain foods, Shoes, Healthcare, Education, Nudity etc etc

Its seems that many of these are given to us to develop an emotional attitude of acceptance to 'socially pushed ideals collective reality' and divorce us from the act of critical thinking ourselves.

I appreciate the intelligent yet ever sometimes cynical, always intelligent and quite often humorous approach here.

It is a much appreciated break from such 'supposed reality'....lol

And thanks Jack for being ever so woody......whilst you have delivered many splinters in your approach I get glimpses of your polished mahogany masterpiece which helps me master my own.

CHeers all

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-03T16:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-stereotypes.22220/post-252240>

I'm glad you're seeing the wisdom in why being an asshole has a point. That is how we wake up the sleeping to see what they cannot. It is the antidote of the Dunning Kruger effect. I just went nuclear on some Japanese guy in the EMF warriors group this AM to wake many up.......that group needs a kick in the ass.

### Gina R — 2018-10-20T18:46:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-stereotypes.22220/post-253804>

> [Simon Wright said:](/goto/post?id=252231)

>

>
> Its seems that many of these are given to us to develop an emotional attitude of acceptance to 'socially pushed ideals collective reality' and divorce us from the act of critical thinking ourselves.
>
> I appreciate the intelligent yet ever sometimes cynical, always intelligent and quite often humorous approach here.
>
> It is a much appreciated break from such 'supposed reality'....lol
>
> And thanks Jack for being ever so woody......whilst you have delivered many splinters in your approach I get glimpses of your polished mahogany masterpiece which helps me master my own.

I hear you! The further I go in the journey, the more and more I see all this around me too. It's soooooo present and impossible to ignore (not that I want to ignore it). I feel an internal pull to be as far away from it as possible!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-20T23:36:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-stereotypes.22220/post-253833>

I'm trying to get people to see the forest from the trees:

MORE ON FACTOR Y: American life expectancy falling, as our tech addiction is rising. Let's not pretend all is well with our 'progress' in this 5G blue-lit world. By 2040, US projected to tumble to 64th place for life expectancy, behind China, a shaming national failure. I hope you really like that fast Netflix downloads for streaming your blue light addictions. [https://www.cnn.com/2018/10/17/health/life-expectancy-forecasts-study-intl/index.html](https://www.cnn.com/2018/10/17/health/life-expectancy-forecasts-study-intl/index.html)

# Thread: Patients are not always their best advocates.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-03T17:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-252251>

Patients are not always their best advocates. No wonder the public is in deep.........they cannot think well. Patients Give Doctors High Marks For Prescribing Antibiotics and rated themselves happiest with their doctor's visit when they got an antibiotic after seeking care for a respiratory tract infection, such as a common cold, whether they needed it or not. [https://n.pr/2DMVq5q](https://n.pr/2DMVq5q)

### Brett Jacques ND — 2018-10-03T22:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-252277>

when there was less emf then the antibiotic placebo effect for viral infections was less problematic now in our blue lit, maxed out emf world, we're seeing real problems with an RX with every visit

### DrEttinger — 2018-10-11T06:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-252869>

My first 2 comprehensive visits with new patients are spent mainly deprogramming. When they finally stop saying, "but what about...?" and "I was always told..." together we begin at the basics and start a fresh build. Within 2 weeks, most know more than all the doctors they've ever visited. Patients only know what they know and what they know has come from their doctors, media, blogs, neighbors, relatives... About as much useful data to fill a thimble. JK, I appreciate your passion!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-11T17:44:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-252904>

It bothers me when I visit a medical school or residency program and see so much talent that is in the pursuit of myth. I think we have a duty to alter that trajectory. The best way to engage the mind is teaching it how to think and not what it should be thinking about. The same is true for the public

### Gina R — 2018-11-02T01:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-254781>

Yes, as a practitioner, seeing all the energy that is spent in "pursuit of myth", is extremely frustrating. I am by far not perfect at this but I agree, we have a duty to alter that. A great way, I find, is to relate to my clients regarding the train of thought that comes up, which they express...meaning I can say "Yup, I used to think in that way as well..." etc.

### Gina R — 2018-11-02T01:06:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patients-are-not-always-their-best-advocates.22222/post-254782>

> [Brett Jacques ND said:](/goto/post?id=252277)
>
> when there was less emf then the antibiotic placebo effect for viral infections was less problematic now in our blue lit, maxed out emf world, we're seeing real problems with an RX with every visit

### Ossa — 2018-10-03T18:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-cures-anxiety.22223/post-252263>

Just wondering if people had suffered with anxiety like myself and how the leptin rx impacted that?

### James Lech — 2018-10-04T15:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dangers-of-5g-wifi-in-schools.22228/post-252333>

5G WiFi is being called Wi-Fi 6. Wi-Fi 6 implementing MU-MIMO and OFDMA for upload and download. This is the same as 5G. In watching JK's talks it is better explained in Vermont 2018, but will be very well explained in Vermont 2019 with the Gut series further explored. With Wi-Fi 6 it is not about power-density but how the actual physics of the waveforms have been changed that is the real danger. Additionally, what is already done by many contract service providers in branding their own telecom device is the pigg-back service / Internet of things. Meaning that person's that walk past your home will be able to piggy-back onto the router for their cellular communications. It is how the industry has gotten around the issue of citing cell towers, by instead having users, in the fine print, authorize for the router to facilitate an external service. This is why you mentioned it being 5G WiFi as it is the same thing, but industry to aid consumers have now called it Wi-Fi 6 vs the complex codes and names from before. It also sets a forced standard for all communication devices going forward to subscribe by.

With Wi-Fi 6, the argument will be that it immits a lower power density and the device you use will have a better battery life, thereby an inverse square law of less radiation, however with the 5G (MU-MIMO and OFDMA), because there are more spectrums there is more impact on the biology. On the bright side, it will be easier to see an epidemiologic pattern as the WiFi 6 will be standardized, vs the 5G for transmitters would be different from zip code to zip code.

When your distilled water is ready,
you can put it in the freezer
when it reaches 0 degrees Celsius (but is still liquid)
take it out of the freezer
shake it untill you see ice in the bottle
drain the water, keep it
throw away ice
the water should have about
145.084ppm instead 150ppm that is a very small positive change
you could freeze that water again and get another couple points

for example VOSS makes big show out of just 146.6ppm and charges accordingly

# Thread: Reverse Type 2 Diabetes!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-type-2-diabetes.22230/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Antonis — 2018-10-04T12:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-type-2-diabetes.22230/post-252324>

Yesterday my Dad told me a story about a man who lives here in Cyprus. At this time the man is 80 years old and he lives a healthy life.
About 10 years ago this man was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes. He was already in his 70's but refused to use any medical care. Then, over time, some problems began to appear. Doctors warned him that he should start using insulin, otherwise he would soon need surgery. His legs already began to get thinner but he insisted that no one would touch him.
Well, my dad narrated the story and I just wanted to share it with you!

I do not know any further details about what happened, all I know is that this old man started to walk every day from 7am until 1:30pm in the sun without t-shirt, hat and sunglasses. Since he was diagnosed with diabetes, he did not stop a day walking in the sun and traveling long distances around the beaches. Then he returns back to his car and goes home.

This old man is now 80 years old and has managed to reverse diabetes type 2 with the power of the sun and without using pills, insulin or even surgery as the doctors have warned him.
Here we are! This is what Doctor Jack says without any doubt.

Thank you doctor Jack for being in our way and opened our eyes. You have all the rights and you have definitely your reasons why you get pissed sometimes.
Thank you for the knowledge you give me.

> ....
> Satiated the hungry do not understand

This reminds me of a scene in the book The Long Winter, in the Little House on the Prairie series by Laura Ingalls Wilder. (These are classic autobiographical novels about early American settlers in the unforgiving upper midwest that I read over and over as a little girl. I’ll bet it would be fun to read them again from the mitochondriac perspective.)

A particularly brutal winter leaves Laura’s settlement nearly starving as supply trains are blocked for months. Her friend (and future husband) recalls that an outlying claim planted wheat over the summer, and he makes a daring trip through treacherous conditions to see if they have any to spare. The outliers claim they have barely enough for themselves, but clever Alonzo noticed that the exterior building dimensions don’t match the interior. He confidently approaches an interior wall and removes an odd peg. Abundant wheat pours out and he negotiates to purchase some.

Will Alonzo make it back with his sled piled high with wheat to save Laura and the other settlers????

Ah, be still my heart!

Lovely tale of what it takes to survive when truly starving. I’m sure there are many others out there with far less romantically idealistic outcomes.

Suggestions? I already know about this one, and it is reluctantly on the list...

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ou-might-need-those-skills.21667/#post-246249](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-you-up-to-speed-on-your-ct-and-hunting-you-might-need-those-skills.21667/#post-246249)

Jan, love that phrase, “Satiated the hungry do not understand.”

Edit: And yes, I did read and like Ayn Rand’s Atlas Shrugged...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organite-thoughts.22235/post-252385>

Anyone have a say about Organite. I find it interesting and suspect for the fact that (William Reich) was jailed and all of his research was burned and buried.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-29T17:18:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organite-thoughts.22235/post-254495>

Organite seems to have picked up a lot of public interest lately.

Anyone persecuted by TPTB for their innovations or theories that go against the grain is someone I would pay a lot of attention to. Reich was ahead of his time with his views on something he called Orgone energy and he deserves more than to be dismissed out of hand.

There are some extreme claims for Orgone practical benefits (such as curing diseases with Reich's orgone energy accumulator device, cancelling health destroying effects of cell towers with organite, weather modification, RF in general etc).

But extreme claims require extreme proofs. Therein lies the problem. There are no reliable/repeatable methods to test organite's ability to mitigate RF and other environmental toxins (at least that I am aware of).

I have made some Orgonite, but more for entertainment than in interests of science. I am always open to new ideas, especially if they can be validated by either N=1 or N=1000000.

Organite is unfortunately still in gray area of unproven beliefs. That may not always be the case. But _never ever say never_ in this amazing and surprising world.

# Thread: Deuterium Depletion with Dr. Que Collins and Dr. Laszlo Boros
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depletion-with-dr-que-collins-and-dr-laszlo-boros.22238/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2018-10-05T14:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depletion-with-dr-que-collins-and-dr-laszlo-boros.22238/post-252397>

"Today we're talking about an element that you probably didn't learn about in school, deuterium, and how it is likely contributing to the slow but steady degeneration of the human population. Our guests are Dr. Que Collins and Dr. Laszlo Boros, who are leading experts working to educate people on deuterium through the Center for Deuterium Depletion so that we can heal our bodies and improve the health of future generations. So what exactly is deuterium? It is a rare isotope of hydrogen (only 0.000155% of hydrogen atoms) that contains a proton and a neutron in its nucleus, and the extra neutron doubles the mass of this type of hydrogen atom. And when two deuteriums combine with oxygen, the resulting substance is referred to as “heavy” water, 2H2O, because of the additional mass. Although heavy water tastes and looks the same as ordinary water, it doesn't behave the same: it has a higher boiling point, a lower freezing point, and ice cubes made with heavy water will sink in ordinary water. These differences demonstrate that the presence of Deuterium can change chemical behavior. So why does that matter? Well, hydrogen is in the air we breathe, the food we eat, and the water we drink, and as we take hydrogen into our body, it becomes part of our blood, organs, DNA, and everything else that makes us who we are. So when deuterium builds up in your body, it changes chemical reactions at a cellular level and has devastating effects on your metabolism. Deuterium buildup can affect your ability to create and use energy, limit your DNA's ability to replicate, change how you respond to clinical treatments, and generally change, on a fundamental level, how your body works and, as a result, who you are. So sit down and get ready for Deuterium 101 because the doctors are about to share some information that everyone on the planet needs to know."

# Thread: Grounding in the winter question?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonR — 2018-10-05T18:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252412>

Seem like a dumb question but, I'm gonna ask anyway. Obviously being grounded is best with bare feet on the ground away from electrical currents is it the same in the winter. I live in Wisconsin and when the snow hits am I getting the same grounded effect by sticking my bare feet in the snow? water conducts and is still attached to the ground. or is the snow creating some sort of insulator and or is it diluting the effects of being completely grounded. switching gears, I always thought I was grounding every day for the better being that I install powerlines underground everyday day and I'm in the sun and my hands are in the dirt almost every day, but I'm surrounded by electric current. I have read into organite and I carry it with me everyday, as well as south poled a handful of magnets together to try and create some sort of repulse against what's coming at me. still learning the science of it all.

### Jan Christer — 2018-10-05T23:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252441>

If you stand on the ground in snow I think you would be pretty well grounded, and the same for having your feet in moist snow, since water conducts the earth's ground. But if you just stick down your feet in loose, fluffy snow, without firm pressure on the ground, then I don't think you would be well grounded. Best check would be of course if you can test this with body voltage measurements.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-06T00:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252448>

What I’ve done is put a black bucket over the spot where I want to ground. Then if it snows that spot is protected. Sometimes I’ll take out a kettle of hot water and pour it on the spot to create some nice warm mud. Depends how crazy I’m feeling that day...

### JasonR — 2018-10-06T02:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-252459>

yea that would work. I remember seeing a video of a guy put a coiled copper line buried into a compost pile and ran water through it, and the heat from the decaying matter produced hot water, wonder how big of a compost pile you would need to keep enough heat to maintain a hot spot im shooting off the hip here kinda thinking it up as I type hahah. it sounds good in my mind

### Simon Suh — 2018-10-21T22:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253871>

I recently heard a Doctor named Sam Milham talking about how much dirty electricity is running on ground we stand on. I am curious to know if it is even safe to do grounding anymore.

### JasonR — 2018-10-22T02:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253888>

I guess it would depend on what size wire and how deep its buried. most wire through residential area is pushing between 15kv and 25kv and the standard depth is 30 inches deep min most of the time its 36 to 48 inches deep. you get into cities that run more juice 750 kv and if its 3 phase wire times that by 3 same with 3 phase 15 and 17kv. every thing is just getting stepped down you have transmission lines that carry a lot of power long distances those are the lines you see 100 feet in the air lots of emfs. then they may go into switch gears and transformers and lower it to your service wires running to homes and business. if you see telephone poles with power lines on it where you live and your powerline comes off a pole to your home then theres no power under your feet. if you see green boxes about 30 by 30 square around you theres underground power.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-winter-question.22240/post-253894>

# Thread: Industry Burning People and Narrative Control
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/industry-burning-people-and-narrative-control.22242/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2018-10-05T21:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/industry-burning-people-and-narrative-control.22242/post-252428>

Narrative is everything for controlling perceptions. Are we being "burned" by Industry? WATCH THE VIDEO. Adam is showing us, in part, how that old phone game of telling details about a story from one person the next is very much alive and kicking. Omission of information is often the larger problem: when details are removed from a story (AKA narrative), it typically leaves another person to make assumptions and fill in what is missing, When Person A whispers a story in Person B's ear, and the Person B whispers the same story to Person C, and the process repeats itself down to Person Z, it is rare if we DON'T GET a new story created. The wise will expect the original story may have nothing to do with the original story at all.

Do we see the narrative pattern on the EMF Issue? It is could be the largest in all history. Those that break down story-telling understand how the mental perception of the masses are controlled. Can you also see the 'hypocrazy' with how someone can be injured by hot water due to a short-term damage source, but when a person is damaged by a longer-term damage source, society can be convinced to look away from 'something implied as minor.' Society and even our court systems rarely consider, "Is it okay to be damaged at all by any source at any intensity or concentration?" Because some types of poisons and damage sources can slow-moving, many unlearned people around us tend to chalk up the injuries are 'lesser' or 'nothing to be concerned about' even though the damage could result in a glioblastoma a decade or two into the future. There are many slower-poison snake bite potentials in this world.

When human flesh is burned with 10 molar Sulfuric Acid, wounds show up very quickly. I have seen this first-hand on my arm. But if a person is burned with 0.01 molar Sulfuric Acid, it may take a lot longer for the damage to appear and manifest to know something is going on at all...sometimes after the lab technician has gone home for the day away from the lab and notices the red splotch appear; and yes I have seen this also first hand literally on my hand. This is one analogy I often use for nnEMF. The Intensity of nnEMF exposures (concentration for biochemicals) is a factor, but not the only one. There are a great number of synergies at work, due to the biophysics and biochemistry involved to promote damage and disease to every single one of our 40+ trillion cells, and then attempt to heal an insult to our biology.

Now listen to what Adam says about the "Disinformation Campaign" and how the "Media bought it."

# Thread: Paul's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-optimal-journal.22245/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul Walia — 2018-10-06T20:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-optimal-journal.22245/post-252501>

Hi guys,

My name is Paul Walia, and I am 24 years old. I was born in Punjab, India and came to California when I was 8 years old. I grew up and currently live in a small town called Ukiah, CA (population: 16,000 -20,000) - which is 60 miles north of Santa Rosa, CA (the place with all the fires after the 5G testing).

**Maternal Linage Information:**

My grandmother, who lives in India, had breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and currently, cancer has traveled to her lymph nodes - which is most likely due to the fluorescent lights she has lived under for decades, and many new nnEMFs put into her home over the years. However, my mom who lives with me in Ukiah, CA is doing okay and has not seen any major health concerns.

**Personal Health Information/Journey:**

As a child, I grew up mostly playing outside and have lived an active lifestyle all my life. My diet has never been good; I was raised on man-made foods especially when I came to California. At the age of 12, I got contacts and I would barely expose my eyes to natural sunlight. After the age of 17, I became a gym rat and followed the calories in calories out bullshit till the age of 22.

I always had trouble focusing in school even as a little kid up until high school. In college, things got a bit better because I started to practice fasting obsessively which lowered a lot of inflammation inside my body. But as things got a bit better I joined a fraternity and everything went downhill from there. I started to drink a ton, do drugs, stay up late into the night, and go to concerts/raves lit up with an unbelievable amount of artificial light with 1000’s of obedient idiots next to me. After going through all that for 3-4 years I did manage to graduate college but started to feel minor depression. I remember not feeling as happy as I once did and losing motivation.

I moved back home after graduating because I did not like my major. I slowly started to recover at home and found curiosity in life with the help of a mentor. I started to read a lot and discovered true happiness in learning about health. I started reading a lot of health books and started to slowly fix myself with diet. The diet part helped but I was still feeling minor depression at times. Once I found Dr. Kruse’s work about a year ago that all changed. My minor depression completely went away and I started preforming better in everything. The biggest change was my thinking, it got extremely better.

As of now, my diet is high in seafood, I drink non-fluoridated water, and my circadian rhythm is strong (I can tell by my morning shits J). I eat seasonally and try to source my foods as best as I can since California has a big fluoride problem. My biggest problem is EMFs in my environment (I live right next to a utility pole). I have been testing my 25(D), and it has NOT gone up with constant sun exposure in the spring and summer time. I’m guessing this is because of the topological effect of EMFs on my skin. I’ve recently been going to the lake near me to get my sun exposure and see if that would be enough to change my 25(D) status (going to retest in a few weeks here).

Other than that, I lift 3 times per week for about 25 mins (outdoor in the sun), sleep 9-10 hours every day on the magnetico, and have good energy levels throughout the day.

**What I Do Now:**

I currently started working as a realtor because it gives me autonomy in my schedule. I don’t have to be stuck in the office, and I can work from home (so I can get sun the majority of the day). Plus, it’s not extremely busy work - so it lets me really focus in on learning more about health and everything Dr. Kruse is teaching.

I have an accounting background, so it was hard for me to understand what Dr. Kruse was teaching at first. However, I’ve started buying and learning the fundamentals from health textbooks, which has helped a lot with understanding the blogs and webinars. I am currently reading about Molecular Biology, Biochemistry, Photochemistry, and Endocrinology.

I’m careful about not getting sucked into the dogma that is taught in some of these textbooks (e.g. Peter Mitchells theory). It definitely helps reading the blogs along with the textbooks to have some contrast and similarities to better assimilate the information.

**The plan going forward:**

My girlfriend lives in San Jose, CA and she deals with anxiety problems. She has gotten better through natural calcium channel blockers, such as magnesium and CBD (but not completely). She’s planning on moving to where I live to get away from the massive amount of EMFs in the big city.

Both of us are currently saving money to move to a location where we can be protected from 5G/4G/LTC, especially now with the news that 4G is enough to break the weak covalent bond of melanopsin and retinol.

**My Reasons for becoming a Member:**

One of the things that have changed my life is developing curiosity. I’ve realized that to get where you need to go in life, you need have curiosity along with amazing mentors. Discovering Dr. Kruse has definitely pushed my curiosity to the next level and I consider being part of his tribe an amazing mentorship program.

My passion in life is health, my dopamine goes nuts learning this stuff and applying it in my life. However, I haven’t figured out how I want to contribute to my passion yet, and how I can really make a difference. I guess that’s why I’m here - to become a student of the game and learn everything I possibly can until I know where I can fit in and make a difference. This quantum health movement really resonates with me and makes me want to take the road less traveled on.

I really like the fact that Dr. Kruse sells education as service instead of selling people supplements along with half-truths. I like the fact his online business helps so many people around the world every day and gives them the power to take control of their own health. This is definitely something I want to emulate; I want to create a business in health that ACTUALLY helps people.

Anyways, I am grateful to be here and to be part of such an amazing community of people. I’m looking forward to interacting with you guys online and in person sometime soon.

Cheers

# Thread: Space mimics a 5G world.......so begin thinking about collateral effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-06T21:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/post-252513>

Astronaut Thomas Pesquet unknowingly describes the circadian disaster that is space travel. We're continually sprinting toward the future without understanding the past and present. Don't assume anything new from a technology standpoint is progress.

[Thomas Pesquet](https://www.facebook.com/ESAThomasPesquet/?__tn__=kCH-R&eid=ARB9ulJdJ0RvvFLRB0KJUqvdActYoWxEWq54vORWl9aSMb_ILgs9aCoZAfHSdHmgP0sPFMcIg6BeCBaO&hc_ref=ARR-KrVOL6SUUjVJTMq0-NeTc-iB_i773rH8e8ZEpMBVpGAkxc96k7f9JCRl-Yh44eA&fref=nf&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARA2NCQn9SQnEDxrhaCk5UUZgswbHRz7kZbBY84_w6GGxWPmkGQ_S_sbru76ChAdYLa_csZqNgKk8V-FrKN4cBHvNHhA0ISX29OD3xPPknGu9J11RM1jhy1wKJ2ayExIbOZ4F5eq2RuP7rgFmQ5obtdl-_A3UPUDCGAPUjHE9Dg4MMqO14P1SxYztScj-0tG6uuhMbNE6sT_vg77_t7iREfrphvUozHr7mXAYQc)
[December 5, 2016](https://www.facebook.com/ESAThomasPesquet/photos/a.181446708731477/538318003044344/?type=3&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARA2NCQn9SQnEDxrhaCk5UUZgswbHRz7kZbBY84_w6GGxWPmkGQ_S_sbru76ChAdYLa_csZqNgKk8V-FrKN4cBHvNHhA0ISX29OD3xPPknGu9J11RM1jhy1wKJ2ayExIbOZ4F5eq2RuP7rgFmQ5obtdl-_A3UPUDCGAPUjHE9Dg4MMqO14P1SxYztScj-0tG6uuhMbNE6sT_vg77_t7iREfrphvUozHr7mXAYQc&__tn__=H-R) ·

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Sunrises. We experience 16 sunrises every 24 hours on the International Space Station as it takes us 90 minutes to do a complete orbit of our planet flying at 28 800 km/h. Of course, we don't notice most of the sunrises as we are working inside, but every now and again I can take a picture. The sunrises there are filled all parts of the electromagnetic spectrum and not similar to terrestrial sunlight. Why don't we realize this might be why space sickness exists?

Images of sunrises as we live from the space station. 24 hours, we can see 16! At 28 km / H, we need only 90 minutes to complete a full orbit around the earth. With our busy schedule, we don't have the opportunity to admire all of them, but I still have the pleasure of capturing one of those sunrises from the sun once in a while. And so far I don't get tired of it!

[https://flic.kr/p/PTopJn](https://flic.kr/p/PTopJn)

### Katie Durham — 2019-02-13T18:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/post-263097>

It concerns me that the 50th anniversary of the great myth is coming up and so few understand we were hoodwinked. The ever paranoid Nixon needed a PR victory in the space war against Russia. The only way to win the war was to fake it.

Of the many documentaries over the years, my favorite is Massimo Mazzucco’s recent “American Moon” that details without question that all the NASA footage we have ever seen of the Apollo missions was staged, and poorly.

I know from a very close friend who was a primary investigator on NASA’s Mars missions concurrent with Apollo that the Apollo astronauts, as officers of the military, were given no choice but to go along. Also that we never had the technology to get back from the moon or to survive the Van Allen radiation belt. There is no reason to believe that a manned launch has ever left low earth orbit. It has set us back on our race to visit Mars since we are lacking a great deal of space experience that was assumed to have been gained.

But I don’t doubt that as July approaches the US will once again gloat in its great accomplishment of 1969. This time in blue light technology so all will be lulled back to sleep.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-02-14T18:32:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/post-263178>

I don't doubt the Apollo moon missions are real (they have since photographed remains of Apollo landing sites including lower stage of lunar landing craft) but all this hoopla about _Musk Manned Mars Missions_ is crazy.

As Dr Kruse has pointed out, radiation exposures in space (like cosmic rays) are off the charts. If they survive the journey there, living on Mars for any time period, even if under surface to escape most radiation, is an invitation to disaster.

It will make good press for a while until volunteer Mars space pioneers all perish from one reason or another. I would call it intentional suicide in exhange for a little glory and making a historical footnote.

### Katie Durham — 2019-02-14T20:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/post-263187>

There have been unmanned flights that would have left debris and could have retrieved moon rocks. But everything NASA showed us in film footage was staged and many have proven that over the years. Which means NASA has never come clean with us as to what Apollo did or did not involve. I think it goes to JK's caution to periodically re-evaluate what it is we really know. On this matter we can re-evaluate by looking at the evidence so many have carefully laid out for us. There are enough dangers in space. It horrifies me that people are even more likely to die, based on fabrications about what we claim to know about space travel simply because we're too arrogant to admit that failure during the Cold War era 50 years ago was not an option.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-02-15T18:49:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-mimics-a-5g-world-so-begin-thinking-about-collateral-effects.22247/post-263277>

Many people subscribe to conspiracy theory that Apollo moon landings were a fraud and fake. Nothing wrong in that.

The way I understand it, a main reason we went to trouble and expense of landing men on moon was to study the Sun from geological artifacts on moon.

They were looking for evidence that our Sun has routinely acted up (about every 12 thousand years it is believed) and suffered a **micronova** (not a nova where a star explodes and destroys itself).

When Sun does this it causes cataclysmic upheavals on earth (hence the many legends from prehistory about Great Floods and such)

Specifically Apollo scientists were looking for evidence like glass beads on the moon. They found them.

According to theorist Doug Vogt, we are due for another micronova around 2046, according to the extrapolated cycle.

There is much that is not explained by conventional science. Modern day scientists may preen in conceit of their greatness but they are mental midgets when it comes to Truth.

### Green Eyes — 2018-10-07T21:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252578>

Would appreciate feedback on my newbie understanding. As I see it, Sunlight is good. There is no substitute for it. That said, my job as a nurse requires me to be at work before the sunrises during Winter. I barely get a chance to pee during the day, let alone sneak outside for Sunlight. While I am working on changing my life, would getting up earlier and sitting in front of a Solor glo lamp for 30 min be of any help or just a waste of time. I do avoid lights after sunset and wear blue blockers at work. Thank all of you, in advance, for pointing me in a helpful direction.

### drezy — 2018-10-07T22:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252580>

### Green Eyes — 2018-10-08T00:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252584>

I work 10 hrs shifts, so generally I'm not able to catch sunsets during Winter. I do get Sunrise and Sunsets the three days of the week that I am off. I plan to pull off the road and catch sunrises during my drive time to work, once Summer returns. I was thinking using the Solar glo lamp on Winter mornings would be of some benefit. Trying to put this into practice in my personal life.

### Ted — 2018-10-08T01:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252587>

Solar glo has UVA and UVB I believe. Those frequencies are not out at sunrise so you don't want to be looking at that light. If you need artificial lights, then an incandescent is your best bet.

### Green Eyes — 2018-10-08T01:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-hacking-sunlight.22251/post-252588>

discourages people from drinking DDW-25 only
for the reason that in case that latter on they would develop cancer he would not have a ways of helping them.

...........
About eating and drinking when thinking of deuterium.
I was not clear when thinking of DDW and that video got this cleared for me.
We run on matrix water (the fact that joining H & O creates energy is incidental) most energy comes from sun anyhow.
1.--So, we, (our biology), runs on matrix water.
2.--The water that we drink newer mixes with matrix water
3.--If we want to keep running we have to eat fat (drinking water is a side story, biologically).

....
\=========================================
one more

I am not clear about what dr Boros said when he said that he have a high ADH (AntiDiureticHormone).

1- does drinking minimal amounts of water makes his ADH go up
or
2- he have a high ADH so he is able to drink minimal amounts of water
\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
About ADH
I have my own story.
ADH=vassopressin
Desmopressin Acetate=synthetic ADH
is sold as 10 mcg/(0.1mL)
that is to large amount of ADH for anything I may need (I found)
but
when I mixed it with sterile water to get
**0.83 mcg or 1.66 mcg**
To be similar to a drug that is about to be produced
**Noctiva (desmopressin acetate) Nasal Spray for Nocturia in Adults**
I got interesting results.

The story of ADH may be continued, if [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) or dr Boros decide to talk about it.

Now we are cooking with water or Olive oil! We "argue"well together. I have also put ADH on my list. My reason being that I read a paper that links dopamin,leptin and lack of thirst. Obesity is tied to a lack of thirst for water. Coke goes down by the gallon,water even makes some people retch. The first thought would be deuterium.

I believe that we run on matrix water. How much external water we need I do not know.
Remember the limits of surviving without water. Is that the size of our camel's hump? Why do we not crave a piece of pork belly when dehydrated to delirium point but an ice cold beer, or dream of oceans? Febrile imagination ?

I have been thinking about the Tarahumara for a long time. However my question is whether we can flip the switch just like that or if there is a Piper to be paid. After all if we were prepared to live like the Tarahumara or Hadza we would have said goodbye a long time ago? We need more info than this video.
I second your call for Dr Kruse to Wade in.

Edit: You have another issue with olive oil. Your source and it's Shelf/ storage life. I can get olive oil from Mendoza San Juan or Catamarca from this year. If your olive oil needs to be imported you have little control of freshness as you only get the expiry date but not the bottling date.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-09T04:53:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-4-hour-podcast-with-laszlo-boros-last-week.22255/post-252684>

This won't be popular but it is true from my point of view and I think JanSz you need to hear it and consider it.

**MARKETING vs LEADERSHIP:** You can have everything in life you want if you will just help enough other people get what they want. Q has this mindset down well. The real leader makes sure before they allow any person to buy anything, they attempt to get them focused on what really matters first. A real leader wants them to eliminate the unecessary and focus upon the extraordinary so that what “they want” is something that really can advance their life. That is leadership that brings true value to those who do not know any better. Often times, leaders will lead others not where they initially think they want to go, but they will guide them to where they ought to be. The marketing leader will jut lead them to what they want with no concern of efficacy or effectiveness for their ultimate goals. Short term marketers always look good. But the game of life is not about quick benefits. It is about sustained success.

Great marketers will take people as far as they will go, not as far as people really should go because it would limit the profits. Great leaders won’t let people settle for less. Nothing liberates your greatness like the true desire to help, the desire to serve mankind. They may not like the manner in which you do it initially, but when they see the value you can bring to masses of people they will respect you. Leaders never look for followers. Look for future change agents.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I am really glad that you have decided to dig out and publish so much science and more important to connect the dots.
There is nobody else having this type of mind set (and willing to share).
The way I look at you Jack, and dr Boros.
You have given us the three legged stool,
and
about last November brought dr Boros to the picture.
I knew about dr Boros previously, but without you I was not able to appreciate his value.
Dr Boros is not Black Swan Mitochondriac you would wish he would become.
But dr Boros is a really big helper with the leg responsible for the water.
There is still more about deuterium, I am sure you will let us know about, in time.

I am drinking DDW for 303 days as of today.
And was thinking why I should worry that much about eating keto.
I think I got my answer.

As it is now,
this 4 hour podcast with Laszlo Boros
make me realize, or rather suspect, that we may not need that much DDW to drink, possibly we do not need it at all.
We run on water, matrix water (105ppm D/H).
Matrix water, eventually become water in cytosol, latter extra cellular and then is peed out.
In managing water we are not as efficient as camel, scorpion or snake,
so we need to drink some water.
But we are drinking too much.
By drinking less many currently obese people could balance their weight easier.
Drinking less should raise ADH and save many night trips to the bathroom, improving on sleep.

Drinking less would increase matrix water's (105ppm) participation in overall body's water balance, that means overall lowering of deuterium ppm in the body.

I hope, you, would expand on that (practical) point of view.

....

this one?

A donkey and a camel were walking along together.
The camel moved with long strides and the donkey
moved impatiently, stumbling every now and then.
At last the donkey said to his companion, 'How is it
that I am always in trouble, falling and grazing my legs,
in spite of the fact that I look carefully downwards as
I walk; When you, who never seem to be aware of what
surround you, with your eyes fixed upon the horizon,
keep going so fast and yet seemingly at such leisure?'
The camel answered: 'Your problem is that your
steps are too short and by the time you have seen something
it is too late to correct your movements. You look all around
and do not assess what you see. You think that haste is speed;
you imagine that by looking you can see; You think that seeing
near is the same as seeing far.
You guess that I look at the horizon. In fact, I am
merely gazing ahead so as to work out what to do when the
far becomes near. I also remember what has gone before,
and do not need to look back at it and stumble once again.
In this way what seems to you baffling or difficult becomes
clear and easy.'
Similarly, donkey-minded people are those who
believe that, they can learn enough to improve themselves by
short sighted means. This includes not looking towards the
future or even into it. It includes demanding a certain pace without
realizing whether it is counter-productive or not......

### drezy — 2018-10-09T22:57:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-4-hour-podcast-with-laszlo-boros-last-week.22255/post-252766>

This one?

Once upon a time in a small village called Kuvuki land, there lived two good friends, Camel and Donkey. They lived happily, helped and cared for each other and also met every night to share stories of their great village.

When Donkey would fall sick, Camel would care for him by cooking and looking after his garden and also travel far away to look for medicines that would cure him.

One day, Camel also fell sick. He could not leave his house, cook or even look after his garden. So he suffered with hunger alone in the house, his garden also dying slowly.

Donkey knew his best friend was sick but did not care to visit him or look for the medicines that would cure him. But luckily after some time, Camel gained some strength and got out of the house.

Immediately, Camel went to Donkey and asked why he did not visit him when he was sick and Donkey arrogantly replied that he was too busy with his own garden and did not have the time to visit him.

Camel reminded donkey how he cared for him when he was sick and angrily told him the he is not a good friend. From that day, they have never been friends again.

Moral of the story, care, love and above all, be there for your friends when they actually need you, because you will lose them and it’s no fun having no friends.

From Balanced nation founder Dr. Beck after he listened to this 4 hour "podcast":

And I quote: [Anthony G. Beck](https://www.facebook.com/anthonygbeck?fref=ufi) said, "Susan Yu Also...and this is not meant in any mean spirit and only for the principle of fact....Dr. Que Collins is obese.

Which is ironic that he holds himself out by his own words... Keto expert.

Furthermore on a few things he says in that linked video.

1\. fat is naturally deuterium depleted. NO..it's not....it's naturally WATER depleted which of course means not containing deuterium. It has ZERO to do with any relation to eating fat from a grass-fed animal. That was sheer talking out his butt.

2\. Deuterium is NOT going to stop UV acting on 7DH Cholesterol from making solar pro-hormones in the human body. That is false.

3\. His little "assay" to measure deuterium in saliva, urine, etc....is bullshit. It tells NOTHING of deuterium status or function in the patient. Whereas, the body dumps deuterium...that is what it does. It would not extrapolate to any volume correlation in the body.

4\. And IF THAT wasn't enough B.S.....his little "breathy" is nonsense for the same reason.

Deep underwater in the dark that little "lantern" dangles in front of the anglerfish's mouth and is lights up\*. Prey and even other predators big enough to eat the anglerfish can be fooled, thanks to lack of perception and curiosity, into thinking they have an advantage. Reduce your own perception by squinting a bit and you'll see that this looks like a fox's face. If you were a smarter predator but didn't have proper perception, this "fox" would be way out of your choice of prey.

My low LH is likely non-pathologic. I solved that riddle a few years back. I realised I missed the most important factor in the HPTA and all hormonal systems. The hypothalamus releases a pulse of GnRH -> stimulates pituitary to release LH/FSH -> stimulates the testes to produce T and that results in negative feedback regulating the axis. But JanSz, what gets GnRH going in the first place? What signals the hypothalamus? It is the SCN which receives the light input. That is how AM sunlight turns on the pituitary to release LH/FSH. This is how light interacts with matter to release some of its energy and that energy is embedded in hormones.

I live at the 45th latitude. It is cold, and it is dark. The light signal is not strong. But cold increases the magnetic effect. Electrons are falling to the ground state as my surfaces are cooled. Hormones are binding tighter to their receptors while releasing some energy. There's not going to be very many hormones flowing through my blood because of this.

I think you should re-read the CT blogs as they will change your life and your perspective on hormone levels and labs. We cannot measure how efficient we are at activating the receptor other than looking at symptoms. The fact that my T levels can remain at the bottom of the range with low LH/FSH and I am asymptomatic is enough proof that my androgen receptors are active. More evidence was conceiving my son, and having the ability to retain optimal body composition and sleeping sound every night.

EDIT: the last bit of evidence for me was when I abandoned ketosis in February 2017 and decided to try it Ray Peat's way: tons of carbs/fructose, low seafood, quit CT and decreased water intake. The results tripled my T levels and yet I felt worse. I believe I induced leptin resistance, and decreased my magnetic sense so my receptors became resistant, leaving too much T in my blood.

> -   Promoting muscle mass and strength
> -   Increasing bone density and strength
> -   Stimulating growth and maturation of bones
> -   Promoting growth of body hair and facial hair
> -   Encouraging sexual arousal and libido
> -   Regulating mood by modulating GABA receptors
>
> ****Symptoms of low testosterone activity in the body include fatigue, low mood, apathy, and loss of strength.****
> But it's not just about the amount of testosterone in the blood that matters! For testosterone to exert any effect on the body, it must first bind to an androgen receptor inside a cell.
>
> **When testosterone enters a target cell, three things can happen**
> **#1.** Testosterone attaches to an androgen receptor and causes a moderate androgenic effect on the cell.
>
> **#2.** An enzyme called 5-a-reductase converts testosterone into DHT, an androgen hormone that is 10x more powerful than testosterone. DHT then attaches to the androgen receptor and causes a **huge** androgenic effect on the cell.
>
> **#3.** An enzyme called aromatase converts testosterone into oestrogen, which then attaches to a different receptor and exerts oestrogen effects on the cell – kind of the opposite of androgen effects.
>
> No matter how high the circulating testosterone levels are, if the body's androgen receptors are sleeping on the job, then #3 will _always_ happen! This results in a _relative_ androgen insufficiency – a much higher level of oestrogenic activity than androgenic activity in the body.
>
> **There might be plenty of testosterone in the blood but without active receptors, the symptoms of an androgen insufficiency may still occur:**
>
>
> -   Increased body fat around abdomen
> -   Fatigue
> -   Reduced libido
> -   Loss of body hair
> -   Hot flushes and sweating
> -   Lethargy
> -   Depression
> -   Increased risk of insulin deficiency
> -   Weaker bones and increased risk of osteoporosis
>
> **There's good news! You can take care of your androgen receptors and even encourage your body to create _more_ of them!**
>
> The sensitivity and number of androgen receptors can be affected by nutrition, exercise and healthy habits – as well as pollutants, poor health, and the wrong food choices.
>
> [https://www.superpharmacy.com.au/blog/boosting-androgen-receptors](https://www.superpharmacy.com.au/blog/boosting-androgen-receptors)

Then they go on to say that intermittent fasting & weight training improve androgen receptor sensitivity (both of which I've been doing for years). They do not mention that CT does it as well (also a habit I've adopted for years).

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) explain why I have 0 symptoms, and no issues with fertility? I have 10% bodyfat, normal/low blood sugar and triglycerides, good bone density, strong beard growth, etc. I'll admit, yes, HCG was a boost, but so was anabolic steroids at ridiculous doses back in my early 20s. Any man will feel a boost from flooding the body with androgens temporarily, but I do not need such a boost.

### Katie Durham — 2019-03-09T22:18:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-4-hour-podcast-with-laszlo-boros-last-week.22255/post-264940>

> [Dean6789 said:](/goto/post?id=264934)
>
> I've been hearing a lot lately about olive oil (and coconut oil) oxidizing easily and turning to trans fat... and destroying the body.

What potentially oxidizes are the unsaturated bonds. Coconut oil is saturated fat and should not oxidize. Olive oil is primarily monosaturated and will not be prone to oxidizing as much as a polyunsaturated fat.

### Ossa — 2018-10-08T07:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-interferes-with-light-frequencies.22256/post-252602>

I heard Jack in a podcast say that living in a city can be bad because emfs can cancel out UV frequencies from the sun. Im just wondering how big of an issue this is. Can you still get vitamin D and absorb UV through your eye at all if living in a city? Is it just less or are you getting none at all?

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-09T01:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-interferes-with-light-frequencies.22256/post-252671>

### Jonathin — 2018-10-10T20:27:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-interferes-with-light-frequencies.22256/post-252820>

It seems that it is not an all or nothing deal. There is probably subtle qualities to the uv that you receive if you are not in a good environment. The concept of intermodulation distortion comes to mind.
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intermodulation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intermodulation)
The body's response does not lie.
