### Jenlou — 2019-06-12T13:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-272496>

Update:

**Deuterium**

So I bit the bullet and got my Deuterium levels tested at the Centre for Deuterium Depletion
\- intial test levels (early March 2019):
Breath 150.3PPM
Saliva: 153.1PPM
Depletion factor: 1.8% (ideal is 7.5% and above)
ATP production factor: 10.8 (ideal range is 50-70)

..so pretty grim (as expected! ). Dr Q advised I go for more blood work at my GP's to look for cancer markers. This has been ruled out.

Advised to drink 25PPM DDW + deuterium depleting breathing mask and high fat diet, which i've been doing for 2.5 months now
due to retake the Deuterium level test in early July.

**Ketosis**

Caught a virus early January which took me 6 weeks to get back to my baseline health levels and threw me out of ketosis - have found it impossible to get back in - attempted until end of April but was concerned with the amount of weight gain (2 stone in 4 months), so am now on a LCHF diet and the addition of some carbohydrates seems to be suiting me better. I do think the pyschological stress of our situation / dealing with these symptoms and trying to implement and maintain a keto diet whilst remaining bedbound didn't help - too much adrenaline / cortisol maybe.

**Progress / Changes**

\- Have moved from bed-bound to mostly housebound again. I feel like the DDW is giving me an edge.
\- I dropped a load of supplements but did add in ActivAMP - which I also feel is helping
\- recovering much quicker from crashes (post exertional malaise)
\- continuing with Red Light Therapy twice daily and as much sunlight, esp early morning as possible
\- Somatic movement / feldenkraise / gentle yoga daily - are helping my stress levels considerably
\- noise tolerance is a little improved
\- 'brain' tinnitus is less extreme
\- some episodes of extremly high inflammation / high histamine (slump phases) which affect my sleep (wired at night) but these do not last a long (2-6 days now, rather than permanently being in those states with hardly little relief)
\- still feel very knocked out in the mornings
\- aiming for 2 saunas a week and am tolerating 20 mins well now, with a cold shower afterwards
\- tolerating cold water in general better - I eased off and built up slowly with the face-dunks and shower temperatures
. Hormones still a nightmare - cramping and back pain from around day 10 through to my period at day 29 - although for the past two cycles, I've felt much less 'stressed' during this phase. Still have episodes of overwhelming depression / low motivation and feelings that I cannot take it anynire - but they are definately reduced somewhat.
\- cognitive symptoms still poor - confusion / forgetfulness / memory
\- still need to look at SNPs and SAPs but struggling too much cognitavly to make any sense of what I do come across
\- increase in other brain symptoms - vision is often fuzzy / wobbly and i'm having strange tingling / tickly sensations shoot through my brain
\- unable to tolerate hardly any screen time ..double-edged sword really .. body can't cope with it, mind wants to as I signed up to Jack's patreon, but am unable to read it
\- big increase in feeling 'hungover' ovee the past 3-4 weeks
\- huge amounts of gas and wind in the afternoons / evenings for the past 5 weeks - am considering some digestive enzymes

### Citizen Scientist — 2018-10-13T19:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d2o-heavy-water.22282/post-253069>

My experience is with John Ellis is that he will do or say anything to get your money. But that's how capitalism works. The machine is actually a distiller. It does distill water. That part works.

According to HYD LLC, the Hungarian company that exports Preventa to the US, when you distill water, you remove 1 ppm of Deuterium. When you double-distill water, you remove an additional 2 or 3 ppm.

Laboratories sell Double Distilled Water for various scientific testing purposes. If that water starts at 150 ppm, then it would be 146 or 147 ppm once processed.

If you had the patience to distill the same water a hundred times in a row, what would the results be?

# Thread: Diagnosing RF increase - 5G or something else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew H — 2018-10-11T22:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252925>

Recently I have been feeling horrible EMF symptoms and general inflammation. Lo and behold, RF levels in my apartment are now 0.05-0.2 V/m. Just several weeks ago they peaked at 0.05 V/m. Measurements were taken with a Cornet ED88T Plus meter. I am trying to figure out the source.

So I am in Houston and 5G rollout from Verizon began on Oct 1. But according to Verizon's [site](https://www.verizonwireless.com/5g/home/), my address does not yet have 5G service available. The rollout was only in certain neighborhoods, though Verizon is not explicitly disclosing which ones.

Given the timing, I suspect 5G. But the fact that service isn't available in my neighborhood, plus the fact that my bedroom Faraday cage has held up well (I'm very sensitive to EMFs during sleep but still sleep great) makes me wonder if there's another source of general RF causing the radiation increase, rather than 5G specifically.

Maybe a new cell tower, though according to Antenna Search the nearest ones are just under 1 km away and I do not see any new ones pop up super close to me compared to a few weeks ago. Plus there is a construction zone across the street, with lots of equipment like cranes, etc. The crane is one of those super tall ones made for building high-rise buildings in cities.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T02:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252946>

Ditto here.

I've noticed a significant uptick in measured microwatts/sq meter where I live, even though 5G hasn't officially launched here.

Downtown area where I used to work was measured yesterday at well over 20,000 microwatts/sq meter and off the meter scale at over 6 V/m. Unbelievable.

My RF meter (Acoustimeter), although a very fine piece of equipment for low end of RF meters, doesn't have a direction finder capability to detect signal strength changes with direction accurately (some of more expensive consumer products have this valuable capability).

**I strongly suspect there is some stealth testing/trials of advanced 4G or incipient 5G networks going on, with higher frequencies and more bandwidth.**

Remember, not all telecom antennas are placed on conventional cell towers (masts). Some are mounted as panels on taller buildings (sometimes disguised, usually not). They can be masquerading as faux rocks or trees (supposedly for 'aesthetic' reasons in some communities). They are known to be hidden inside church steeples too (steeple and faux objects, e.g. fake vegetation are constructed with plastic type material transparent to RF signals).

### Andrew H — 2018-10-12T04:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252949>

Aha, now there is a possibility!

The question is - how do I shield it? If 4G, can use standard methods. If 5G, well... the one company that's gotten back to me about 5G shielding basically said oh we'll just have to wait and see. But I'm not the type to wait and see, while I'm in pain at the moment.

I'm contemplating putting a naturelle Faraday cage over my desk with a shielding ground mat underneath, since that seems to work well for my bed.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T18:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252976>

I don't know if 5G mm wave forms will penetrate shielding cloth in common use now (you know, the silver impregnated cloth fabric people are using as canopies for their bed as a sleep sanctuary, etc.). I strongly suspect that small waves can easily slip through these mesh fabric apertures.

Anyway, the true 5G mm wave beams are coming later, according to my information. It seems that 5G networks that are launching all the time now are souped up 4G RF with higher frequencies and more power density. That is my take, anyway.

In any case, 5G RF, like 4G, will still be attenuated (greatly reduced) for the most part by solid metallic shielding and dense material like concrete.

I am extremely concerned about people building Faraday cage type enclosures for protection. Unless you're sure no RF is leaking in through any cracks or seams you run the risk of creating a mini microwave oven. RF beams that slip in could ricochet around the enclosure until they are absorbed by your body. Please be careful.

### drezy — 2018-10-12T19:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252981>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=252976)
>
> I am extremely concerned about people building Faraday cage type enclosures for protection. Unless you're sure no RF is leaking in through any cracks or seams you run the risk of creating a mini microwave oven. RF beams that slip in could ricochet around the enclosure until they are absorbed by your body. Please be careful.

^^ And aint that the crux of it.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-13T18:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-253064>

Another factor to keep in mind is that in a _5G_ world we have another worry about mw RF shielding-- **jump conduction**.

Dr Kruse has mentioned this 5G phenomenon numerous times. From way he discusses it I assume jump conduction is when an electrical current from 5G wireless radiation is capable of flowing through air to a conductive material or a ground--- depending on circumstances it may electric shock.

That is why he recommends against clothing designed for EMF shielding that might facilitate jump conduction in a 5G environment. I would assume the same applies to Faraday cage solutions.

He has hinted of some serious design work he is engaged in to develop shelter solutions in 5G infested environment.

He also recommends selling your current house soon and building one of these safe houses when they are commercially available.

As a personal side note, perhaps you should sell your house relatively soon. The global housing bubble of recent years (notable in Canada, Australia, and USA) is showing signs of rolling over now-- with house prices likely to continue to deflate. Best to sell near the peak of bubble and put proceeds to good use.

# Thread: Shielding EL Car Radar 24-79 Ghz (5G Range)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anders Sagevik — 2018-10-12T00:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252931>

What would work most effective shielding Radar Frequency
from 24-79 Ghz.

The Yshield Carbon Based Paint (Tested between 1-18 Ghz)
VS Solid Lead plates ?

I am a EMF sensitive person.
My neighbours have just bought a two EL cars.
When it is parked close to my home I get very dizzy and EMF symptoms. I have checked all sources of EMF with my meter. It is not Wifi/Blueetotth/4G etc.
It is not any Low LF or magnetic fields either.
It is also not the charger that makes dirty EMF on the power grid, I hve checked all those.

A engineer friend told me that EL cars have Radar on both front and back.He told me that the frequencies of radars like this is in the high Ghz. Here is the specific info about radar in EL cars:

Reverse Driving sensor and parking assistance uses frequencies in the 24 GHz area and has a outgoing start effect at 100 milliWatt'

Cruisekontrol and distance sensor uses frequencies from 76-79 GHz and have max outgoing effect on 300 Watt average effect on 0,2 Watt..

I am trying to understand this logically,, the car is off but still makes me dizzy. The symptoms only arrives when the car is parked within about 10 meters from me, when it is over 10 meters and "out of sight" behind a wall or something, there is no issue.

I know that if the EMF wave is in the high Ghz it is a very intense and short wave, that should be rather easy to shield, and should get weaker on distance.

So if this sources is some sort of radar in different settings, the freqs would be 24Ghz and 76-79 Ghz.

I know there is some shielding paint from Yshield that gives 99.9% at 1Ghz. I asked the owner there, and he said it is only measured up to 18Ghz sources, but the painting should also reduce all freq above 18 as well. Would you think that both 24Ghz and 76-79 would be “short enough” waves to be stopped with a 30 cm thick concrete wall + a layer of the shielding paint?

The EL engineer I talked with told me that he thought that when 5G arrives with freq from 20-80 Ghz, concrete would not be enough to shield, he said it needed to be metall enforced in the concrete.I know they use solid Lead plates in hospitals to shield rooms from X-rays etc.

So I wonder if you think Lead plates would be more effective shielding in combo with the concrete rather than the paint, or if the paint also acts like metall and would do the same job?

### OptimalSkr — 2018-10-12T15:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252963>

Could be a charging station for the car near the house??? does and EL car mean "electric"???? Might be they are plugging it in even when its "off"

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T18:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-el-car-radar-24-79-ghz-5g-range.22287/post-252977>

You didn't mention gauss readings for electromagnetic ELF exposure near the cars, so I don't know how you managed to rule that out too.

Howdy, all. Citizen Scientist here. (Not my real name.) Located in FEMA Region IV, the heartland of American Deuterium. Nor is it the ideal altitude or latitude.

If we ever meet in person in a Mexico or a Vermont, you'll find I'm the nicest person. But here, on this forum, you'll find me focused on the grim pursuit of eternal life.

We're all prematurely-aged these days, and I am starting from square one. No SNPs for me. (Do you know _who_ started 23andMe? Do you know _why_ they started 23andMe? The more you know.) Which goes double for Facebook.

This group of people on this forum is unique. You [starfish](https://www.urbandictionary.com/define.php?term=starfish) are determined to survive, to thrive, to optimize. And we're here to help each other. I've seen the opposite in the world of commercial health, where those people won't help but will charge. But while I'm here, I'm here to help you.

Science should be in the hands of the citizens. No individual, who just wants to live, should be held hostage by supplement makers, charlatans, lunatics, podcasters, doctors, and device-makers who, somehow, need a ten-thousand-percent mark-up. If you can do-it-yourself, do. Knowledge is power. (Didn't Jack once say, _"Ignorance is a burden to others, and a prison for oneself."_)

So I'm here to learn, challenge myself, and optimise.

Most interested in the discussion around deuterium depletion for water right now.

But wanted to introduce myself first.

# Thread: KREBS BICYCLE.........What is it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krebs-bicycle-what-is-it.22292/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-13T15:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krebs-bicycle-what-is-it.22292/post-253027>

What is Kreb's bicycle?

It is where the TCA and urea cycle meet in your mitochondria. "Krebs bicycle," composed of the urea cycle below, which meshes with the aspartate-argininosuccinate shunt of the citric acid cycle on the left. They meet at the enzyme fumerase which adds water to both cycles. The water it adds is also quite special because the water added comes from the mitochondrial matrix and not from the extracellular fluid compartment. Fumarate produced in the cytosol by argininosuccinate lyase of the urea cycle can enter the citric acid cycle in the matrix of the mitochondrion and is converted in several steps to oxaloacetate. This is how these two cycles operate in unison.

When the two wheels of this bicycle do not rotate freely we get collateral effects in other cycles. One of those areas of collateral damage is in the methionine cycle which controls the flux of homocysteine and the sulfur laced amino acids that make up the major anions of our blood that interact with sunlight. One oxaloacetate accepts an amino group from glutamate by transamination, and the aspartate thus formed leaves the mitochondrion and donates its amino group (nitrogen cycle) to the urea cycle in the argininosuccinate synthetase reaction. Intermediates in the citric acid cycle are boxed in the picture below. When this process is broken reactive nitrogen signal is awry in a cell and it leads to more mitochondrial damage. What breaks it? Anything that is capable of slowing the spin rate of the cycle or its substrates. Deuterium's kinetic isotope effect is the number one offender in illness states. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/18925873](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.patreon.com%2Fposts%2F18925873&h=AT0peAt-xEdGdPwf9kuEcY_-ywCGt6oLnlGxRXF_suBBHAJciUOFkAvM2YxLriMcMzOl-dgSQOfbNQjrjxkvmKGJuAy85CGmVfZeGDbADKJMN9Q44myr7WxDe3l1dnnZeChHd2ra95NGbUIt9Aw5evSexQMLutIKdVc6wphYTE3ELRL2u53Em2os_m2nfao9CygXM2EVasFPV5EqamkAouj9Uxtpn29QwJbZIHAY9ODQM1Tv3IH_bDjJOEiGUstMAe1i0Jcz3t9U5XUJwkRxUG1pneuQKGna_Tkdy44W2jd70ph2MhH_dYpuugAHx2P2pJRqfVrzNAqyHpndUCEG-2om4_O-vZZWWxx0jxf-pAoFyLuHa1WGTku44RWEXnfaXpM_gYIWRSXx8gyuxECycpmYim8nIPaME78)

Melanin is also adjacent to most mitochondria in most organs, which suggests its function with mtDNA UPEs and its own are powerful for some reason. Melanin is also paramagnetic and this makes it sensitive to the magnetic fields created by the spinning Fo head of the ATPase.

Melanin paramagnetism varies with its hydration status by the heme proteins such as CCO. Hydration Status and Paramagnetism: Melanin’s paramagnetism is hydration-dependent.

Water sorption shifts the comproportionation equilibrium (hydroxyquinone ↔ semiquinone ↔ DDW, with lower deuterium, alters this equilibrium differently than normal water. Deuterium affects hydrogen bonding and proton mobility, so DDW enhances proton release in melanin’s redox reactions, increasing semiquinone radical concentration (the primary paramagnetic species).

Studies on hydrated melanin show interfacial water layers (3–5 layers) dominate its structure, with possible proton cation (e.g., Zundel ion) signatures. DDW’s lower deuterium content should enhance these proton dynamics by the laws of physics, stabilizing or amplifying the EPR signal when studied. No one is studying this topic in biophysics yet.

**If they did we'd have more answers for AD and ALS. This is why the 20 yr failed amyloid paradigm has set us back in AD science. What are the implications of all these biophysics for demyelinating diseases, like MS, ALS, BPD, AD, and PD? Inquiring minds will find this blog fascinating.**

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-08-10T15:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis.22297/post-275792>

It's genetic so no "cure" - you need to receive the gene from both parents in order to express the disease but you are a carrier so all children you have need to be tested also - and unfortunately you can't eat any raw seafood because is often contaminated with the bacterium Vibrio Vulnificas that thrives on iron so can lead to limb amputation and death.

### Greg Weil — 2018-10-14T16:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-ohio-41-degrees-n.22302/post-253124>

I found the Nourish Vermont videos a week or so ago and Dr. Jack's insights have tied together 40+ years of seeking better health.

I comment to anyone who will listen that I believe in the future, Dr. Jack's focus on external versus internal sources of disease will become as widely accepted and quoted (and more accurate) than Hippocrates famous 2,500 year old “Let food be thy medicine and medicine be thy food.”. Hippocrates just didn't know what he meant by "food" was "light".

**I'm terrified that I had cataract surgery in 2015.** I don't recall a word from the ophthalmologist (or even any fine-print) that the implants would block anything, but here we are.

So my questions is, "now what?" **How do I get UV AB (and IR) into my eyes?
**
Greg

We are on a 5-year plan to retire in Princeville, Kauai. I guess I have to pray we survive northern latitude sunlight deprivation (and advent of 5G) until we can get to 22.2236 !

So followup question and especially if we can speed up our timetable; Am I still screwed unless I have these optical implants removed?

### l.i.am — 2018-11-15T22:34:23+0200

### caroline — 2018-11-16T04:53:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-ohio-41-degrees-n.22302/post-255735>

### Greg Weil — 2018-11-18T15:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-ohio-41-degrees-n.22302/post-255895>

Thanks!

I’m so happy I’ve found this site but am simply unable to move south yet. Knowing its almost futile, I still get out in the cold mornings barefoot on 1 foot and receive feeble amounts of natural spectrum. I note with interest that Jack’s son is working toward a degree that will allow him to engineer electrical devices that produce light spectrums at the proper time of day to mimic the sun. I’m already trying to do that myself (I’m sure much to Dr. Jack’s chagrin), fully understanding only Mother Nature can do it exactly right.

The secret to Québec-based Eaux Vives Water Inc.’s ESKA water lies 600 kilometres north of Montréal. Eaux Vives then bottles and distributes it across Québec, Ontario, and other parts of Canada

### Linz — 2018-10-22T17:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-content-in-canadian-maple-syrup.22306/post-253920>

Patrick,
Your maple syrup question has disrupted my plans for the day! I intended a quick forum catch up, and rather than an answer for you I have many more thoughts (and lots of missing connections).

I'm not sure if there is any relevance to D levels in maple trees but I learned last week in a chance conversation, that maple trees do not take up dangerous radioactive isotopes but many other tree species do in the same circumstances. So maple syrup is less affected by a nuclear leak than food from other trees!

Sycamore trees have become very toxic to horses in recent years (high levels of hypoglycin-A in some of the seeds is thought to be the cause of a fatal atypical myopathy) but the trees have been present on grazing land in the UK for many years. One of our insurers has called the rise in poisonings as 'meteoric'. Before today I had been wondering about circadian effects on the equine immune system making the animal less tolerant of the toxin and also the implications of changing deuterium levels in the grazing, and how wild animals respond. This morning I found a paper (attached below) about Ackee fruit which is used in West Africa and Jamaica. The unripe fruit has toxic levels of hypoglycin-A but this is transferred to the seeds as it ripens (and changed to hypoglycin B) so the fruit can only be eaten ripe with all the seeds removed. The paper then goes on to say the fruit is canned in brine for export but a lot of ackee arilli waste is generated. This substance has a high lipid content and the toxic part is water soluble so it can be safely processed into ackee aril oil for consumption. The changes to Hypoglycin in reproductive/growth parts and the way it can be reduced in lipid processing makes it look like a deuterium problem.

In Sycamores, hypoglycin-a levels in the seeds vary a lot between trees and also between individual seeds on the same tree. Yet another question!

![Free Radical Scavenging Capacity, Carotenoid Content, and NMR Characterization of Blighia sapida Aril Oil](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/jl/2018/1762342/)

### jeff taylor — 2018-10-14T22:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.22307/post-253175>

Hi all,

I have been following Dr. Jack's work for the last 2 years or so. It is totally fascinating, and I have been greatly enjoying the process of implementing the lifestyle as best I can into my life. I am fortunate to enjoy fairly good health, but recently have been noticing some chinks in the armor. Normal suspects, low Vit d (low but within range). Low testosterone (once again within range). So I have decided to get a little more serious about light, water and magnetism and see what the results will be. As you may suspect, I have done my first set of labs, so I have a starting point.

Little more about me, since this is the introduce yourself section. I am a chiropractor, upper cervical specialist. I live in southern oregon. I am extremely interested in biophysics and the physics of health, so here I am. I am going to become a more active member of this community. Looking forward to getting to know you all.

### jeff taylor — 2018-10-14T23:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplementing-light.22308/post-253176>

So I am starting on my own personal N=1 journey. I am realatively healthy so I am looking for optimization. My diet is good, high in seafood, low in garbage. I drink at least 2 liters of high quality water per day. I have a magnetic bed cover on order. Been bathing in sun this summer, but as we enter fall and winter a question arises that has likley been gone over many times here. what is the best way to supplement light. Here is what my plan is and I am interested in feedback and thoughts

1) I am considering getting a led red light panel, body sized (probably joovv) and using 10 min/day
2) In looking at Jack's work, it seems to me that this needs to be balanced with the right amount of UVA and UVB to optimize. My thinking is that we can use the sun to estimate how much one should be getting based on the amount of red light. (my understanding is that Red light is 41% of the equation, not sure about UV) Very interested on peoples thoughts on this, as I suspect getting this ratio correct is the key to not making one sick.
3) I plan to get to Hawaii 2x this winter to get as much natural sunlight as possible.

1\. Find a 40+ milliGauss wall, power cord or lamp connected to 60 Hz (or 50 Hz) AC power grid. You know this because you have an electric field or a magnetic field meter.

2\. Put ES-problem area of the body next to wall for a few minutes without a pendant or crystal

3\. Say ouch a lot

4\. Repeat process by putting ES-problem area of the body next to wall for a few minutes wearing a pendant or crystal

5\. Say ouch a lot again

6\. Back away from wall so levels are back down under 1 milliGauss

7\. Say ahhhhhhhh

Using the Inverse Square Law literally means understanding where to be and how to mitigate common things that are known nnEMF producers. Not having wires run under the couch or bed is another great idea. Pulling the bed back from a wall another foot is another great idea. Pushing the speakers back on the computer desk another foot is pretty wise. Keeping the WhyFry Router and phones at a greater distance away is wiser than putting the microwave on or near the brain. ### axel brandt — 2018-10-16T04:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puting-the-blog-knowledge-to-work.22313/post-253353>

I'm 45 and have some trouble realizing that I cannot do some type of efforts anymore. One year ago, I had to quit running and playing tennis for 6 months due to bilateral hip labral tears. I went to see an orthopedic surgeon who offered me an arthroscopic surgery, but decided to try spontaneous recovery. By that time I started to read this blog and with the help of the epi-paleo diet, CT, and eating gelatin on a daily basis, I resolved my hip pain. Returned to play tennis, and started to run, without problems. Unfortunately, I started two months ago with a terrible back- pain after digging a big hole in my backyard, but didnt quit doing sports. Last two weeks I had to stop, and barely walk since then. I suspect to have an herniated disc, and will have an MRI done this week. I started to take a lot of sun on my back, with ice, 24 hs fasting, gelatin, and also dailyTENS (transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation) with high Frequency and low intensity. It's incredible the ammount of new things that I learned here, beside being an extraordinary good scientific divulgator, Jack is a good teacher. Any other suggestions to treat my lumbosciatic pain are welcomed !

### James Dang — 2018-10-16T20:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puting-the-blog-knowledge-to-work.22313/post-253415>

> [axel brandt said:](/goto/post?id=253382)
>
> Living in buenos aires outskirts, semi-rural area, 34° south latitude, my winter (july) vit D level is 27, summer 42.HDL 68, LDL 182, total cholesterol 262, TG 62, BUN/Creat 18,35 , basal insulin 3,7, TSH 1,46 , total testosterone 5,6.Sleeping very well, but used to have severe sleeping patterns.

Can you be more specific with your back pain? You say lumbosciatic pain but is it really there. Many people who mention sciatic pain actually have a few other issues going on. Obviously impossible to diagnose over the web but just to throw some bread crumbs your way:

**Physical:** Legit Chiropractors can be a godsend which I send many clients to (there are many bad ones so get a referral - anyone that doesn't try to fix you on the first 1-2 visits and tries to put you on a "plan" - RUN AWAY). It could just be a structural thing which can be fixed. I've had hundreds of clients get results with the 3 recommended chiros I send them to. It could be your shoes that you're wearing. Your walking gait could be suboptimal. Many people also don't know how to run properly. Lookup POSE running method. I was taught by Dr. Nicholas Romanov himself and it's a game changer for runners. Your body angle, the way you "pull,pull,pull" is vitally important in how you make impact with the ground. Etc.

You also may need to strengthen yourself through strength training since you re-injured it digging a hole. More spinal extension strength and just posterior chain strength in general + better digging technique

**Nutrient:** As [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) said, it could be Vit D or melatonin. Your Vit D is 27 which I think is considered pretty low being in Beunos Aires. From what I've read, it could be low for you but be optimal for someone else. You may also might want to look at your gut biome as the gut, after muscle testing, can be causing lower back issues. How is your digestion? Do you Rest + Digest?

Hopefully that helps and you can continue on your reading.

> But that misses a big part of Circadian Rhythm.
> The UVa may be even more important.
> So seeing sunlight specially early by sunrise, in the morning is important (on clear days, but also any time, fog, rain, snow, clouds notwithstanding)
>

> They were exposed to black light there.
> Constantly indoor, when outdoor, there is not much daylight in Chicago.
> Black light=UVa
> That crew was surprisingly healthy. Same people worked there for 20+ years, no sick days.

> 12 hrs/day

incredible hack, you have robust Vitamin D levels. I would see to buy a red sperti lamp. Thank you again, for sharing your knowledge.

Thanks, Jan. My tube black light (365nm) was off Amazon -- [https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006MQSR6/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o07\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006MQSR6/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o07_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) . I no longer wear prescription glasses since cataract surgeries, but the IOLs I have do block some UV. I'm unsure which is preferable, the 365nm or ~385nm... Am I good with both/either one? Always appreciate your posts. Cheers!

# Thread: Screens, IRIS, sunlight, blueblockers/clothing

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1

### Bastian — 2018-10-16T06:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screens-iris-sunlight-blueblockers-clothing.22314/post-253364>

I guess I know the answer to my questions, but I might not like the answer.
So here goes...

I am always running IRIS on my computer to change color temperature and blue light exposure. Do I still need to wear blueblockers and clothing when I am using my computer
a) inside / daytime
b) inside / nighttime
c) outside

I guess for a+b the answer is yes to blueblockers and clothing since I don't get the IR/UV light to balance the blue (even so it is mitigated through IRIS)?!
For c, my hunch is that I am fine being naked and not wearing blueblockers when I am outside on the computer, particularly when the sun is out since that would give me full spectrum exposure?!

How am I doing?

# Thread: Circadian optimizing down lights in new house construction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-17T21:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253508>

Hi all,

I know the ideal is darkness at night pierced only by firelight or candle, followed by perhaps moderate use of low flicker incandescents or black lights. But, pragmatically we have the need to install down lights, and I am looking for the best option. While Incandescents seem to be favoured, there are SORAA bulbs which are flicker and blue free, but not yet available for pots in the ceiling.

Enter:

[https://www.lsgc.com/collections/all/products/good-day-night-led-downlight](https://www.lsgc.com/collections/all/products/good-day-night-led-downlight)

Assuming this could be wired (would not want the wireless), and assuming it is verified to be relatively low flicker or flicker free - the automatic circadian adjustment seems like a pretty good compromise. We have a large household and most of the family does not adhere to light hygiene practices, so objective here is to minimize harm while maintaining mostly normal function for household.

Thanks for any feedback on this or other options for down lights!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253510>

You have options.......but your family context would probably determine my answer. Our answer at the Farm would not be everyone's answer. I used circadian light with a set color temp of 2700K = 9AM.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253511>

YMMV. I have no young kids at home. That is a big deal in making the decision. It is not as easy as you think

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253512>

Here is why:
It bothers me when I visit a medical school or residency program and see so much talent that is in the pursuit of myth. I think we have a duty to alter that trajectory. The best way to engage the mind is teaching it how to think and not what it should be thinking about. The same is true for the public and all those non-black swans out there. I wonder when they will choose to awaken?
[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-...arning-mental-illness-jack-kruse/?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-infants-children-struggling-learning-mental-illness-jack-kruse/?published=t)

May I ask what you installed?

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-18T14:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253576>

Just thought it was worth tagging this product here :

[https://www.soraa.com/news\_releases/90](https://www.soraa.com/news_releases/90)

This reads to me as though a power line option is available for this circadian lighting system, but require further verification.

"
**Connected and Secure**
SORAA Sky communicates over existing power lines in your home with proven, industry-standard HomePlug® GreenPHY technology, which means no wireless hub or internet connection is required to provide seamless integration with no wireless dead spots – just a plug–and-play connection to the Internet. The result is the most advanced security available, with end-to-end encryption of data and sensor events.

"

### Mhoej — 2018-10-18T21:56:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253628>

It's a challenge going candles only, but it is worth it. Bought a new place a year ago and we never installed any lights, in fact I took down all existing lights (incl. the fridge, oven, freezer) and haven't had any lights in our place for a year now. There are no light bulbs to turn on. The place before that we always had the lights off. It's candles and some red light torches only. It makes it easier that wife is 100% on board and our kids are still little.

Because of the high latitude we're at it is easy in summer with the very long days, but more challenging in winter. In the winter-time I make "swedish torches/canadian candle" (google a pic) to put outside, which gives a break from the darkness. Most people think we're nuts, but I think they're nuts for accepting all the ailments that come with melanopsin dysfunction (which we've had/have our fair share of). All of it is made much easier by living in the country side with a forest as neighbour for sure, low of population density is your friend.

If you don't want to eat crappy food stop having it in your house. If you don't want kids/yourself to watch tv throw out the stupid box. If you don't want nnemf in your house, then don't settle for just remembering to turn wifi off at night, hardwire it. If you don't want your family exposed to artificial lights then remove it from the house.

# Thread: steel framed tiny house, aluminum exterior and no electrical infrastructure inside= good?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-framed-tiny-house-aluminum-exterior-and-no-electrical-infrastructure-inside-good.22325/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lindsay Alfieri — 2018-10-18T16:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-framed-tiny-house-aluminum-exterior-and-no-electrical-infrastructure-inside-good.22325/post-253585>

Would a steel framed tiny house on wheels with aluminum exterior siding, metal roof and no interior electrical infrastructure be a good living environment for an EMF sensitive individual? Would any of this change when 5G rolls out?

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-23T18:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-framed-tiny-house-aluminum-exterior-and-no-electrical-infrastructure-inside-good.22325/post-254019>

Good question, and hopefully Dr Kruse has an answer based on ongoing development at the Farm.

I too looked into some of the tiny (and not so tiny) houses made from shipping containers that are increasingly popular.

I knew the relatively thick steel cocoon would provide more protection against RF radiation (even to an extent 5G mmW) as long as no radio wave source slipped in through windows, doors, etc. Also-- that you did not use microwave devices (routers, cell phones, etc) inside.

My gut feeling is that these metal-clad homes are not a good idea because of many unknowns. I know Dr Kruse has recommended steel roofs on structures (protection from space-beaming microwaves) and I think that is okay.

So, jury is out for me on this important topic.

### drezy — 2018-10-23T18:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-framed-tiny-house-aluminum-exterior-and-no-electrical-infrastructure-inside-good.22325/post-254020>

That in a very rural area.

### Marko Pollo — 2018-11-01T03:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-framed-tiny-house-aluminum-exterior-and-no-electrical-infrastructure-inside-good.22325/post-254679>

I have lived short term and worked in shipping containers and have dreamt of an underground hideaway made of them for ages but believe they are far from optimal. Although they provide a smaller version of the above ground prisons that most homo sapiens live in, I think in most scenarios that a cold adapted black swan type would be better served by emulating our hunter gatherer ancestors and building the skills and mindset to hand dig small dens, hidey holes or tunnels. A pick and shovel would give you a huge advantage over paleo man. Id take a backpack full of butter and salt pork over a giant cache of dehydrated food any day. Being able to live like an animal, avoiding conflict and moving arround seems a better bet than putting eggs in one basket and sitting in the dark breathing farts.

### Lindsay Alfieri — 2018-11-01T03:48:59+0200

# Thread: Have I been working NightShift the past couple years?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253591>

I just had a come-to-jesus moment this morning, because I've noticed a few unfavorable changes in myself over the last few weeks... Of course the days are becoming shorter rapidly, but I've been doing a lot of CT to offset this - works well.

BUT, I realised, I am driving to the office when its still pitch black (leave my house around 6:15 AM). My eyes are getting stabbed by car headlights (even though I wear blue blockers driving). I get to work around 7 AM (sun is not quite risen yet) and walk into my building full of fluorescent and LED lights. I've been doing this for probably the last 7 years.

In summer, this is a non-issue, because I get my morning sun prior to leaving my house (sun is up by 5:15 AM at summer solstice), but once September/October hits, I now have this mismatch. The biggest struggle I've dealt with over the years has been hypogonadism (secondary - meaning low LH/FSH). I've learned how critical a sunrise is for turning on the pituitary, and I'm betting now that blue light SANS red (from fake lights) before sunrise absolutely destroys the VitA/Melanopsin bond and screws up the diurnal release of the pituitary hormones from AM sunlight. EVERY year I've always noticed I go downhill in terms of seasonal affective disorder and adrenal fatigue by around mid-end October. The only times I haven't had these issues is when I was on hormone replacement (HCG). But because I'm off my meds, it seems to be affecting me now. The only thing that helps is doing deep CT prior to going to work. I know because I missed 1 day this week, and felt terrible.

I spoke with my wife about it this morning, and she has never faced the degree of seasonal affective disorder I have despite making less effort as a mitochondriac, but the one thing I realised is she always went into the office later (never earlier than sunrise). Most times, I'm in the office as early as 6:30-7:00 AM. I think this has been screwing me. The reason I like working early hours is so I can be home in time to make dinner before sunset (which is 4:30 PM at winter solstice) and I like working a compressed schedule so I can get more days off so I can focus on growing my business, but now I'm starting to see the detriment. I need to reconstruct my AM especially in winter.

Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-18T17:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253593>

Not sure why you're waiting to repair this environmental defect unless you have a death wish.

### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253598>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=253593)
>
> Not sure why you're waiting to repair this environmental defect unless you have a death wish.

Everything just clicked for me last night Jack. I've taken immediate action (i.e. coming in later) but now I'm working on a long term plan. I drove in just as the sun was rising. It was -5 C with the windows down and sun in my eye the whole way while I was still warming up from my 45 F plunge in my pool. What an amazing way to commute to work.

That part was easy to change. Now I just have to figure out when I can work on my business! I guess weekends.

### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253599>

I'd rather work 7 days a week with an optimal circadian rhythm than work 4.5 days a week and totally fuck up my AM. That has become my takehome.

It's time to REALLY thrive this winter. I think I did pretty good in the past all-things-considering, but I settled for the B. Time for an A+

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-19T02:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253656>

I got something for you.

What is coming for you is a lot of hard choices and reality.

The only thing standing between you and your goal is the bullshit story you keep telling yourself as to why you can't achieve it. This is Jim Thorpe below.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Take a careful look at the photo before you process anything I will write below.

You can see that he's wearing different socks and shoes. This wasn't a fashion statement. It was the 1912 Olympics, and Jim, a Native American from Oklahoma represented the U.S. in track and field. On the morning of his competitions, his shoes were stolen. Luckily, Jim ended up finding two shoes in a garbage bin. One of the shoes was too small and the other shoe was too big, so he had to wear an extra sock. Wearing these shoes, Jim won two gold medals this day.

**This is a perfect reminder that you don't have to resign to the excuses that have held you back.** So what if life hasn't been fair? Shift work, fires, shit always happens, hurricanes occur, illness visits us. What are you going to do about it today?

Whatever you woke up with this morning; stolen shoes, ill health, failed relationships or the realization your are fucking up your future with a shift job. Don't let it stop you from running your race. Be unique and run from all excuses.

You can experience more in life if you'll get over the excuses and get on with living. When you allow excuses to become part of your calling card, you've announced to the world you play small. As a result, you stop growing. End that now. Black Swan level shit here. Not everyone can handle this kind of truth. Those who cannot.............they are not my people and who I am looking for.

Kris, your only question now should be. Who are you? Who are you for you. Rhetorical question.

### kris90 — 2018-10-19T20:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253726>

So what did Kris do about yesterday? I sat down with my boss and set her straight. I told her I'm coming in the office later so I don't pierce my eyes with headlights and fluorescent bulbs in the office before sunrise, and I told her I'm leaving before sunset so I can avoid the same.

What did Kris do today? Saw my Doctor who completed a medical assessment stating my sensitivities to bright fluorescent lighting is disruptive to my wellbeing, and that I should be permitted to work from home unless absolutely required to attend meetings in-office.

Is this Black Swan worthy? Here's to the beginning of my NEW life. A reconstructed AM to optimize the release of my pituitary hormones to bring me from a B to an A+

### kris90 — 2018-10-19T20:21:28+0300

Thanks. We'll see how my hormone panel looks in a couple months.

It makes perfect sense now why my early hours didn't hurt me in the summer and as soon as I'm driving to work in the dark, boom, I start to feel subtle unfavorable changes in my wellbeing. CT helps immensely, but light trumps everything.

### kris90 — 2018-10-26T15:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-254227>

So my new morning looks like this:

\-Wake up in darkness, do my CT
\-Leave the house around 7:30 AM, sunrise is 7:35 AM (so it's already light outside especially on clear days)
\-Let morning sun shine through my open window on the commute (driving countryside route)
\-Get to work just past 8:00 AM, logon, check emails, then go outside for sungazing (15 mins)
\-Go out again around 9:30 AM - 10:00 AM for the UVA/IRA light (15 mins)

One thing I wonder is that while driving to work, I'm still getting hit with all the carlights and street lights even though the sky is already blue (on a clear day). Since the sun is just rising, not sure if I should still wear my blueblockers on my commute?

# Thread: Aldosterone for hearing loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss.22328/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stcrim — 2018-10-19T00:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss.22328/post-253640>

New here and noticed there were several threads on Aldosterone. I have been through the only internet program offered and I can tell you (for me) it does not work. First aldosterone has a really short half-life so human available prescriptions are in and out of the system in and out of the system in minutes. I spoke with the only pharmacist that compounds aldosterone for humans (at the time) and she said she has never heard of it working for anyone with hearing loss but does have other values. Her honesty was refreshing.

The same one story about aldosterone success has been circulating the internet for years and years. If aldosterone was a success for anyone the internet would be teaming with stories - it's not.

Aldosterone is inflammatory and hearing loss is or appears to be caused by overactive inflammation.

Does anyone know anything that might slow or stop progressive hearing loss????? Or the hearing loss from Meniere's that doesn't have the associated dizzies, etc. - Steve

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-19T01:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss.22328/post-253644>

CLOSED UNTIL FORUM AVATAR RULE IS FOLLOWED.

# Thread: Cancer to Optimal LIFE - Vervaina
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-to-optimal-life-vervaina.22330/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Vervaina — 2018-10-19T01:22:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-to-optimal-life-vervaina.22330/post-253649>

Hello friends

I am starting this optimal journal not only for support with my adventure with cancer but I also hope my experience may help the many out there struggling with cancer.

I was introduced to Jack Kruse by my MND a few years ago at which point I became so engaged with all the truths from his blogs I have made reading this material pretty much all I do in my free time because I can’t get enough of it I hope you do as well.

Background – as a child I grew up in a home with a low-fat diet lots of gluten and grains along with items like fake salt, saccharin, and margarine. We had free health care so as kids antibiotics were handed out like candy and we were on them a lot including “long acting” injections.

Probably due to abuse I had anorexia by the age of 10 and not the “I want to be skinny” kind of eating disorder. I was bone thin and ashamed. I hid in large clothes. Then added bulimia as a teen. I have bounced back and forth between anorexia and bulimia anytime I have had a shift in hormones throughout my life.

By the time I was 20 I had – chronic fatigue from an EBV with mono infection, lip herpes, chronic mouth sores, chronic strep (tonsils removed at 17), chronic dehydration (from lack of thirst) and so of course constipation/kidney infections/ heart palpitations, also pneumonia several times, and I was dx with HASHIMOTOS by nuclear medicine at 20.

Currently: ER/PR+ Her2- DCIS (breast cancer) stage 3, hormone issues, poor redox/ heteroplasmy, poor mito function, break down of collagen/muscle/ tendons in upper and lower body, severe insomnia (fixed only by RSO)

My main SNPS = MTHFR (A1298C andC677T) , TNF, CBS (A360A, C699T), COMT, VDR, GAD1 (multiple), FUT 2, AGT, BHMT, multiple NOS2 and SOD2, some of the CYP and NAT2 as well as mitochondrial pathways,

According to my ONCO score and GRCC test my cancer shows unfavorable for both metastasis and fast growth. In May of 2018 my cancer was found to be stage 2 with a 3cm tumor and clean lymph nodes 2 months later I had a mastectomy and my tumor was at 9cm and was staged at 3 with infiltrated lymph nodes. So I lost an arm pit and lymph nodes to cancer as well.

My current therapies – I have chosen to do no chemo or radiation (much to my conventional doctor’s dislike). I am instead going to an integrative cancer clinic where I am doing IVs 5 days a week alternating days of – high dose Vit C, artisinate, ozone, Meyers cocktail. Also getting acupuncture every week and taking cistus tea and a large number of supplements (which I hate doing) based in my RGCC test (test to see what kills my cancer cells) and also based on my spectracell micronutrient testing. They are giving me natural substances to keep my estrogen uncomfortably low but I am not taking tamoxifen which was also offered to me.

But most importantly I am doing sunrises every day, morning UV/IR while in the ocean, getting some afternoon UV, grounding, magenitco pad, DDW, survivors soup and seafood, CT, keto, calorie restriction, MB and RSO, amber glasses, early bedtime

I have labs I can list but for now in a nutshell I can tell you that my:

Leptin is normal

HS Crp is elevated (of course)

Growth hormone is 103

Estrogen is low

Circulating cancers cells are elevated at a 6 (Drs would like them down to a 3)

Nagalase is high (looking into injectable GCmaf)

Cytokine profile shows that I am TH1 dominate but with a high IL8

MARCONS was neg but showed a large (non MARCON) staph infection

CBC is in normal range

Genova GI profile shows basically no digestion of fats including Long chain fatty acids, cholesterol, phospholipids, and cholesterol (but the calprotectin was fine and enzymes were high range) beneficial bacteria were all middle to high range and no yeast or pathogens

And a few more tests I can post later… [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I would be so honored for you to look over my labs one day

I am going to be journaling about some questions I have with my current therapies - for now I will start with 2 biggies:

1\. Drs are thinking about IV NAD I have read that if your cancer is newer it is a great therapy but if your cancer has been around a while NAD could fuel its growth. This also makes me question taking 3 drops of MB 5 days a week, I feel so good taking it – maybe my cancer cells do as well?

2\. I was taking BHRT before my dx due to low hormones across the board. Getting my estrogen numbers up made me feel incredibly good. BTW – The Drs say this cancer has been around a long time and I had it before I started taking BHRT. So I actually got an ER+ cancer with very suboptimal estrogen levels. Now they have taken me off BHRT and I feel bad again. I am afraid low hormones were a player in what caused this cancer in the first place and here I am again with low hormones… scares me. Suzanne Somers and TS Wiley might have a thing or two to say about this.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-19T02:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-to-optimal-life-vervaina.22330/post-253652>

Key thing to mention where did you live 0-20 and what was in the environment.

### Vervaina — 2018-10-19T17:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-to-optimal-life-vervaina.22330/post-253707>

Born and raised in San Diego County - lived in a small more rural town about 30 min from the beach. No power plant near by or anything weird like that. We had asbestos in our home and my dad was obsessed with spraying the yard and house with chemicals for bugs and weeds often. Much of what we ate was cooked in a microwave (regular food not frozen meals which maybe worse because it takes so long to cook raw foods in a microwave)

Our temps were more extreme than the coast - hot in summer and down to freezing in the winter nights. We were poor so we ran no heat or air so we lived within our natural temps. ​

We almost never watched TV, had to spend our days outside and early bedtimes.

A weird thing for me is that my dad was raised in the Roswell NM area back in the 40's and 50's. He and most of his sibs died young of cancer (breast and lung). However neither of his parents had cancer and in fact his dad smoked into his 90's. So growing up around that radiation may have affected the kids in his family.

One more MAJOR stressor for me - my youngest pulled a pot of boiling water onto himself when he was 9 months old - 3rd degree burns over 20% of his little body - he was in critical condition, spent time in the burn ICU then a burn ward and was left with disabilities for some years. I spent many years without sleep and I wonder if this hardship that went on for years may have contributed to my illness.​

Moved to KY 10 years ago... and here is where I went wrong. The cards were stacked against me already then moved to a higher latitude. Around the time I grew the tumor I had been holding my son in a hospital hyperbaric chamber for 4 hours a day 5 days a week for 2 months, and while I was doing the chamber I was on HCG/HCG diet (eating disorder), had very poor hormone levels, was exercising hard 6- 7 days a week and was indoors 24/7 as it was winter and I was up late every night on the computer researching everything I could do to help my son. I was exhausted but thought I just need to push through with diet and exercise. All this = cancer I would say

I need a time machine

### Vervaina — 2018-10-20T01:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-to-optimal-life-vervaina.22330/post-253756>

Hmmm the start to my journal was a little dark - just trying to give context to my current health adventure.

My life is amazing and bright. I want to Fast Forward to current me and how much living in a quantum world has changed things.

So 3 years ago I became a patient of Dr Brett Jaques (listed in the practitioner link on JK website). He is great! And he teaches his patients about Jack's work. Being a long time M Sisson fan I was familiar with the monster Leptin thread. I just didn't know Jack was the real deal back then. Changed my life.

I was seeing Dr Brett for tanked hormones with debilitating symptoms, chronic fatigue, migraines with vomiting, horrible muscle and joint pain, more than just brain fog (I had developed an adult onset stutter and my brain was not working), frozen shoulders, back spasms and pain (for which I was doing prolo with prp q 3 weeks) and fibromyalgia . There were weeks I was bed ridden which was not like me as I am a go-getter and strive to be super mom/wife.

I right away began applying what I was learning.

I dropped my million supplements I was taking to the core few.

Instead of laying in bed I took my tired butt outside barefoot in as little clothing as I could and stayed outside 6-8 hours a day.

I was already keto but wasn't drinking enough water.

I didn't have a smart phone only a flip phone that I used for emergencies since I have always disliked technology so I was good there.

I don't watch TV and have zero social media accounts (as a rebellion against the technology monster) so I was good there.

I got rid of our wireless phone system set up wired land line in the house.

My husband wired our house so that all computers and electronics could be plugged in and not on wifi.

We started shutting down electronically at night.

I wore amber glasses and went in my room so I could be with candle light only after 8 pm.

It was like magic. After a few weeks I was feeling soooo much better. The problem was as soon as I went indoors my symptoms came back almost immediately.

The good thing was that it was all proof I was onto something real. I tried gentle exercise when I could like walks or a few yoga poses. I never went more than an hour without going outside for a bit. I got a magnetico pad.

I got my son to get outside and grounded with his shirt off everyday. He also felt so much better outside. My husband is an IT manager so he has been a harder case to get him away from a screen and outside but after awhile he started to see there was something to all this and he will attest to how much better he feels after getting outside and unplugged.

Once I read enough I started to see the error of my ways. I was missing the early AM light.

The game changer was the AM sunrise. I have to drive or walk a ways to catch it as it can't be seen in the valley where I live. I can't tell you how awesome this time of the day is for me.

After 3 years of work - I tolerate being inside more but will be sickened if I do too much of it. A trip somewhere like Walmart used to wreck me now I tolerate short trips to stores much better (with amber glasses) . When i go to the occasional movie with my DH I always wear my amber glasses and find the bright screen intolerable now.

My husband and I enjoy our wonderful kids and life that we now have!!!

I still have so much work to do to heal but that is ok. I think if I could just move in [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) and have her life my Warburg metabolism would shift back to norm and I would be healed!

Got a meyers cocktail for the first time today and then felt not good but weird and foggy for a few hours. I am better now but urine is weird, not like B vitamin smell but chemical like.

Wondering today about Resveratrol and other antioxidants like fisten, pomegranate extract, and blueberry extract and such and their Deuterium levels. They are fruit based and concentrated so they maybe high in Deuterium? They also can also lower TH1 response. I wonder if they are not the best things to take if you are trying to avoid D and trying to stay shifted towards TH1 side of immunity to fight cancer....

Had a cool experience. Was on the beach waiting for sunrise and all the sea gulls from up and down the beach gathered in a line next to me just before the shore. They opened their eyes 1/2 way and fluffed out there feathers and all took in the sunrise together staring right at it. When it was done they flew off. I think they are a part of this tribe.

**Haplogroup H**
18,000 Years Ago
While some members of R traveled far and wide, some remained in the Middle East for tens of thousands of years. Haplogroup H arose among the latter group, from a woman who likely lived less than 18,000 years ago. ### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-25T15:44:06+0300

So I might have to retract my previous statement. I "think" I now know what a 5G tower looks like and they seem to be popping up along some of the highways;(

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-25T17:57:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254126>

> [Mark Osinski said:](/goto/post?id=254116)
>
> So I might have to retract my previous statement. I "think" I now know what a 5G tower looks like and they seem to be popping up along some of the highways;(

So Mark, what does a 5G tower look like? I know 5G's tell-tale appearance as DAS mini-antennas (you know, those smallish gray boxes or cylinders on street and utility poles as well as sides of buildings).

I always thought they would add 5G broadcast antennas on existing major base stations (cell towers).

A typical cell tower (4G etc) can be on a lattice tower or monopole, with stacked layers of rectangular antennas arrayed in groups of 3, with each group usually covering 120 degrees of radius broadcast coverage. Wi-fi antennas can be smaller and different than standard rectangular gray box. Backhaul antennas can be attached to a tower-- they look like satellite dishes or drums.

If it is a busy communications tower, different wireless carriers (the facility's 'tenants') will have their own layer on the stack. Many cohabiting carriers on a tower makes for a bristling, fearsome looking evil creature. I know you have seen them and felt a silent shiver.

If you have a RF meter you are best advised to take radiation readings frequently to see what is coming your way. Today's meters can't measure 5G mm wave forms so be forewarned.

Good luck, and if it is time to get outta Dodge get out while the gettin' is good.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-25T18:17:19+0300

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=254126)
>
> Many cohabiting carriers on a tower makes for a bristling, fearsome looking evil creature. I know you have seen them and felt a silent shiver.

Absolutely.

### Mark Osinski — 2018-10-27T21:00:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-boston.22332/post-254368>

This is what the towers I see more often look like... If this is not 5G then great. I might have a bit more time. Thoughts?

Stop ordering RBC tests on this ratio.........

This is why the Fish oil blog I wrote in California right before the Paleo conference in 2011 was written.

I was skeptical of Robb Wolf's opinion then and now this buries the paleo solution for me.

More wisdom in the Epi-paleo Rx gets data: The O6/03 ratio is useless. Just eat the right kind of fish and the more tech addiction you have the more of those fish you need and less grass-fed cows you should eat. This study is free from bias of self-reports. It showed a higher level of marine omega-3 fatty acids in the blood (repeatedly measured over 13 years mind you, was associated with aging that was FREE of CVD, kidney disease and cognitive decline. They found the n-6/n-3 ratio was not important. More bad news for the food gurus. LOL.

The hits keep coming for the paleo paradigm crumbling with new data. #mitochondriacwisdom trumps "bro science". The take home: Eating oily fish under the power of the sun and doing it chronically is the best way to remain healthy no matter how old you get. Now, I hardly notice EMF when I am exposed (no debilitating symptoms). I still try to mitigate as much as possible in any case.(wi-fi and such has been off for a while at home)
I'm happy! Thank you for your information, Dr. Kruse!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-sensitivity-fixed.22334/post-352908>

And if holds a cell phone near head has "pain" in the head/skull. Can also tell me a storm is coming when it is 100 miles away, without checking weather. Can "feel it" in body. For the record, the individual is VERY sensitive to pain, always has been.

1) Is there a shield available/fabric one can wear over lap at work to protect the gut and body? I bought faraday fabric, and it does work, but is there something premade wearable at work that does not make a person look like he/she is wearing a snuggy or a blanket (professional environment) yet still protects? Am doing research. Any advice is appreciated. Maybe I will bring out the sewing machine and make a decent work jacket...with a faraday lining.

2) Are there good EMF meters out there? We've measured the elec field using a voltmeter near lamps, outlets but I would like to purchase a good EMF meter if reasonable price, and high quality.

Encouraging this person to find a different line of work but not everyone can just jump ship. That said, your health is your wealth. We are doing as much as possible to be healthy here. I wish I had known who Dr. Kruse was 10 years ago. We figured out a lot of things on our own (without understanding all the deep science behind it) but having access to this site, the blogs etc would have anchored us more as we figured things out. God bless. Just glad we were led to Dr. Kruse's site now, rather than never.

# Thread: Aldosterone for Hearing Loss II
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss-ii.22336/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stcrim — 2018-10-20T03:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aldosterone-for-hearing-loss-ii.22336/post-253772>

The original thread does not seem to be getting unlocked after the avatar was corrected so posting again:

New here and noticed there were several threads on Aldosterone. I have been through the only internet program offered and I can tell you (for me) it does not work. First aldosterone has a really short half-life so human available prescriptions are in and out of the system in and out of the system in minutes. I spoke with the only pharmacist that compounds aldosterone for humans (at the time) and she said she has never heard of it working for anyone with hearing loss but does have other values. Her honesty was refreshing.

The same one story about aldosterone success has been circulating the internet for years and years. If aldosterone was a success for anyone the internet would be teaming with stories - it's not.

Aldosterone is inflammatory and hearing loss is or appears to be caused by overactive inflammation.

Does anyone know anything that might slow or stop progressive hearing loss????? Or the hearing loss from Meniere's that doesn't have the associated dizzies, etc. - Steve

### Bastian — 2018-10-20T10:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-if-you-are-lean-stem-cell-loss-on-ct.22337/post-253781>

Just wondering about effects of CT as I am quite lean, not much body fat at all.
Anybody done a serious CT protocol being lean? Have been doing my own CT version (cold showers daily, sitting in a cold tub for 10-15mins 3-4x a week) for the last few months and about to start with CT prescription.
I think I just go ahead and see how I feel. My concerns is loosing/killing too many fat cells, especially if stem cells are hiding in my subcutaneous fat cells as I have learned today. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-the-midwest-on-my-way-back-to-the-south.22345/post-253823>

Diving In! My name is “Sunny Gale”, no pun, and yes an oxymoron which seems to characterize my personality.
I discovered Dr. Kruse a few months ago, and now listen to him 1-2 hours a day. I would absolutely love to spend all my time, working to help others understand Docs work and how to apply it to their lives.

I have a degree in Nutrition and Dietetics, and worked in the Fitness industry for 15 years. When I was in the midst of clinical rotations, I was sickened by what I saw as “healing modalities” for sick patients. I knew I did not want to go on to become a R.D., and that Diet alone.... was not the answer to healing the physical body. Fast forward 20+ years, and I am now working as a commercial trucking/transportation liability insurance adjuster. (Long story) Since staring this career, my health has taken a nose-dive. I was diagnosed with Fibromyalgia and low thyroid, and am currently take prescription meds. 5 years ago I was a runner, and in natural light at day. Now, 70-80 hour work weeks, keep my enslaved in artificial light. Seriously looking for to get out of this profession, and to move back to the South by next April 2019.

After finding Dr. Kruse, I recently started my current protocol:
Sun gazing; sunrise, sunset
Mediation (following Dr. Joe Dispenza)
“Light” break at work every hour, with my lunch break in the sun, barefoot, sitting on the grass, when I can.
Switched out light bulbs at home to a red bulbs, now installing full spectrum, but try to keep them off as much as possible. I have natural light coming in the sliding glass door, and large double pane windows.
\- - (Would love to hear the light that Dr. Kruse says he keeps on over his shoulder in his podcasts)
Blue-blocker glasses while at work (although since reading, I’m not sure my brand is really helping me)
\- - purchasing yellow/amber glasses this weekend
Iris is installed on all my computers
Grounding; use a grounding mat at computer, and sleep on a grounding sheet (in an apt. Plugged into grounded outlet)
Diet: Was Vegan, transitioned to vegetarian, and now have added back in seafood
Supplements: Have begun weeding them out, per Docs suggestions

I’ve read many posts, and I thank you all for the wisdom and love that you share! Looking forward to living and sharing these truths to all who will listen. Love and Light!

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-21T18:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-metabolic-trap-shines-during-the-symposium-on-the-molecular-basis-of-me-cfs-at-stanford.22349/post-253859>

Judy Mikovits and other brilliant researchers were able to fathom that human endogenous retroviruses were a root cause of many apparently intractable diseases like CFS (lamestream science of course howls that this is controversial).

Endogenous means these viruses were present at birth, attached to DNA. But others can be acquired later in life.

Dr Dietrich Klinghardt was among first medical doctors to understand this and is developing natural treatments to help silence these retroviruses and keep patients healthy.

I believe nnEMF is causing even more havoc with reactivation of the human enogenous retroviruses. Stay tuned.

# Thread: ACK!  I swallowed the red pill!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ack-i-swallowed-the-red-pill.22355/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeebs — 2018-10-22T18:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ack-i-swallowed-the-red-pill.22355/post-253935>

I’ve been slowly swallowing the red pill for a while now and recently I got a good thwack on the back from an activist to send it home and finally see a big picture and then another activist lead me to more puzzle pieces and an even bigger picture. From many avenues I’m seeing increasing levels of urgency and knowing what I know today I am not and cannot be a bystander any longer.

I arrived here because I’ve been researching in earnest since before getting pregnant with my first. I’m the last of my siblings to have kids and with observing my nieces and nephews I was not willing to accept the status quo for my children I was choosing to bring into this world. I wanted to be more proactive and avoid the mistakes that so many people were making in ignorance. I knew many things were wrong and I needed to know and understand more.

I’ve always been open minded with a desire to learn. I probably became aware of Dr. Jack Kruse sometime in 2016 and I have been mostly listening to him through podcasts and when I can I’ve read blogs and forum posts (along with the robots, I see, thanks for that eye opener… one of many). Dr. Kruse has definitely been one of my favorite virtual mentors and I was often confused by all his talk about being controversial because I did not find him so at all.

My immediate family and I don’t have too many health issues. Having 2 little ones now, most of my learning opportunities happens through podcasts/audio.

I want to build community with more like minded, aware individuals and perhaps help others swallow the red pill if they are open to it. I want to know of more solutions and be a part of creating them if possible. I’ve read that with membership there are private Facebook groups (Why over Facebook? How about using hidden forums or other venues?).

I’m always wanting to learn more and help where I can….. any suggestions, please let me know. Thanks!

~In Michigan~

(PS- Frustrated with all the “obedient idiots”)

### jeebs — 2018-10-22T18:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ack-i-swallowed-the-red-pill.22355/post-253940>

Also, thanks for this link: [https://ehtrust.org/letter-on-health-risks-of-wi-fi-in-school-for-principal-and-school-administrators/](https://ehtrust.org/letter-on-health-risks-of-wi-fi-in-school-for-principal-and-school-administrators/)

I've been wanting to discuss this topic with the school since before my daughter started going and didn't know how.

I changed some of the letter's wording and notified ehtrust of a few broken links and emailed it off to the principal and copied others. I was so pleased with the principal's response. He was ignorant and seemed genuinely concerned and wanted to know what to do with Wi-Fi at home. He also has 2 daughters in the same school so he has "skin in the game" so I hope Wi-Fi is eliminated or at least reduced.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-22T18:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ack-i-swallowed-the-red-pill.22355/post-253941>

welcome.

The story of the Red pill is an interesting one.

It is amusing to be accused of having a closed mind when what got me here, in the biophysics world, was having an open one. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Let them ridicule and laugh, Black Swans who are built by nature, while in nature will smile...and keep winning.

### jeebs — 2018-10-22T19:11:16+0300

Unless there is already one out there what are people doing with these?
Are there some ideal prefabs meant for burying?
I would take it we want insulation?
How do we waterproof these?
Back fill depths?

### Bic — 2018-10-25T11:56:10+0300
