# Thread: Struggling a little bit - looking for input
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Baldr — 2018-11-15T04:34:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/post-255649>

Hello,

I wrote the following on Saturday, but couldn’t post it then:

Seems like I am at a crossroad in my life. Just had a beer and a lot of carbs, and listening to my favorite music at the moment, with the wifi on for a while (because I had a fuckit moment), just to boost my dopamine a little bit. I still feel so stupid and slow it is insane. I am 25 years old and should be at my peak now, but instead I am at the rock bottom. My life has since the start just spiraled down to what I am now. And the days go by, day by day.

Nothing works out anymore. Whatever I do. Not that it ever has though, this is really all I know. I feel very insignificant and kind of lost. Hopeless. And now with 5G coming… it is just overwhelming.

(Just had beer #2 because I have a writer’s block right now, but it is still a struggle to get anything down. Don’t think the alcohol was a good idea, but I gave in for the craving, which has been a recurring theme the last months.)

I have had social anxiety literally since I can remember. This has been my main problem my whole life. And even though I have tried everything under the sun, like benzo medication (just one package 5 years ago because I don’t want to be dependent on that) and extreme amounts of exposure therapy to name a few, it has just gotten worse. A lot worse. I cannot get together with friends anymore, and I get a bad stress response just meeting my relatives other than my parents, like my grandma who is half blind (not kidding). I am barely able to function anymore.

To provide some context:

\- Long distance running training from age 9-16 which ended in overtraining.

\- Addicted to porn from age 13 to 24

\- Got my first laptop from school at age 16. Many hours in front of this every day at school, and every night in my room. And on top of that around 3 hours of video games in front of the TV every night. (age 16-19).

\- Got my first cellphone at age 13 (luckily not earlier)

\- Exposed to wireless network from age 16 at home and at high school.

\- In the military (2012): one of the guys at my room just had to have his wifi router 2m from my bed because the free wifi at the base was not good enough.

\- Started university 2013, but decided to quit after 1 year because of social anxiety (I wanted to focus 100% on it to get rid of it). Kept living in the same basement I was renting and started working in a factory.

\- For a 5 year period I was literally sleeping directly under and 1.5m from a wifi router, just separated by an old wooden floor. Mostly 4G, but the last 10months of my stay there it was 5G.

\- Also: constant blue light all day. Working beside a wifi router too, most of the time 1m from my head (so constant wifi exposure). Got minimal amount of sun, when I did I used sunblock and sunglasses.

\- Felt like shit at this time, began researching ways to improve my situations, got into Paleo 4 years ago, and went very low carb from the get go. Also got into coldshowers which I have been doing fairly consistent for 4 years (long before I found this site through Jack´s interview with Tristan at Primal Edge Health).

\- In this period: Constant static shocks from my car especially. Also, when I was near the smartmeter which was installed in the basement I got an urge to shake coming from my gut moving up to my head (hard to describe).

\- Stopped working 1 year ago because I was sleeping 3-4 hours after work every day, started living on my savings which I do to this day.

\- When the 5G wifi modem was installed last autumn (I think it was, hard to remember) I became a zombie. The little I had left of magnetism left me. At the same time I started a Zero Carb diet; almost exclusively grain fed red skeletal meat. Very high in protein and moderate fat. Because, you know, just a change of diet will fix all my problems… Over the winter I began experiencing symptoms of Parkinson’s Disease; emotionless face, rigid and slower movements (couldn’t walk straight), involuntary head movements etc. (I still have this in some amounts to this day). I seriously thought this was keto adaptation… For 6 months. Obviously, I wasn’t thinking very clearly. But I kept on doing it. It was a struggle to just go to the grocery store to buy my food, because of the dizziness and pressure in my head.

This pressure in my head started exactly 3 years ago. This was not so bad in the start, but has since just grown stronger. It is located behind my eyes, forehead and sometimes nose. I was supposed to have an MRI taken in august, but since I got into the nnEMF thing and started reading about the health effects from this, I really didn’t want to take it, so I cancelled it. I got the smartmeters removed and moved asap to my parents’ cabin.

This pressure has gotten a little bit better after I started eating more raw fish, and it gets even better when I use nicotine gum and it is almost entirely gone shortly after I have been freediving in the sea for 3-4 hours. But it is not permanently gone.

Where I live now (at my parents’ cabin) there are no wifi signals from neighbors. I use almost no man-made light anymore. Whenever I do, I use Blue light blockers. I use candles now. No wifi 99% of the time. The nearest celltower is 5km away. Nearest power line 75m away (250ft) though. I recently started sleeping grounded. This is a rural area, low population density. Lots of trees, and right by the sea. I use my smartphone a little bit too much (searching the internet), but have it mostly on airplane mode. My sleep has improved a lot because of this.

I have not done CT for a while though, because I am so sick of the pain. I have pushed myself for so long with almost no results to show for, and are beginning to fall apart. I give in to carb cravings (and really go nuts when I get a taste of something sweet). A month ago, I was eating ketogenic high fat, but can’t do it anymore. It doesn’t work. I can’t keto adapt. Also, I got a lot of sun in the summer, but got only minimal improvements from it. I was very consistent with getting AM sun and eating a BAB, and I noticed my sleep improving further, but now it is dark here most of the time and very little sun.

I try to eat a lot of seafood though, and have had several bad reactions to it. When I was still ignorant about nnEMF/BL I ate a can of sardines and vomited. Months later I found out about the MHS from Inger at this site, and went nuts with it. Downed some raw mackerel (and threw two heads in a blender and chugged it) and 5 hours later had the worst nausea, stomach cramps and vomiting of my life. I happened to be at my parents’ house at the time (this was early summer 2018), and my father called the ambulance because I felt like I was going to die (but they couldn’t come because of lack of capacity). But I got over it, and have had several milder reactions after this, but still really bad. I understand this is a lack of magnetism in me(?). Raw fish filet does nothing for me anymore, but whenever I have raw fish liver I get a similar bad reaction. I just had 100g of raw wild caught pollock liver and 37 hours later I still have stomach pain because I cannot seem to digest it. I have to lay on my grounding mat most of the time as to avoid the worst pain and vomiting, and I have nightmares at night and insane carb cravings in the day. Yesterday I literally ate pure sugar out of the box. (Edit: this settled down, but it took me over 50 hours to digest the fish liver).

So, based on all this I think I have an extreme lack of DHA, and I also think I have had this since I was born. I think that something happened during my mother’s pregnancy with me, because my two younger brothers don’t have the issues I have. She was working in a company that sold computers at the time (1992-1993). She absolutely hates that I mention this, and gets very defensive and take it personal, so it is hard to get more info out of her. Maybe I am wrong though, but it is obviously important to me finding the truth so I can fix myself.

She had a brain tumor a couple of years ago, it was removed by surgery. And she thinks it was a complete coincidence she got it. It all started with epilepsy attacks and she is now on medication for this. My parents’ house is absolutely saturated with wifi from neighbors. They all have 5G now. Mostly 5GHz I think. Many of them have 2-3 routers each, and one of them has a 24Ghz wifi coming in at full force. All of these are coming into their living room and bedroom (I checked with my laptop). I have removed the smartmeter for them, and they turn off their own wifi at night, but that is about it. They won’t do more. I told them to move, but they don’t think it is a big deal. I cringe when I see her hold the iPhone to her ear when calling someone, and when they sit in front of the flatscreen watching Netflix every night. I have kind of given up on them, I have to focus on myself now.

So, my outlook on life is kind of dark at the moment, but I think this will get better if I can fix my dopamine levels by getting a lot of DHA into my cell membranes. I am willing to do whatever it takes, even if it means eating disgusting raw fish liver. So here are my alternatives as I can see them now:

1\. Continue living at my parents’ cabin and using my father’s car (which I absolutely hate, being dependent on them). This is a good place to live for all the comforts of modern life, but the seafood is hard to get. The nearest fish market with fresh seafood is far away (and I am also beginning to run low on savings), and I have found out the only foods that I think will fix this for me is raw fish liver or oysters (which is hard to get here). There are no oysters here, and the fish is very hard to catch because there is very little fish left in the oceans here for some reason. So, it requires a lot of work just to catch a little bit (not to mention the liver is truly disgusting, just the thought of it nauseates me). It is also worth mentioning I have a job offer here at my uncle’s dairy farm.

2\. Or I can go somewhere there is oysters. Because in an odd way I feel like I crave them, even though I have never tasted one. Amazingly, the last years there have actually been an “invasion” of Pacific Oysters in Scandinavia. They are supposedly by the millions, and are blacklisted by the government. I am not a religious person, but one begins to think when there is an invasion of oysters free for the taking at the same time 5G is being rolled out. They have not come where I live yet, so I need to travel at least 10 hours further south (by train) to get to them. The caveat though is that I will have to live in a tent, and this is the darkest season of the year. But cold, dark and boring is probably what I need now. Just eat as many oysters as possible as long as there is daylight, then have a campfire and sleep.

Either way I think I really have to eat as much DHA as possible to improve my redox, because now I don’t have enough magnetism to actually get the DHA into the cell membranes. Does this sound correct?

I have low energy almost 24/7, and my short-term memory especially is extremely bad. Seems like it is just getting worse.

Thanks

Update: I recently received the news that my grandmother on my father’s side has skeletal cancer now. She finished chemotherapy and got rid of breast cancer 5.5 years ago, but has since just been getting worse. She has been housebound most of the time for years, because of poor health. Living and sleeping with 5G wifi for 1.5 years now. Before that 3G and 4G for many years. My grandfather is addicted to coffee, has destroyed sleep and can’t fall asleep before 2 AM at night. They all live in the city and eat the SAD, using fitbits, iPads, iPhones, watching TV constantly. My grandmother on my mother’s side has had glaucoma for years, but is in otherwise good condition despite being ten years older than my father’s parents. She lives in the countryside with no wifi (but smartmeter now and big overhead powerlines) and eats a lot of herring/cod and occasionally drinks raw milk (beside the SAD).

### ellore28 — 2018-11-15T09:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/post-255651>

Baldr, from your story it sounds like you are at a tipping point and I hope things improve for you. You've made good decisions by moving out to the cabin and decreasing your WIFI exposure, convincing family can be the trickiest part of the equation...it's hard to stray from the pack. I hope you're not isolating yourself completely though, good friends are the best company. Your quest for seafood is awesome, keep it up! In a pinch, are canned salmon and tuna more available? Don't forget omega 3 eggs have DHA too. Keep on sporting your blue blockers, I've noticed huge improvements by wearing mine and avoiding blue light whenever possible.
When you say you started sleeping grounded? is this a sleeping mat you plug in? or are you on a dirt floor? From what I have researched anything plugged in for grounding is not a good idea so I"m just curious.
I can suggest (it helped me) a power sun solar glo 160W light bulb during the day in areas of low sunlight for VIt D..it is expensive though(needs a special lamp encasement for the heat) you did mention low funds so i'm just throwing this out there for when you do have spending money...the reptile section in the pet store has them...or amazon i'm sure...Good luck, and remember you're not alone!

### Baldr — 2018-11-15T16:33:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/post-255671>

Thanks for the encouraging words ellore28.
Yes I can get canned tuna here, but I usually go for canned sardines and eat at least 150g of that a day if I don´t have any other seafood. Salmon I stay away from regardless if it is canned or fresh. It is almost always farmed here in Norway and it is really a dirty business. In my opinion it is really unhealthy and nutritionally poor food. For example: The salmon actually don´t get the red/orange color you see in the shop because of how unnatural their food and life is, the flesh is white, so they inject some kind of substance to make it look bright red/orange. It really disgusts me. I personally stay as far away from it as possible and tell other people to do the same, so if you can; avoid Norwegian Farmed Salmon.

I sleep grounded on a grounding mat I bought, which I have on my bed with a wire plugged into the earth outside my window. And I know it is far away from any underground cables so I think I am good. At least I feel good when sleeping on it. Thanks for the suggestion on the light bulb, I will look into it.
Thanks again

### Inger — 2018-11-15T18:53:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/post-255687>

Welcome Baldr! Another Norwegian here!   Now we are at least three

Well, I think you have an awesome place to be at in the moment! Enjoy it! I am a little jealous   Would love to live in a cabin like that....

Is 5 G already present in Norway?
But so lucky you had a place to escape!

Where about are you in Norway? I have been in Stavanger fishing Mackerel and there was a ton we got.. I had 3 mackerels every single day for many weeks..lol South of Tronheim schould be plenty of oysters too, you just need to find the right beaches... But further north it is too cold though...

I felt so bad that you did my fishhead smoothie and got sick in your belly
I should have put a warning on the video, that it can be dangerous if your belly is not healthy
One have to adapt slowly it seems. Of course if the fish is old it might give some issues too. I have had some real old fisheadsmoothie and it was so stinky but I did not really get sick in my belly, just a little off feeling. That said I am very used to eat all kind of raw stuff.

I am very sure if you now dive into thze dark, cold and boring, get yourself some good books to read, and just let yourself heal.. you will feel way better soon!
I read science books, and... lots of books of Osho! I LOVE them. Osho, and Amoda Maa. She learned at Oshos. and she is wonderful. Those books have helped me so huge with the mental side. And of course, to be in silence and avoid everything, as good as I can, that drains my energy/health.
Wintertime is so awesome to read, sleep, meditate (being in silence, just enjoying the now)

..and then.. when spring comes, you will feel it in every cell of your beeing, how everything comes to life and it will be so beautiful as ever before! Or maybe as it felt when we were kids  then everything felt so magic.. the most simple things...

I would not worry too much about getting oysters if you can get other wild caught seafood. And good quality meat/organs, like lamb. That is awesome nutrition too

### Phosphene — 2018-11-15T20:45:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/struggling-a-little-bit-looking-for-input.22508/post-255692>

Welcome! You are getting some wonderful advice and I will add another voice to embracing the cold, dark and boring. I have some of your same issues and though it makes me sad that someone not much older than my sons is so affected, just think of how lucky you are to have found these teachings.

I have an opportunity to travel to New York City this weekend, and I’m choosing to stay at my quiet, rural house all alone and dive deep—maybe even the electric totally off. A few candles, webinars, CT, chanting, yoga, simple fish. Maybe even a few minutes naked in the first snow of the season. That would be a first for sure!

I know how hard it may seem to look at cold, dark and boring as a blessing, but it really is. Embrace it.

# Thread: Trolls on the street - When blue-light tech cause a misinformation effect
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trolls-on-the-street-when-blue-light-tech-cause-a-misinformation-effect.22509/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-11-15T12:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trolls-on-the-street-when-blue-light-tech-cause-a-misinformation-effect.22509/post-255652>

Today I had an experience that made me reflect on my own experience when I was blue-light and nnEMF toxic. I was parked by the Petrol station (Gas Station), with my kids asleep in the back and dog behind them. When I was out the car my dog jumped over and onto my kids to get to the driver's seat. My daughter is awoken and begins to cry, I'm angry because our dog is not meant to climb over, and my daughter is crying. When I open the driver's door, my dog understands this and cowers in panic only to get her collar caught in the handbrake and begins to panic and squeal. I get her loose and take her to the back of the boot (we have a van type car), and put her in, and hit the sports bag below her. This I did for two reasons, one I was angry but too my dog was in a panic and wanted to jump over again, so hitting down she cowers to lie down and squeals again. Now you have to keep in mind that our dog is a squeeler at times, even when she somestimes would meet another dog and they jump by her she would give off a squeal and cower like she was bitten and the owners would go into a state wanting to correct their dog, and telling them no, yet I have to explain to them that their dog was not in the wrong.

I get it, the whole explanation above is not an ideal situation, and it, unfortunately, attracted the unwanted attention of a public low dopamine troll. A lady (very overweight, huge sunglasses, fully covered) on a very overcast day (the picture of a blue light toxic, low dopamine person), is filling her car up, approximately 15 meters (50 feet) away. So not only is she inhaling the petrol fumes, wearing sunglasses in a low-light environment, but also my body and back is in her line of view between her and my dog in the back of the car. Naturally, the described scene doesn't help. She then starts raising her voice and accusing me of abusing my dog. Despite my informing her that my daughter is crying and I need to check if she is hurt and console her, the lady only carried on, wanting me to not attend to her daughter and get attention instead. Then when not happening, getting her girlfriend to try to join in too.

Now the breakdown. The advantage of attending to your chronobiology, is you are able to raise your dopamine levels. This enables you to think laterally and not have your brain go into stupid mode. As JK explains in the Time Series, when performing properly, our quantum computer operates at immense speeds and enables us to make quick decisions. However, when this is not working like it is meant to, the Neurochemistry and cause a number of side-effects. For example, low-dopamine and acting on the backup ancient pathway of a low oxygen tension leads to glycolysis and PPP. When this happens, our vision of things narrow, and we are not able to see the whole picture. Our IQ and EQ drop. Then we also experience a thing called the misinformation effect, where I brain sees and processes things differently to what the camera lens in our eye saw.

I experienced this personal years ago when I was a blue-light, nnEMF, vegan toxic affected person. My eyes and recollection of the incident was different to what the CCTV footage showed. Being low dopamine as well, it was even harder for me to accept and I would try run to every person to get acknowledgement as a means to try and raise my dopamine levels. Why did this misinformaiton effect occurr, because when you are blue-light toxic, and your dopamine is low, you develop a **Fractal View of Nature**.

We understand from JK, that blue light destroys melatonin and dopamine in the eye and in the brain. When we develop a cognitive bias toward nature, out eye produces Beta-endorphin via POMC (Pro-opiomelanocortin) and solar exposure of the eye. This POMC is vitally important in combating any opiad addiction because it aids in developing an opiad within our eye and brain byproduct that is more powerful.

The inherent curse with this blue-light bidirectional microwave environment, is that the environment not only drops dopamine but destroys DHA as well. Thereby, people will tend to go off the deep-end. The lady went onto FB social media, posting pictures of my car, writing a whole bunch of statements and getting a opiad kick from the social media kickback. One of the co-administraters / community inspectors of the closed FB community group, was made aware of the situation and was kind enough to give me a call and get the other side of the story. Clearly, the posts and comments must of been really going out of control. I did not get to see them. I explained to her the first paragraph above and she was satisfied and ordered the FB post by the lady to be taken down by the administrator. I appreciated her experience in such things, as clearly it is not an uncommon situation in our blue-lit environment. At first she asked me to write what I explained on the FB post as a comment but then after further applying her mind, told me rather to not make any statement and rather ordered the post removed and for her to act as the proxy. My assumption that if I had made a comment in the explanation, like with the situation of me stating my daughter's needs in the car is more important to the lady's dopamine fix, it would have only spurred more of the other low dopamine trolls to carrying on even further in their opiad fix. Especially, since low dopamine inhibits our brain from seeing the full connection and how all the pieces fit together. Hence, a Fractal View of Nature.

The above is well explained in a podcast interview with Jack with the link below.

In [Luke's story, the most epic Jack Kruse interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=4843&v=5W6x7EsE8C4&ab_channel=LukeStorey)

# Thread: CT simple questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GringoPerdido — 2018-11-15T15:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-255665>

Couple simple questions on CT Kruse’s way. I learned the Wim Hof way which is a bit different.

1- How do you decide when to get out at the beginning?

2- Do you stay in if shivering?

3- Over time, does the shivering go away?

### drezy — 2018-11-15T16:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-255670>

> [RobInDC said:](/goto/post?id=255665)
>
> Couple simple questions on CT Kruse’s way. I learned the Wim Hof way which is a bit different.
>
>
> 1- How do you decide when to get out at the beginning?

My family's N=3

10-15 minute timer. Now a 20-30 minute timer. I sometimes get a little burp near 20 minutes in that I assume is a signal of some sorts like my gut vitamin d receptor calling up from the engine room.

> 2- Do you stay in if shivering?

None of us remember shivering much.

> 3- Over time, does the shivering go away?

For all in my family we do not shiver anymore. I did 2 x 5min dunks at 42ºF yesterday and layed on a lounge chair afterwards. No shivers.

Answers above from my wife and son.

### Phosphene — 2018-11-15T18:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-simple-questions.22510/post-255684>

I’ve been doing longer (50 min) but less frequent sessions because I (mistakenly?) thought longer was better. Plus getting the tub filled enough to cover mid-torso uses so much water it feels wasteful to not stay in as long as possible. Plus damn the warmup is difficult now that the sun can’t joyfully help it along...

I don’t shiver much beyond the initial shock (~50F well water) till about half an hour in, then I slog it out the last 15-20 minutes because I feel I should be pushing myself. Maybe I need to just get out before it becomes torturous.

Still hate cold showers (and so does my tangly hair). Currently start with warm to tepid, then end with a reluctant minute or so of cold on head and backside.

# Thread: Facinated and looking for "mito" community
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facinated-and-looking-for-mito-community.22512/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-16T01:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facinated-and-looking-for-mito-community.22512/post-255718>

I am always investigating renegade scientist types because I know what is out there in mainstream is generally ineffective by the number of people on $$$pills, and myself being so often found three standard deviations from the center of the bell curve of human health. Despite having decades of moderate disease, I have never been on any western meds. I have had a lifetime of illness which includes MS like symptoms that come and go with stress, as well as weak joints. I have tried many different lifestyle changes over the years living on 5 different continents in different settings being fully in the grid and at time way off the grid with moderate but conditional results. I have been with teachers who have exhibited a lot of the things Jack has talked of(often using a unintentional CT) but it seemed like, in hindsight, that did not have the full perspective from a quantum physiological perspective. Imitating those teachers methods have been helpful but the outcome has been limited for me due to my already compromised matrix. I look forward to learning more even though there seems to be a steep learning curve, but what I have encountered sounds right to the soul and gut.

I have had one problem or another since a young age. The worst of it began almost 28 years ago when I grinded my knees down to the bone from trying to cycle across America that lead to a series of diseases which rendered a muscular 175 guy down to 120 in a lot of pain. Having no insurance(in this dumbass country afraid of national health care) I was forced to seek alternative healing methods which in hindsight may be a blessing. It didn't heal for years and morphed into chronic fatigue/MS like stuff. Eventually my digestion collapsed on top of that. I was able to get out of the worst through some CHI/prana related yogic practices eventually and got back up to 134 pounds. I have explored many other methodologies including addressed stored emotions within traumatized cells which allowed me to run for the first time in 24 years. Things are better but I call myself a 50% person because I can not express my soul nature at a 100% due to limited energy.

Perhaps the silver lining is it had forced me to become a citizen health scientist as I am naturally fascinated and not shy at all to the the "strange" if it will yield results. I know Jack has mentioned take no prisoners when you are trying things that can help you despite losing fame, fortune, family or friends, and I have lived that way for the most part. Had I not, I think I would have been dead long ago or at least my soul would have living in what I would call a pathetic way. I see life as a exploration and not as a specific goal. I don't know why so many people at my age 50 want to stop learning. Reminds me of a Chinese saying that people often die at thirty and their bodies die at 65. I look forward to learning and meeting others experimenting.

THanks

# Thread: Amanda Elmgren's Optimal Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amanda-elmgrens-optimal-journey.22514/>

### amanda elmgren — 2018-11-16T03:40:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amanda-elmgrens-optimal-journey.22514/post-255727>

I was born in Dec. - (not the best I read… ) 10lb 4 oz to a 32 yr old mom w/gestational diabetes. Breastfed. I stayed a ‘big girl’ until I hit puberty then I pretty much lost my baby fat and was grown up 5’6” and stayed at 118 lbs. My folks planned me and I stumbled into a family as the baby 7/8 years younger than my sis and bro who were not at all interested in my being there (how do I know? they almost let me drown/ my bro let my baby carriage roll down the hill, etc). It was an emotionally tough life and I felt very isolated tho I know my folks loved me (it was my siblings I was worried about). Consequently, I felt the world was hugely scary and I had to keep on the right side of people in order to keep living so I learned to be a people pleaser. It was only a few years ago I realized how menacing I believed the world to be all these years and how powerless I felt - until I consciously realized that was not the case and I have been retraining myself that it _**is my life** and people aren't really out to kill me/tear me to bits verbally**.**_

Lots of silver fillings in baby teeth then adult teeth. Missing 10 adult teeth, 4 molars ok. But 2 upper left, 2 upper right and 1 bottom left and 1 bottom right which have meant a lot of dental work that now includes titanium implants, one set of bridgework and another removed because of decay/abcess. And one baby tooth - nearly 64 yrs old and it’s still in there - amazing design. Silver is out now. I understand titanium is an antenna of sorts ?

**NOW:** My health has gone downhill markedly these last 2 years. I was diagnosed with Mycobacterium Avium Complex (MAC) intracellulare around 2014. In 2000 I started with a lil cough that wouldn’t quit so I’m thinking I picked it up around then. But it could be ignored so I did especially when my dr told me it was asthma without any testing. It really didn’t bother me much - I tuned it out. (And prior to 2000 I know my lungs were fine - they tested for HHT in my lungs cuz my mom was diagnosed.) So I got really sick spring 2017 from a kid’s cold and my lungs took a real hit. I have anemia and had it when I was pregnant, too. I ended up at the pulmonologist and now my O2 saturation goes from 88 - 92with occasional spikes up/down; I use O2 to sleep at night and after about 7:30 pm a lot of evenings cuz it goes down; also when I walk now. This has really happened in this last year. I’ve avoided getting sick for a few years now but another lil kid got near me and I couldn’t fight it off and as of 11/11/18 on doxycycline. It carries a sun warning but it’s too smoky to see the sun in No CA this week from the largest fire in CA history, the Camp fire. I am severely underweight at 100 lbs when I should be 120 and I've been fighting to keep this weight for over a year and haven't been able to gain.

(How can I offset this hit? I hate that I am killing my good bugs but my lungs were hurting. I am eating fish daily, lots of ghee/ bacon fat. DDW JUST ARRIVED in 10 days from Qlarivia!! I feel my energy is good, though. I’m wearing my blue blocker glasses/ covering my skin around TV, screens, exposure.)

I’ve lived for 5 years in an apt complex where wifi is provided 24/7- they didn’t tell us clearly when we moved in, tho I don’t know if it would’ve made any difference. This is indirectly how I found Jack. My former naturopath is a student of Dietrich Klinghardt so I listened about how people won’t heal in nnEMF, and listened to Lloyd Burrell for months about electrosensitivity (I got Milham’s electricity book). I’ve listened to umpteen summits on health and when Lloyd did one on EMF I was thrilled with what I heard, and most thrilled with Jack Kruse. Klinghardt introduced me to Pollack and Fritz Popp; Klinghardt uses the body’s light to see what is going right and wrong which is interesting. So hearing about light from Jack helps me know I am at the root of my problem. I kept praying to God to get me to the root causes. With EMF the hardest thing was getting to the place of believing something I couldn’t see or feel with any of my senses was killing me slowly, now more quickly, and asking my husband to uproot yet once again (move 7 or 8) for my sake. But my body systems are breaking down - my skin is wrinkly like an elephants, hairs curled like eyelashes, some small areas of numbness in my toes and too many odd little things to mention. So I know it’s dire and we are making plans to leave the 5G Sacramento CA area. Northern CA with the Santa Rosa fire , Napa fire and now huge Camp fire is getting hard to live in.

Family history: **Mom** only child, smoked for years, probably during pregnancy?; had 4 way bypass, gall bladder out, HHT Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangesia diagnosed in old age, stomach/ gut issues long time, polycystic kidneys eventually on dialysis. Type 2 diabetes, osteoporosis, but mind held out till died at 80. Bro/sis wonder if she was depressed/ had rheumatoid not diagnosed.
**Mom’s mom** - type 1 diabetic for years, stroke. Mom’s dad died in an accident when mom was a child.
**Sis** is 72 now and rough shape - rheumatoid for yrs, poor leg circulation, osteoporosis (Coke Zero), Barrett’s esophagus, leukemia in 2017 , lives in MA.
**Bro** 71 former truck driver - can’t sleep & had 3 blood clots and lived tho ankle wound stayed open for years but is now closed; ulcer nearly killed him this summer so stopped drinking whiskey and too much coffee- doing better. Plans to exit CA also.
**Son** 36 , 6'5" w/ polycystic kidneys, lasik. Athletic, works outside.

I will get more up-to-date labs but will post what I do have soon so I can at least get this off.
I had a phone consult with Jack a few weeks ago and it was very clarifying, to say the least.
I am loving the responsiveness of the community here, especially the personal care Jack shows people when they post, and feel very encouraged.

How to get the most energy (so your body have that most important resource?

We are creatures of sun, sunlight (as it is at ground level)
it is #1 resource.

But also one would get (internal) sunlight energy by maximizing matrix water production.
The synthesis of matrix water results in energy release.
Mitochondria makes water (preferentially) from fatty acids only.
It will not make it from carbs (directly).
Eating carbs will promote body fat production.
So to be able to make that water you have to have
body fat
or eat fat.

One of the reasons mitochondria decides to make a water is if it get signal that more water is need then available.
Proper water in the body is regulated by thirst.
When you are thirsty
means that your ADH is trending down.
ADH-AntuDiureticHormone
But there is a way to fool that system,
if you wait 20-30 min, the thirst may/will go away.
ADH will/should go up.
So if you do not drink right away, on average you will drink less water and mitochondria will produce more.

You should get more energy that body will use to repair itself.

Additional benefit, you will spend less $$ on Qlarivia.

But you must eat fat (pork lard, olive oil) or you will get even more skinny.

### amanda elmgren — 2019-01-29T23:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amanda-elmgrens-optimal-journey.22514/post-261760>

My maternal haplotype is **J1c2b;** as for the SNPs, etc. do I send over the whole mess of data or how do I find out what specifics to look for and send?

My family heritage is Scottish-ish. Grandma worked in textile mills, widowed early with one child so life was tough around the Depression. Don't know when she became Type 1 diabetic but before I came along in 1954 and she controlled her diet well as far as they knew then. Non smoker; she aged and had a stroke, died about 1965. never remarried.
Mom born in 1923, smoked as the common habit was throughout my childhood; sedentary office job, had 2 kids in Aug 1945/Oct 1946, then me in 12/54; GB removed; on dr's orders quit smoking; yrs later heart disease w/4 way bypass in her 60's; polycystic kidneys misdiagnosed for years finally needed dialysis; mystery bleeding turned out to be Osler-Weber-Rendu or (HHT) diagnosed around 1998 and treated. Osteoporotic and stomach issues; my brother and sister wondered if she had depression/ rheumatoid from her behavior when I was small. She died at 80

Labs done 12/13/ 18 in Sacramento - Dr's comments: "Your hemoglobin A1C is the same as previous.
CRP remains elevated due to chronic lung infection.
Every thing else normal or close to normal."
She did tell me I have anemia of chronic disease; dexa shows osteoporosis of femur (Enlightening to see all the ways melanopsin dysfunction messes things up)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-01-30T04:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amanda-elmgrens-optimal-journey.22514/post-261797>

MAC = a RBC melanopsin disease from a bad environment.

WHY?

What is in RBCs? HEME

Because red blood cells (RBCs) modulate innate immune responses by scavenging chemokines, so this is why parabiosis studies show benefits with blood that is young and has an ability to clear these molecules, so I have hypothesized that RBCs may attenuate CpG-induced inflammation in multiple organs through direct scavenging of CpG-containing DNA.

The innate immune system detects pathogens by the presence of highly conserved pathogen-expressed molecules, which trigger host immune defenses. **Toll-like receptor (TLR) 9 detects unmethylated CpG dinucleotides** in bacterial or viral DNA, and can be stimulated for therapeutic applications with synthetic oligodeoxynucleotides containing immune stimulatory “CpG motifs.” Our mitochondria used to be a bacteria so I am betting that it also works with RBC's to sense the damage to our colony of mitochondria due to the liberation of CG Islands from the cytochromes in our mitochondria that are destroyed. TLR9 activation induces both innate and adaptive immunity. The TLR9-induced innate immune activation can be applied in the prevention or treatment of infectious diseases, and the adaptive immune–enhancing effects can be harnessed for improving disease therapeutic targets.

The family of Toll-like receptors (TLRs) appears to play a pivotal role in the innate immune system for the detection of highly conserved, pathogen-expressed molecules. To enable the rapid detection of infection, each of the 10 TLRs currently known to be expressed in humans has apparently evolved to be stimulated in the presence of certain types of pathogen-expressed molecules, which are either not expressed in host cells or are sequestered in cellular compartments where they are unavailable to the TLRs. Activation of a TLR by an appropriate pathogen molecule acts as an “alarm signal” for initiation of the appropriate immune defenses.

A central task of the innate immune system may well be to determine whether a newly encountered pathogen is present extracellularly, as with most bacteria and fungi, in which case the “correct” type of immune response to be initiated is a T helper cell (Th) 2–like response, or intracellularly, as with most viruses and intracellular bacteria **(MAC)**, in which case the innate immune system should induce a Th1-like immune response capable of killing infected cells. Recent studies indicate that the innate immune system accomplishes this feat, at least in part, by making use of the ligand specificity and the cellular site of expression of the TLRs.

When this process is active **_anemia of chronic disease is EXPECTED_**.

The innate immune system appears to use TLR9 for detecting unmethylated CpG dinucleotides, which are relatively common in bacterial **(MAC)** and viral genomes but are highly methylated and uncommon in vertebrate genomes.

It has also been shown in the literature that RBCs homeostatically bind mtDNA, and RBC-mediated DNA scavenging is essential in mitigating tissue injury after CpG-DNA is liberated from heme-based proteins which can lead to anemia of chronic disease. This is very common in lung diseases. What kind of disease states should we expect to see cf-mtDNA elevated? Any disease where inflammation is induced by heme protein destruction = lung pathology, blue light hazard, nnEMF, sepsis, trauma and most mitochondrial and RBC diseases tied to alterations in circadian biology. All neurodegenerative diseases fit this bill too.

### amanda elmgren — 2019-06-27T01:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amanda-elmgrens-optimal-journey.22514/post-273344>

So Richard and I left California in mid-February and set out for the Gulf coast. Went to FL but the preponderance of pine trees was too much/depressing for me and the sand is so gorgeously fine that I didn’t feel comfortable at the beach with my oxygen machine.

This move started as all about me, but in considering Richard as well we ended up in Bogalusa, LA, at latitude 30.77 population 11,000. I am outside every day now in the a.m. for an hr or 2 from 6:30-10 getting sun on my skin. The tree cover is so great we can’t see the sunrise like we did in Davis, CA but my Scottish skin is getting freckles all over me like I had as a kid once more. That’s exciting to me! I’m just now trying to understand the UV and when to be out for more Vit D - so greater than 3 is about 9 or 10 a.m. if I read my [weather.com](http://weather.com) UV ok. D minder not so helpful yet

Our house has dirty electricity so we’ve taken to turning off circuits unless we need them until we can get a building biologist to help us out. My oxygen level is in the 89-92 range still and it dips pretty much every afternoon or evening to the low 80’s for a few minutes. I sleep with 1 or 2 lpm. It feels good with the greater humidity except for the one or two incredibly oppressive afternoons we’ve had so far. (so our minister told us that we are nearly tropical & how the July/Aug afternoon showers don’t cool down anything but just leave everything steaming. What do you do, Jack?)

A few months ago skin on my hands started with lil patches size of pencil eraser I don’t like - reminds me of what my mom had that would crack and keep her hands out of water. Mine are just beginning, nothing thankfully more, no noticeable progression. I’m wondering auto-immune?

Diet here is tough - I joked that never have I been anywhere else that the 5 food groups are all starches… or fried. Using Walmart (!) and Whole Foods for organic and the local seafood store. What do you do, Jack? I just found out that the local farmers market town of Franklinton uses fluoridated water. No bueno, right?

Something I’ve been struggling with is the paleo / carb thing because I recall you writing that hypoxic people use carbs better than fat even though the end output amt is less so it’s 2nd rate way to go… I tried just to do the paleo and it was a big struggle to even gain 1 lb. So I’ve been adding a slice of gf bread in here and there, etc. Also just last week it got dicey because I felt that all the fat I’d been trying to eat for a few years overloaded my gall bladder and I was just floating in fat that wasn’t digesting. So I got some digestive enzymes and cut back. Now I am adding a bit of fat back in, grass fed butter mostly.
I finally am gaining a bit of weight - 6 pounds in 6 months.

Since coming to Bogalusa I’m not the same person on a spiritual level, and at that unseen level I have already been healed of a lot of ‘dirt’ that kept my physical being held down. There may be more to shed, but moving here to this friendly and poor part of the world has started our lives over spiritually. I believe the real healer is Jesus and the power of his name used on my behalf removed that ‘dirt’, and then we utilize all the resources we have available as it makes sense and we are led by the spirit. Each person is truly an n=1. Richard and I feel we have a contribution to make to this ‘train wreck’ of a town…

My maternal haplotype is **J1c2b - what does this mean to me?**
I read about fluorescent lights and realized my folks built the house I 100% grew up in with fluorescents in all the ceilings. I hated it then. Messed me up, right?

I’m very thankful for this forum and you, Jack, to contribute so powerfully to my life and I’m excited for my consultation upcoming in August.

Which is more damaging to your leptin receptors? If you have perfect solar exposure , i.e. The Masai, can you still become leptin resistant by eating a Standard American Diet?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-20T15:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-too-many-carbs-pufas-or-blue-light-a-bigger-risk-for-lr.22515/post-256059>

Cytochrome damage in the mitochondria from alien light waves always trumps food as a cause of LR.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-20T15:50:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-too-many-carbs-pufas-or-blue-light-a-bigger-risk-for-lr.22515/post-256060>

More proof that food gurus have absolutely no clue that a broken mitochondrial engine at the cytochrome level is a far bigger deal than food macronutrients. When cytochrome one is defective diabetes is one of the diseases that manifest. Excessive blue light exposure and nnEMF can cause this effect MOST FREQUENTLY. Cytochrome 1 is made from the NAD+/NADH couple.
NADH:ubiquinone oxidoreductase (complex I) plays a central role in the respiratory electron transport chain by coupling the transfer of electrons stripped from carbohydrate foods. This occurs when the food electrons are passed to NADH and onto ubiquinone (CoEnQ10). This helps create the proton gradient across the mitochondrial membrane matrix necessary for ATP synthesis via the proton channel in the ATPase. Understanding the atomistic details of the electronic wiring of all Fe/S clusters in complex I have been revealed by using the tunneling current theory and computer simulations.
It turns out both density functional theory and semiempirical electronic structure methods have been used to examine antiferromagnetically coupled spin states and corresponding tunneling wave functions to understand how variations in electrons are handled by cytochrome 1.
Distinct electron tunneling pathways between neighboring Fe/S clusters have been identified; the pathways primarily consist of two cysteine ligands and one additional key residue.
Internal water between protein subunits is identified as an "essential mediator" enhancing the overall electron transfer rate by almost three orders of magnitude to achieve a physiologically significant value. That water is made by cytochrome 4 = cytochrome C oxidase and it is deuterium depleted by design to allow for these key quantum interactions.
These key cysteine residues were further characterized by sensitivity of electron transfer rates to their mutations, examined in simulations, and their conservation among complex I homologs.
The unusual "electronic structure properties" of Fe4S4 clusters in complex I explain their remarkable efficiency of electron transfer and it is NOT THE FOOD that one eats that determine the interaction. They are just substrates in the molecular machines and not the key actors. It is about time we wake up to these realities because now researchers can examine them. In the early days of biochemistry, science was blinded to these actions, so it was natural to "GUESS" food mattered. Today, we know better but this information has yet to have a clinical impact or made the textbooks. There is nothing special about food electrons but there is something very sensitive and specific (quantized) about how cytochrome is built to work and why it makes superoxide in ways that it does. The pulse of superoxide is quenched and controlled by another chromophore protein called melanin. Melanin is a UV absorbing protein made of the aromatic amino acid tyrosine.
It is remarkable that the most fundamental energy-generating machinery in MITOCHONDRIA is based on the WAVE properties of electrons, which allow for an efficient transport of energy-carrying particles along the chain of redox cofactors toward molecular oxygen via quantum tunneling. [http://www.biochemj.org/content/425/2/327](http://www.biochemj.org/content/425/2/327)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-20T15:53:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-too-many-carbs-pufas-or-blue-light-a-bigger-risk-for-lr.22515/post-256062>

**Today’s quantum factoid:** proteins coded for DNA and RNA are crystals that pulse in sunlight and they float in liquid crystalline water made by mitochondria at cytochrome 4. Here you see another key cytochrome protein in action. They all have coherent motions but their freedom within their lattice structure does not allow total degrees of freedom. Proteins on earth coded for by DNA/RNA work best in a liquid crystal mesosphere because this aligns and focuses energy and information quanta transport and transmutation. Proteins allow for deformations because of their peptide bonds. This arrangement allows them to generate polarization wavefronts along with the simultaneous creation of proton wavefronts in conductive bands in the water around proteins. This arrangement allows for both metabolic pumping and photonic coupling of light to push the cell further from equilibrium while coupling light energy and information to biochemical pathways involving electron and proton movements. This implies that weak mechanical changes call tensegrity or pressure waves, heat, or electric charge will be easily propagated by small non-linear modulation of the proton currents in cell water. This is easier and has more fidelity when deuterium is absent in these regions. These hydrogen bonding networks link to the connective tissues via the ordering crystals in matrix water and this creates ordered dipole crystals that connect directly to the ion channels of the cell membrane. This is how light controls ion and voltage gates channels via second messengers like calcium flows. Proton conduction in water is how it is done coherently with superb fidelity.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-20T15:55:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-too-many-carbs-pufas-or-blue-light-a-bigger-risk-for-lr.22515/post-256064>

Who knows what the circadian rhythm is? Essentially, it's our biologic clock that's 24 hours. 'Circa' in Latin meaning "approximately" and 'Dias' meaning "day" so approximately a day. This clock they found is approximately 24 hours, I wonder why that would be?
Simple, it's because our body has 'tuned' to the sun, over millions of years of evolution, as its frequencies change during the day and determine the day and night cycle.
What we need to do is to be outside during the day, spending as much of our time outside in natural light as possible. The key thing that is hard for some people is you never ever, ever want wear sunglasses as they destroy the spectrum of sunlight.
The sun has a natural full spectrum. There's UV, infrared, green, yellow, blue etc. When you wear sunglasses you cut out certain frequencies primarily ultraviolet. Why are all parts of the ultraviolet spectrum so important?
When ultraviolet became more present in the atmosphere 600 million years ago, life exploded in its complexity, and over time we have evolved to use the energy of sunlight in many of our biological processes.
Ultraviolet light has huge benefits for our health and there are many places you can learn about why the sun is needed and necessary for optimal health. [https://www.frontiersin.org/article...ZqQVBZFr7oS9fOoLefiuqqBPTkfaoXxPuW3ZJ0vN5VteE](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fgene.2018.00452/full?fbclid=IwAR2agz-ghXkpixZqQVBZFr7oS9fOoLefiuqqBPTkfaoXxPuW3ZJ0vN5VteE)

Did you know that tilt varies in many ways that create non-linear relationships? What might the collateral effects before our species as they vary? Do the position of the sun at the tropics and the presence of the sun to the arctic circle in ways that we have not yet connected to things that live? Is this relationship related to other living things too? Is this why trees cannot exist above a certain latitude? Is this why humans have trouble at these places? Is this why there is a spot under Antarctica that science is impotent to explain? Might the evidence be found in our brain right now? Is this why the Ancient Pathway in mammals exists? Could all this be why Jack wrote CT4 and CT 6?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-20T15:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-too-many-carbs-pufas-or-blue-light-a-bigger-risk-for-lr.22515/post-256066>

What would most guess is under Antarctica close to our south pole? The linear thinker would be lead to quickly respond to the answer was a geothermal volcano. But a Black Swan would never assume this because it is too linear and easy. Nature never uses Occam's razor in her recipes. There is nothing parsimonious about the photoelectric effect or the mass equivalence equation, is there? So what would be your guess is causing this "odd phenomenon" under our Southern Continent?

Human thinking is fools gold. It falls prey to linear thinking and relationships far too often. For example, we've long assumed in our solar system that planets are formed outside stars in planetary discs “for the ‘obvious’ reason linked to our observations as humans – that’s where we find them.” in our telescopes. But then we forget about the similar relationship of creation found in our own species. Namely, we humans, equally ‘obviously,’ are outside our mothers as we live our lives – yet we did not start there!

We never stop to see this blind spot of linear thinking. We observe the beginning of human life all the time as an internal process that becomes external. With planets since humans did not observe their creation, we assume how they are today are best explained by their current position.

I do not think it is a geothermal unit at all. I think it is an electromagnetic effect tied to why the Earth's tilt varies between 22 and 24.4-degree tilt and that this hot pocket varies as the tilt and the electromagnetic relationship of the sun the Earth varies. **_Today's insight on dietary issues for the Black Swan:_** For many or most people, a healthy low-carb diet will probably produce many health benefits and improve many health conditions if consumed in place of the standard American diet. The real Black Swan question under dispute in science is whether low-carb diets have a metabolic advantage vs. high-carb diets. My position has always been this is hogwash. The diet does not matter much when you understand how critical the engines within mitochondria are. It turns out that when cytochrome 1 is damaged, has high heteroplasmy rate, or its cysteine residues are damaged, or those residues are not surrounded by matrix derived water, then and only then does it appear that glucose and insulin are toxic. Appearances are deceiving to those who do not understand the details of the atomic latticework inside of the cytochrome complexes. Today's dietary half-truths are a leprosy of unreality disfiguring every human creature who believes it.

# Thread: Alexis' Optimal Journey- MS like issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-16T18:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-255768>

Some background. After 3+ decades of moderate illness at times debilitating I looking forward to seeing what a quantum approach can do that chemicals can't. I have done some quantum-ish methods via spiritual yogic practices that have helped somewhat, but can only be maintained while I focus on it which at a certain point feels like a robotic experience just surviving. It occupies too much of my psyche to maintain it in a practical way. Currently I drive a schoolbus which allows me to work part time and gives me windows to relax because if I do full time work my nerves get overloaded. The only thing that sucks is that being able to work part time limits income stream, but sadly for me being able to support myself without relying on anybody is a relatively high standard for now. Hopefully that will change with increased vitality.

Historically I was 175lbs at 5'7" with little fat at age 22 and one summer I tried to ride across the USA on a bicycle. I ended up grinding my knees down to the bone. They did not heal and the pain migrated to lower back, shoulders, carpal tunnel, neck, foot bone spurs. I realized I was body armouring then I decided I had to stop or would continue to implode my joints. I stopped through awareness of that and my weight dropped 35lbs in 3 months. I then became muscularly inflamed all over my body and the weight dropped down to 118. During that time I feel the mass inflammation burned out my nervous system which has never fully recovered as of yet 28 years later. As a teen I did have a weak liver and eyes were frequently jaundiced. It is better now but I can't pour crap oils into it. I do not put on weight no matter how much I eat, but I am going to try and eat a deuterium appropriate diet, plus CT to see it that changes.

SInce hearing about Mitochodriacs about a month ago I have been reading several hours a day about it. I started with the face dunking for about 2+ weeks ago and tried to move onto cold showers. It seem to over stimulate my kidneys and my mid back started to cease up. So I retreated a bit and started cold plunges 48deg(15min) with just my calves and part of my thighs for about the last week. In the next day I will shift all the way to slightly over the pelvis. I will slowly raise the water up to my neck over the next few weeks is my plan. The first day hurt like hell but more and more the pain subsides so that is encouraging. I also added epsom salts to the mix to neutralize the chlorine/fluoride. Now I got a tupperware container out on the deck. I keep torso well covered for now(but not the arms). It is 32deg today- I guess I don't need to buy ice .

As far as light mitigation I got my blue blockers and Iris installed on my comp. I go out to the park and sit on the ground for about 20 min every morning with my bare legs and hand touching the earth. At night I see my computer screen to almost no blue light setting too. I try and stop all computer and lights by 9:30. Turn off the lights and listed to a podcast. I am sleeping much better since doing this aspect of the mito work and sleep about 8.5 hours a night. I used to need melatonin here and there but now I don't ever use it. My nerves seem a little bit more stable too but hard to say for now.

Food- I was a vegan until about a year ago for about 5 years. It did not serve me well, but in general food does not serve me well, though. I often wish I can take a hunger pill and not bother with eating again. I know, no such luck. Anyway I have transitioned to eating a lot of sardines. Cut further back on my carbs. I did a bunch of oysters but after a while it seems to create some cramping in my colon so I will still eat them when I can, but til I get used to it, it will have to be in moderation. I use a lot of hemp oil which was manufactured at my latitude. I bought fish oil in a container but read that Jack said it does not serve the second cytochrome so it is useless in that form. I am at a quandary with the fat intake needing to from fish because it seems like you would have to eat a lot of fish to get 20 -30 grams of fat. For now I get some from sardines and most from hemp oil. If anybody reads this, how to they get their fat intake?

I ordered a 23andme test so will follow up on that. I have been blood tested for the basic stuff about once a decade but never any signs of disease in the blood. I also got an MRI last year and the brain looks normal.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-16T22:15:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-255793>

I was able to do CT up to my navel in 45 deg water for 15 min. It was very doable while I was doing it so next time I will go up to my upper chest. My dilemma is that I feel fine after for about an hour but then beyond that it takes hours to fully warm up. I am also getting a bit of mid back pain as I am guessing is CT overstimulating my adrenals(which alt therapist say are a bit weak) For a while the cold makes me slightly incontinent while I remain cold for about 3 hours. I am wondering about what is the protocol to warm the body back up for skinny people like me with prolly only 6% fat. It seems like my body lacks the resources to warm myself up unassisted without a lot of heat. I decided to go for a brisk bike ride after about an hour which helped generate some internal heat. Any ideas anyone.?

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-24T22:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-256501>

I think it has been a week.

For a while I seemed to be losing weight on the epi paleo diet eating a lot of canned oysters, sardines, occasional pork ribs, chicken, carrots, brussel sprouts, onions, some fish oil, and lots of hemp seed oil, with very few carbs. Once my weight drops below 130 I tend to get light MS/CFS like symptoms with tingling thighs and weird feverish fatigue. I am guess there is just not fat flying around in the body to proper myelinate then. Anyway for about 2 weeks I was having such symptoms that I don't get that often. Out of desperation I decided to try some locally grown chicken livers from a local free range farm. Almost immediately that tingling/funky weak feeling in my thighs disappeared. I have been eating about 2-3 chicken livers a day. Tastes nasty but I don't give a shit if it serves the function. My desire for carbs has mostly diminished though it was never that high.

My sleep continues to get deeper. I can say my overall stamina has increased much yet but it is not bad either. Trying to cram a massive amount of knowledge in the brains right now with this site and I seem to have the energy for it so that is good. I know I need some tests done. I am guessing my condition is weak adrenals with a liver that can't process massive amounts of liver.

MY CT goes up and down. I dipped in 48 deg water up to my chest of 13 min and it took a long time to warm up. I usually feel good when I get out initially but then feel a bit sickly when it feels like cold menthol like blood is being pumped through my body. Trying to find a balance with that yet. It is a fine line as my kidney area on my back tenses up and I become prone to back pain then. I am following it up with some exercise and hot shower to rewarm up the core. Today I just did my feet calves and arms in 36 deg water because I had to be physically active later and did not want my back seizing on me.

I set up a grounding wire to my room and not sure if I sleep better with it. I need to check if it is passing some EMF hotspots. Can only experiment on so many things at a time.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-25T02:42:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-256526>

Danish media are telling the story that Multiple sclerosis has doubled in their country in 20 years and medical researchers don't have a clue why? Are they telling the truth? Or do they know why?
This Danish cohort study from 2012 says they do know. Do you?
The study, which is based on a small population, found little evidence among longtime subscribers.
"No evidence" might be evidence, in fact!
The paper is interesting because it is based on the same subscribing data used in the Danish cohort study on cell phones and cancer some years earlier. That study has been criticized heavily in the EU for putting business users in a category of non-exposed EMF groups. Considering the recent NTP data this is data that should be re-assessed.
The Danish cohort study on cell phones and cancer was partially financed by the country's two biggest telecom companies.
When you pay, you generally get results you want in science.
Denmark cell phone use is some of the highest in the world and now MS is exploding there. This is older cooked data from a different epoch of the tech age tells a different biologic story of the risk of "cellphoning" . Using food and diet to fix a quantum problem is not a wise maneuver. [https://journals.plos.org/plosone/a...qJmKK501szd6ojsm3EfqB8tHwCjHa9VOGk-I1HRz5xUMs](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0034453&fbclid=IwAR1Q4-1RhSNXxsqJmKK501szd6ojsm3EfqB8tHwCjHa9VOGk-I1HRz5xUMs)

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-25T16:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-256562>

Also, it seldom went for source of the problem like you do Dr. Kruse. You are prolly the first western Doc I have seen who can nail out so many problems back to its source backing it with pure science. Henceforth, I usually utilized eastern approaches which were relatively more thorough towards the individual nature. But all of them were created in a time before man lived out of harmony with natural law. They have great insight but the latest shift with emf, wifi, 5g, led etc is beyond that science. I always felt bummed about western medicines direction because I am fascinated by technology/insights of the body. Thanks for opening my eyes on how impactful these things are because I have been trying to dumb myself down to it over the years trying to manage.

I recently had to move out of my girlfriend's house due to EMF issues and a recent install of smart meters. I landed in a friend's house which is a 100 years old which I guess has simpler wiring even though it is more densely populated with smart meters everywhere, and sleep way better for now without my heart feeling like a jackhammer. In a few months, I will move out to a dilapidated tax sale house I bought for 13K that has all of its wiring cut out by meth heads so needs rewiring. I am playing with the idea of not rewiring much and just getting solar panels and a fireplace if the city leaves me alone with the idea for a while. That will work til 5g comes into town which will be hopefully a few years at least in my section of town. Then time to move again prolly...... :-(.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-12-07T21:18:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-257529>

So it has been about 2 weeks since I posted in my journal... Relatively good progress. I am now able to do CT relatively comfortable with about 45 deg water. I could go colder but have been told there is no reason especially if you are super skinny. I am starting to like it more and more now that my kidneys are not feeling like they are clinching up and causing mid back pain. I am not a hundred percent sure but I have in the last few weeks had almost zero back pain which would come about after the nights when I play pickleball at the gym and would like to attribute that improvement to CT.

One thing that is quite important is that I am starting to use Wim Hoff's breathing techniques and that has helped keep my body calmer in the cold.

I plan to get deeper into using Wims techniques along with Jacks approaches to mitigating nnEMF and lights while avoiding mismatch foods. I look forward to future results.

I have modified CT a bit to make it more doable too for now where I wear a sleeveless sweat shirt hoodie on and go in the water with it. I go about up to my mid chest so the upper part of my core body stays relatively warm. The rest is under water. It is quite comfy once I settle in and stay there for about 20 min. Warming up is still a bit of a endeavour for me where I do get drop down(can't remember official term) after about 10 min and for about half an hour feel a bit queasy when my blood feels a bit "poisoned" from the cold. However somehow it seems like the warming up period is far shorter and less drop down when I go in after playing pickleball for 2 hours. For me working out afterwards just seems to stir up the cold blood too fast. Also with doing it at night after pickleball, I can get into a warm bed where my body is going to drop my body temperature down anyway. The only catch is, it is at night so it is a lot colder outside where just the other night it was 25, and also there is a lot of nnlights out. If all goes right I should move into my own house in a few months where that can be mitigated better.

The epi paleo diet is quite doable and I overall like it. I eat lots of sardines and some oysters. I've added more and more pork into my diet. I still have to eat like a locust to keep my weight at 132 but feeling more solid.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-12-25T04:46:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-258944>

So after about 6 weeks of CT I noticed I was getting more and more neurotis in my legs. Kinds of like a sciatica of L4 down the sides and front of thighs. For about 4 days I stopped CT completely and the neuritis stopped but some old body pain came back. So I decided to do just what Ben Franklin used to do for now and just dip feet and calves in ice water. That seemed just about right for now. Still searching around the home where i can drop a good grounding wire that does not have dirty electricity around it. Overall health has been better. Still too skinny though and have to decern information on this site designed for hog transformation. Will order a couple of these to try. [https://www.amazon.com/NODONU-RU-Mask-Sleep-Fragrance-Free-3pieces/dp/B0012I955A](https://www.amazon.com/NODONU-RU-Mask-Sleep-Fragrance-Free-3pieces/dp/B0012I955A) to see if I can maintain better hydration.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2019-01-13T00:44:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alexis-optimal-journey-ms-like-issues.22516/post-260185>

Things are overall good. COntinuing to do mini CT's up to my knees and about 10 cold face plunges a day. Also got my Haplo type report. Not sure what to make of it. Anybody know? My Haplotype is a **N1b1**

**N1b**
19,500
Years Ago
**Origin and Migrations of Haplogroup N1b**
Your maternal line stems from a branch of N called N1b. All the members of N1b trace their maternal lines back to a woman who lived approximately 20,000 years ago in southwestern Asia, where her descendants remain concentrated today. N1b reaches its highest levels among the Mazandarani of northern Iran and the Georgians of the Caucasus Mountains between the Caspian and Black Seas. It is also detected at lower levels among the Egyptians, Saudi Arabians, Bedouin, and other Middle Eastern populations. It is quite rare in Europe, where it may have spread at the end of the Ice Age when the climate became more hospitable, or later during the introduction and expansion of agriculture to Europe about 7,000 years ago.

**N1b1**
14,000
Years Ago
**Your maternal haplogroup, N1b1, traces back to a woman who lived approximately 14,000 years ago.**
That's nearly 560 generations ago! What happened between then and now? As researchers and citizen scientists discover more about your haplogroup, new details may be added to the story of your maternal line.

**N1b1**
Today
**N1b1 is relatively common among 23andMe customers.**
Today, you share your haplogroup with all the maternal-line descendants of the common ancestor of N1b1, including other 23andMe customers.
1 in 680
23andMe customers share your haplogroup assignment.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-17T17:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/post-255834>

I've been starting CT slowly over the last three weeks with 10 days of face dunks, then I tried to jump into a cold shower but my mid back started to spasm too much so I stopped that. I think weak adrenals are an issue. So added 10 days of dunking just up to the knees in 45 deg water with the rest of the torso and head kept warm.

Yesterday, I was able to do CT up to my navel in 45 deg water for 15 min. It was very doable while I was doing it so next time I will go up to my upper chest I think. My dilemma is that I feel fine after for about an hour but then beyond that it takes hours to fully warm up. I am also getting a bit of mid back pain again as I am guessing is CT overstimulating my adrenals(which alt therapist say are a bit weak). Its possible my liver is weak and not producing enough colesterol(I've been eating plenty of colesterol). The problem is I remain cold for about 3 hours and for a during which time the cold makes me slightly incontinent. It seems like my body has not enough energy to warm myself back up for a long time. I am wondering about what is the protocol to rewarm the body for skinny people like me with prolly only 5% fat and being underwieght (5' 7",128lbs sadly I have lost 6 pounds since doing Jack's diet and CT, but hoping it is temporary direction). I decided to go for a brisk bike ride after about an hour which helped generate some internal heat.

From my understanding the effects of CT will only last while the surface of the skin stays under 60 deg. Should I just monitor the skin, and as soon as the skin temp rises above 60 I should just jump into a hot shower and get the body temp back up? Ideally seeking advice from a lizardtype instead of beartype on this issue LOL.

Any ideas anyone.? Thanxs for looking.

I think you are right in me trying too much at once between the change in the lights, diet and CT. I am ditching the strict diet part for now and eating when I am hungry and whatever my body craves. It seems like a lot of the focus it is for fat people and I am the opposite. I will prolly try and stick a few pounds back on before resuming CT, but at least once more I was thinking I will try it once more and just do a hot shower after thirty min. ### Inna — 2018-11-18T15:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-advice-on-ct.22518/post-255890>

I do well in the water and even enjoy the feeling, I freeze during 5 min walk back to my condo (weaning my towel, mittens and boots), I leave the door open, my hands are so frozen, it is hard to turn the key, then I ride my stationary bike to warm up, I have 2 reptilian glo lamps full spectrum (the larger lamp) and UV spectrum (smaller), I ride 25 min (5 miles) on the bike with the lams in wearing my kiniki swimming suit which usually dries up on me, and I feel great afterwards. I usually ct in the morning at sunrise

Sometimes I ct later on the day, If the sun is strong (noon time), I put my bike on the balcony and do not need the lamps

# Thread: Brain Fog Cure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-cure.22521/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101

### Safeby2 — 2018-11-18T03:19:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-cure.22521/post-255872>

So everytime I eat anything I get fatigued and brain fogged for a few hours. This has been this way ever since Ive been sick 6 years ago. Ive had stool tests done in the past, no parasites. Only an overgrowth of klebsiella pneumoniae. Ive since cut all starch from my diet as i was having symptoms of AS. That went away but the brain fog after eating didn’t.

Raw makes it a little less foggy, but its still there. Seafood, im a little less foggy but im still foggy. I have to drink copious amounts of coffee in order to remedy the issue.

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

I do keep my phone on airplane mode 95% of the day. Only watch TV with blue blockers on and I usually only do maybe 1 hour tops, sometimes in the evening. I rise with the sun, it actually hits my face in the morning to wake me up with my window. I spend lots of time outdoors with my pups. We go in the woods, to the springs, everywhere that northern florida has to offer. I check for ticks regularly so thats not an issue. I walk barefoot a lot as well.

Im stumped and getting tired of the feeling. When im done digesting food Im okay and clear headed.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-18T06:30:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-cure.22521/post-255876>

When your respiratory proteins are spread too wide you get this effect with food. Respiratory proteins in the mitochondria must have a specific distance between them and when they are healthy they are optimized. When they are not, you call this condition a raised heteroplasmy. The reason is simple.......every increase in distance by one Angstrom decreases energy and information transfer by a factor of TEN.

Could you provide me a link or explain further into respiratory proteins? I want to learn as much as possible, but im just not sure where to start. I like to be as efficient as possible and dont want to necessarily start down the wrong rabbit hole. Thank you Dr. Jack Kruse! Have a beautiful sunday!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-fog-cure.22521/post-255928>

Before i eat i can do a lot of physical exercise. I got up with the sun, went to the local park, did a good pullup workout. Then went to the UF football stadium and ran the entire stadium sprinting up the stairs. Then i bear crawled up the stairs until I eventually couldnt go any further.

### Safeby2 — 2018-11-19T03:05:12+0200
